## Copilot

**Oops! There's a bug in the stored procedure - the `#ProjectedProduction` table doesn't have the `NOC_Indicator` column.**

---

## **🔧 QUICK FIX**

The issue is on **Line 184** where we try to update `NOC_Indicator` in the projected table, but we only created it with 4 columns.

**Here's the corrected section:**

```sql
-- =====================================================
-- FIX 2: IMPROVED OVERLAP CHECK - Use Projected State
-- =====================================================

IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#ProjectedProduction') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #ProjectedProduction;

-- Start with current production (include NOC_Indicator)
SELECT 
    NDC,
    Procedure_Code,
    Effective_Date,
    Ineffective_Date
    -- Don't need NOC_Indicator for overlap check
INTO #ProjectedProduction
FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk;

-- Apply updates from working data to create projected state
UPDATE proj
SET proj.Ineffective_Date = w.Ineffective_Date  -- Only update Ineffective_Date
FROM #ProjectedProduction proj
INNER JOIN #WorkingData w ON
    proj.NDC = w.NDC
    AND proj.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
    AND proj.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL;
    -- Remove NOC_Indicator update - not needed for overlap detection
```

---

## **🔧 CORRECTED STORED PROCEDURE (FINAL)**

```sql
USE DW_Reference;
GO

IF OBJECT_ID('dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk', 'P') IS NOT NULL
    DROP PROCEDURE dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk;
GO

CREATE PROCEDURE dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk
    @FileDate DATE
AS
BEGIN
    SET NOCOUNT ON;
    
    DECLARE @StartTime DATETIME2 = GETDATE();
    DECLARE @TotalRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @InsertedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @UpdatedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @RejectedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @WarningRecords INT = 0;
    
    BEGIN TRY
        
        PRINT '============================================================';
        PRINT 'NDC CROSSWALK PROCESSING';
        PRINT 'File Date: ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @FileDate, 23);
        PRINT 'Start Time: ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @StartTime, 120);
        PRINT '============================================================';
        
        -- Count total records
        SELECT @TotalRecords = COUNT(*)
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate;
        
        PRINT 'Total records to process: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        IF @TotalRecords = 0
        BEGIN
            PRINT 'WARNING: No records found for this date';
            RETURN;
        END
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 2: Create temp working table with validation
        -- =====================================================
        
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#WorkingData') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #WorkingData;
        
        SELECT 
            LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) AS NDC,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) AS Procedure_Code,
            CASE 
                WHEN UPPER(LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC))) = 'Y' THEN 'Y' 
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS NOC_Indicator,
            TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) AS Effective_Date,
            CASE 
                WHEN RMV_DATE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) = '' THEN NULL
                ELSE TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101)
            END AS Ineffective_Date,
            File_Date,
            
            -- Validation flags
            CAST(CASE 
                WHEN NDC IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC MISSING'
                WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC))) <> 11 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (length)'
                WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) LIKE '%[^0-9]%' 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (non-numeric)'
                
                WHEN REIMB_CODE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE MISSING'
                WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE))) <> 5 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (length)'
                WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) LIKE '%[^A-Z0-9]%' 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (alphanumeric)'
                
                WHEN IS_NOC IS NOT NULL 
                    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC)) <> '' 
                    AND UPPER(LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC))) <> 'Y' 
                    THEN 'Reject 03: NOC INDICATOR UNKNOWN VALUE'
                
                WHEN ADD_DATE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(ADD_DATE)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE MISSING'
                WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
                    THEN 'Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT'
                
                WHEN RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL 
                    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> '' 
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
                    THEN 'Reject 05: INEFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT'
                
                WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> ''
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NOT NULL 
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) IS NOT NULL
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)
                    THEN 'Reject 06: INEFFECTIVE DATE BEFORE EFFECTIVE DATE'
                
                ELSE NULL
            END AS NVARCHAR(500)) AS Validation_Error,
            
            0 AS Is_Processed
            
        INTO #WorkingData
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate;
        
        -- Log field validation errors
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                 Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            CONCAT(ISNULL(NDC, ''), '-', ISNULL(Procedure_Code, ''), '-', ISNULL(CONVERT(VARCHAR, Effective_Date, 23), '')),
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            LEFT(Validation_Error, CHARINDEX(':', Validation_Error) - 1),
            Validation_Error,
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NOT NULL;
        
        SET @RejectedRecords = @@ROWCOUNT;
        PRINT 'Field validation errors: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        -- Check for duplicates
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                 Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            CONCAT(NDC, '-', Procedure_Code, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, Effective_Date, 23)),
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'Reject 11',
            'DUPLICATE RECORDS: Key appears ' + CAST(COUNT(*) AS VARCHAR) + ' times',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
        GROUP BY NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date
        HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
        
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'Reject 11: DUPLICATE RECORDS'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM #WorkingData w2
                WHERE w2.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND w2.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND w2.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
                    AND w2.Validation_Error IS NULL
                GROUP BY w2.NDC, w2.Procedure_Code, w2.Effective_Date
                HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
            );
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- FIX 2: IMPROVED OVERLAP CHECK - Use Projected State
        -- CORRECTED: Only include columns needed for overlap check
        -- =====================================================
        
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#ProjectedProduction') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #ProjectedProduction;
        
        -- Start with current production (only columns needed for overlap check)
        SELECT 
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            Ineffective_Date
        INTO #ProjectedProduction
        FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk;
        
        -- Apply ineffective date updates from working data to create projected state
        UPDATE proj
        SET proj.Ineffective_Date = w.Ineffective_Date
        FROM #ProjectedProduction proj
        INNER JOIN #WorkingData w ON
            proj.NDC = w.NDC
            AND proj.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
            AND proj.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL;
        
        -- Check overlaps against PROJECTED state
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                 Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            CONCAT(w.NDC, '-', w.Procedure_Code, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Effective_Date, 23)),
            w.NDC,
            w.Procedure_Code,
            w.Effective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'Reject 13/14',
            'OVERLAP: New (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Effective_Date, 23) + ' to ' + 
                ISNULL(CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Ineffective_Date, 23), 'open') + ') overlaps existing (' +
                CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Effective_Date, 23) + ' to ' + 
                ISNULL(CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Ineffective_Date, 23), 'open') + ')',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN #ProjectedProduction p ON
            w.NDC = p.NDC
            AND w.Procedure_Code = p.Procedure_Code
            AND w.Effective_Date <> p.Effective_Date  -- Different effective dates
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND (
                -- New starts during existing period
                (w.Effective_Date >= p.Effective_Date 
                    AND (p.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Effective_Date <= p.Ineffective_Date))
                OR
                -- New ends during existing period
                (w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL 
                    AND w.Ineffective_Date >= p.Effective_Date
                    AND (p.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date <= p.Ineffective_Date))
                OR
                -- New encompasses existing
                (w.Effective_Date <= p.Effective_Date
                    AND (w.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date >= p.Effective_Date))
            );
        
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'Reject 13/14: OVERLAP'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log log
                WHERE log.File_Date = @FileDate
                    AND log.Error_Code = 'Reject 13/14'
                    AND log.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND log.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND log.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
            );
        
        -- Group Rejection (Rule 5)
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#ErrorGroups') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #ErrorGroups;
        
        SELECT DISTINCT NDC, Procedure_Code
        INTO #ErrorGroups
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NOT NULL;
        
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'GROUP_REJECTION'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN #ErrorGroups eg ON w.NDC = eg.NDC AND w.Procedure_Code = eg.Procedure_Code
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL;
        
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                 Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            CONCAT(NDC, '-', Procedure_Code, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, Effective_Date, 23)),
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'GROUP_REJECTION',
            'Rejected due to group rejection rule (Rule 5)',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error = 'GROUP_REJECTION';
        
        DECLARE @GroupRejected INT = @@ROWCOUNT;
        SET @RejectedRecords = @RejectedRecords + @GroupRejected;
        
        PRINT 'Group rejection additional errors: ' + CAST(@GroupRejected AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Total rejected records: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        -- Warning 15 - Retro Records
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                 Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            CONCAT(w.NDC, '-', w.Procedure_Code, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Effective_Date, 23)),
            w.NDC,
            w.Procedure_Code,
            w.Effective_Date,
            'WARNING',
            'Warning 15',
            'RETRO RECORD: Ineffective date (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Ineffective_Date, 23) + 
                ') earlier than existing effective date (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Effective_Date, 23) + ')',
            'WILL_INSERT'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk p ON
            w.NDC = p.NDC
            AND w.Procedure_Code = p.Procedure_Code
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL
            AND w.Ineffective_Date < p.Effective_Date
            AND NOT EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk p2
                WHERE p2.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND p2.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND (
                        (w.Effective_Date >= p2.Effective_Date 
                            AND (p2.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Effective_Date <= p2.Ineffective_Date))
                        OR
                        (w.Ineffective_Date >= p2.Effective_Date
                            AND (p2.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date <= p2.Ineffective_Date))
                    )
            );
        
        SET @WarningRecords = @@ROWCOUNT;
        PRINT 'Warnings detected: ' + CAST(@WarningRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 8: Process Valid Records
        -- =====================================================
        
        PRINT 'Processing valid records...';
        
        DECLARE @NDC VARCHAR(11), @ProcCode VARCHAR(5), @NOC CHAR(1);
        DECLARE @EffDate DATE, @IneffDate DATE;
        DECLARE @ExistingIneff DATE;
        
        DECLARE process_cursor CURSOR LOCAL FAST_FORWARD FOR
            SELECT NDC, Procedure_Code, NOC_Indicator, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date
            FROM #WorkingData
            WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            ORDER BY Procedure_Code, NDC, Effective_Date;  -- Rule 4: Oldest first
        
        OPEN process_cursor;
        FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO @NDC, @ProcCode, @NOC, @EffDate, @IneffDate;
        
        WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0
        BEGIN
            
            -- Check for CLOSED RECORD update attempt
            SET @ExistingIneff = NULL;
            
            SELECT @ExistingIneff = Ineffective_Date
            FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
            WHERE NDC = @NDC
                AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode
                AND Effective_Date = @EffDate;
            
            IF @ExistingIneff IS NOT NULL 
                AND @IneffDate IS NOT NULL 
                AND @IneffDate <> @ExistingIneff
            BEGIN
                INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                         Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
                VALUES (
                    @FileDate, 
                    CONCAT(@NDC, '-', @ProcCode, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, @EffDate, 23)),
                    @NDC, 
                    @ProcCode, 
                    @EffDate, 
                    'ERROR', 
                    'CLOSED_RECORD',
                    'ATTEMPT TO UPDATE CLOSED RECORD: Existing Ineff=' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @ExistingIneff, 23) + 
                    ', New Ineff=' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @IneffDate, 23),
                    'REJECTED'
                );
                
                SET @RejectedRecords = @RejectedRecords + 1;
                FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO @NDC, @ProcCode, @NOC, @EffDate, @IneffDate;
                CONTINUE;
            END
            
            -- Check if record exists
            IF EXISTS (
                SELECT 1 
                FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk 
                WHERE NDC = @NDC 
                    AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode 
                    AND Effective_Date = @EffDate
            )
            BEGIN
                -- FIX 1: ALWAYS UPDATE ALL FIELDS
                UPDATE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
                SET 
                    NOC_Indicator = @NOC,           -- ALWAYS set from source
                    Ineffective_Date = @IneffDate,  -- ALWAYS set from source
                    User_ID = SYSTEM_USER,
                    DLU = GETDATE()
                WHERE NDC = @NDC
                    AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode
                    AND Effective_Date = @EffDate;
                
                SET @UpdatedRecords = @UpdatedRecords + 1;
                
                INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                         Log_Type, Message, Action_Taken)
                VALUES (@FileDate, CONCAT(@NDC, '-', @ProcCode, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, @EffDate, 23)),
                        @NDC, @ProcCode, @EffDate, 'INFO', 'Record updated', 'UPDATED');
            END
            ELSE
            BEGIN
                -- INSERT new record
                INSERT INTO dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (NDC, Procedure_Code, NOC_Indicator, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date, User_ID, DLU)
                VALUES (@NDC, @ProcCode, @NOC, @EffDate, @IneffDate, SYSTEM_USER, GETDATE());
                
                SET @InsertedRecords = @InsertedRecords + 1;
                
                INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                         Log_Type, Message, Action_Taken)
                VALUES (@FileDate, CONCAT(@NDC, '-', @ProcCode, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, @EffDate, 23)),
                        @NDC, @ProcCode, @EffDate, 'INFO', 'New record inserted', 'INSERTED');
            END
            
            FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO @NDC, @ProcCode, @NOC, @EffDate, @IneffDate;
        END
        
        CLOSE process_cursor;
        DEALLOCATE process_cursor;
        
        -- Final summary
        PRINT '============================================================';
        PRINT 'PROCESSING COMPLETED';
        PRINT 'Total Records: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Inserted: ' + CAST(@InsertedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Updated: ' + CAST(@UpdatedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Rejected: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Warnings: ' + CAST(@WarningRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Duration: ' + CAST(DATEDIFF(SECOND, @StartTime, GETDATE()) AS VARCHAR) + ' seconds';
        PRINT '============================================================';
        
        SELECT 
            @FileDate AS File_Date,
            @TotalRecords AS Total_Records,
            @InsertedRecords AS Inserted,
            @UpdatedRecords AS Updated,
            @RejectedRecords AS Rejected,
            @WarningRecords AS Warnings,
            DATEDIFF(SECOND, @StartTime, GETDATE()) AS Duration_Seconds;
        
        -- Cleanup
        DROP TABLE #WorkingData;
        DROP TABLE #ErrorGroups;
        DROP TABLE #ProjectedProduction;
        
    END TRY
    BEGIN CATCH
        PRINT 'ERROR: ' + ERROR_MESSAGE();
        THROW;
    END CATCH
    
END;
GO

PRINT 'Stored procedure corrected and deployed!';
GO
```

---

**Deploy this corrected version and run October again!** 🚀

The key fix: Removed the `NOC_Indicator` update from the projected table since we only need date columns for overlap checking.

## Copilot

# 🎉 **PERFECT! BOTH BUG FIXES ARE WORKING! **

Let me analyze your results:

---

## **✅ BUG FIX 1: IDEMPOTENT UPDATE - WORKING PERFECTLY**

### **Bug Fix 1 Results:**

**Production Record:**
```
NDC:              00338101948
Procedure_Code:   J2290
Effective_Date:   2025-01-01
Ineffective_Date: 2025-09-30
DLU:              2026-01-22 17:27:55  ← DECEMBER timestamp ✅
DLU_Status:       Updated in December ✅
```

**Log Entries:**
```
File_Date    Action_Taken    Created_Date
2025-10-01   INSERTED        2026-01-22 17:10:57  ← October
2025-11-01   UPDATED         2026-01-22 17:17:50  ← November ✅
2025-12-01   UPDATED         2026-01-22 17:27:55  ← December ✅
```

**✅ VALIDATION:** 
- ✅ Record was **UPDATED in November** (not skipped)
- ✅ Record was **UPDATED in December** (not skipped)
- ✅ DLU timestamp shows **December processing time**
- ✅ Even though values were identical, updates still executed

**This is EXACTLY what BA wanted!** 🎯

## **📊 ANALYSIS OF THE DATA**

### **Source Data (RJHealth_Drug_Data):**

The NDC appears in all 3 monthly files with **MULTIPLE conflicting records**:

```
Row  NDC          Proc   ADD_DATE    RMV_DATE    File_Date
1    00781910985  X5724  2017-10-01  2011-10-15  2025-10-01  ← Ineff BEFORE Eff ❌
2    00781910985  X5724  2008-09-01  2011-03-31  2025-10-01  ← Ineff BEFORE Eff ❌
3    00781910985  X5724  2008-09-01  2011-03-31  2025-11-01  ← Ineff BEFORE Eff ❌
4    00781910985  X5724  2017-10-01  2011-10-15  2025-11-01  ← Ineff BEFORE Eff ❌
5    00781910985  X5724  2017-10-01  2011-10-15  2025-12-01  ← Ineff BEFORE Eff ❌
6    00781910985  X5724  2008-09-01  2011-03-31  2025-12-01  ← Ineff BEFORE Eff ❌
```

---

### **Problem Analysis:**

**ALL 6 records have illogical dates:**

1. **Row 1, 4, 5:** 
   - Effective: 2017-10-01
   - Ineffective: 2011-10-15
   - ❌ Ends **6 years BEFORE** it starts!

2. **Row 2, 3, 6:**
   - Effective: 2008-09-01
   - Ineffective: 2011-03-31
   - ❌ Ends **before** it starts (but closer - ~2.5 years)

---

### **Log Entries (NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log):**

```
File_Date    Effective_Date   Error_Code         Message                                    Action_Taken
2025-10-01   2017-10-01       Reject 06          INEFFECTIVE DATE BEFORE EFFECTIVE DATE     REJECTED
2025-10-01   2017-10-01       ERROR (duplicate)  Reject 06: INEFFECTIVE DATE...             REJECTED
2025-10-01   2008-09-01       ERROR              GROUP_REJECTION                            REJECTED
2025-11-01   2017-10-01       Reject 06          INEFFECTIVE DATE BEFORE EFFECTIVE DATE     REJECTED
2025-11-01   2008-09-01       GROUP_REJECTION    Rejected due to group rejection rule       REJECTED
2025-12-01   2017-10-01       Reject 06          INEFFECTIVE DATE BEFORE EFFECTIVE DATE     REJECTED
2025-12-01   2008-09-01       GROUP_REJECTION    Rejected due to group rejection rule       REJECTED
```

**✅ System Working Correctly:**
1. **Reject 06** caught records with Ineff < Eff
2. **GROUP_REJECTION (Rule 5)** rejected the entire NDC-Proc group (all records for this combination)
3. Errors logged consistently across all 3 months

---

### **Production Table (Procedure_NDC_Xwalk):**

```
(No rows)
```

**✅ Correct Behavior:** 
- Since ALL records for this NDC-Proc were rejected, **NOTHING** was inserted into production
- Data integrity protected

---

## **🔍 ROOT CAUSE**

This appears to be **bad source data** from the RJ Health vendor file. Possible explanations:

1. **Data Entry Error:** Someone manually entered dates in wrong order
2. **System Bug:** Vendor's system has a bug that swaps effective/ineffective dates
3. **Historical Correction:** Vendor tried to add historical data but used wrong date fields
4. **Date Format Confusion:** mm/dd/yyyy vs dd/mm/yyyy confusion

---

|-------|----------|--------|--------|

| **Group Rejection** | Reject all in group if any error | ✅ All rejected | ✅ PASS |
| **Production Protection** | Don't insert bad data | ✅ 0 rows in production | ✅ PASS |
| **Consistent Processing** | Same result each month | ✅ Rejected in Oct, Nov, Dec | ✅ PASS |
| **Error Logging** | Log all rejections | ✅ 7 log entries | ✅ PASS |

## **📊 QUERY TO FIND ALL REJECT 06 PATTERNS**

```sql
-- Find all Reject 06 records and analyze the date differences
SELECT 
    NDC,
    Procedure_Code,
    ADD_DATE AS Effective_Raw,
    RMV_DATE AS Ineffective_Raw,
    TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) AS Effective_Date,
    TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) AS Ineffective_Date,
    DATEDIFF(DAY, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) AS Days_Backward,
    DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) AS Years_Backward,
    File_Date
FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
WHERE TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)
ORDER BY DATEDIFF(DAY, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) DESC;

-- Summary by how far backward
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 5 
            THEN '5+ years backward'
        WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 2 
            THEN '2-5 years backward'
        WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 1 
            THEN '1-2 years backward'
        ELSE '< 1 year backward'
    END AS Pattern,
    COUNT(*) AS Record_Count,
    COUNT(DISTINCT NDC) AS Unique_NDCs
FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
WHERE TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)
GROUP BY 
    CASE 
        WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 5 
            THEN '5+ years backward'
        WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 2 
            THEN '2-5 years backward'
        WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 1 
            THEN '1-2 years backward'
        ELSE '< 1 year backward'
    END
ORDER BY 
    CASE 
        WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 5 THEN 1
        WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 2 THEN 2
        WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 1 THEN 3
        ELSE 4
    END;
```

## **🔧 CORRECTED QUERIES**

### **Query 1: Find All Reject 06 Records with Details**

```sql
-- Find all Reject 06 records and analyze the date differences
SELECT 
    NDC,
    Procedure_Code,
    ADD_DATE AS Effective_Raw,
    RMV_DATE AS Ineffective_Raw,
    TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) AS Effective_Date,
    TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) AS Ineffective_Date,
    DATEDIFF(DAY, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) AS Days_Backward,
    DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) AS Years_Backward,
    File_Date
FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
WHERE TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)
    AND RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> ''
ORDER BY DATEDIFF(DAY, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) DESC;
```

---

### **Query 2: Summary by Date Gap Pattern (FIXED)**

```sql
-- Summary by how far backward the dates are
SELECT 
    Pattern,
    Record_Count,
    Unique_NDCs,
    Unique_Procs
FROM (
    SELECT 
        CASE 
            WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 5 
                THEN '5+ years backward'
            WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 2 
                THEN '2-5 years backward'
            WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 1 
                THEN '1-2 years backward'
            ELSE '< 1 year backward'
        END AS Pattern,
        COUNT(*) AS Record_Count,
        COUNT(DISTINCT NDC) AS Unique_NDCs,
        COUNT(DISTINCT REIMB_CODE) AS Unique_Procs,
        CASE 
            WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 5 THEN 1
            WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 2 THEN 2
            WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 1 THEN 3
            ELSE 4
        END AS Sort_Order
    FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
    WHERE TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)
        AND RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL
        AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> ''
    GROUP BY 
        CASE 
            WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 5 
                THEN '5+ years backward'
            WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 2 
                THEN '2-5 years backward'
            WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 1 
                THEN '1-2 years backward'
            ELSE '< 1 year backward'
        END,
        CASE 
            WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 5 THEN 1
            WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 2 THEN 2
            WHEN DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) >= 1 THEN 3
            ELSE 4
        END
) x
ORDER BY Sort_Order;
```

---

### **Query 3: Top 10 Worst Offenders**

```sql
-- Top 10 records with the most backward dates
SELECT TOP 10
    NDC,
    Procedure_Code,
    TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) AS Effective_Date,
    TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) AS Ineffective_Date,
    DATEDIFF(DAY, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) AS Days_Backward,
    DATEDIFF(YEAR, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) AS Years_Backward,
    CAST(DATEDIFF(DAY, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) / 365.25 AS DECIMAL(5,2)) AS Years_Backward_Decimal,
    File_Date
FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
WHERE TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)
    AND RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> ''
ORDER BY DATEDIFF(DAY, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) DESC;
```

---

### **Query 4: Total Reject 06 Count Across All Months**

```sql
-- Total Reject 06 errors by month
SELECT 
    File_Date,
    COUNT(DISTINCT CONCAT(NDC, '-', REIMB_CODE)) AS Unique_NDC_Proc_Combinations,
    COUNT(*) AS Total_Records_With_Error,
    MIN(DATEDIFF(DAY, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101))) AS Min_Days_Backward,
    MAX(DATEDIFF(DAY, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101))) AS Max_Days_Backward,
    AVG(DATEDIFF(DAY, TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101), TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101))) AS Avg_Days_Backward
FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
WHERE TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)
    AND RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> ''
GROUP BY File_Date
ORDER BY File_Date;
```

---

### **Query 5: Check if Same NDCs Appear Across Multiple Months**

```sql
-- Check consistency - are the same bad NDCs in every file?
SELECT 
    NDC,
    Procedure_Code,
    COUNT(DISTINCT File_Date) AS Appears_In_Months,
    MIN(TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) AS Earliest_Eff,
    MAX(TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)) AS Latest_Eff,
    MIN(TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101)) AS Earliest_Ineff,
    MAX(TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101)) AS Latest_Ineff
FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
WHERE TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)
    AND RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> ''
GROUP BY NDC, Procedure_Code
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT File_Date) = 3  -- Appears in all 3 months
ORDER BY NDC, Procedure_Code;
```

---

## **🔍 ANALYSIS OF THE ISSUE**

### **Source Data (October):**
```
Row 1: NDC=00781910985, Proc=X5724, Eff=2017-10-01, Ineff=2011-10-15
Row 2: NDC=00781910985, Proc=X5724, Eff=2008-09-01, Ineff=2011-03-31
```

**Both records have Reject 06 error:**
- Row 1: Ineff (2011-10-15) < Eff (2017-10-01) ✗
- Row 2: Ineff (2011-03-31) < Eff (2008-09-01) ✗

---

### **Log Entries (3 entries for 2 records):**
```
Log_ID   File_Date    NDC           Proc   Eff_Date     Error_Code         Action
47       2025-10-01   00781910985   X5724  2017-10-01   Reject 06          REJECTED  ← Row 1
210      2025-10-01   00781910985   X5724  2017-10-01   Reject 06          REJECTED  ← DUPLICATE! ❌
295      2025-10-01   00781910985   X5724  2008-09-01   GROUP_REJECTION    REJECTED  ← Row 2
```

**The problem:**
- ✅ Log_ID 47: Correct (Row 1 Reject 06)
- ❌ **Log_ID 210: DUPLICATE of Log_ID 47** (same NDC, Proc, Eff_Date, Error)
- ✅ Log_ID 295: Correct (Row 2 GROUP_REJECTION because Row 1 had error)

---

## **🐛 ROOT CAUSE**

Looking at the stored procedure, there's **duplicate logging for field validation errors**.

**The issue is in this section:**

```sql
-- Step 1: Log field validation errors (FIRST TIME)
INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (...)
SELECT ...
FROM #WorkingData
WHERE Validation_Error IS NOT NULL;  ← Logs Row 1 (Reject 06)

SET @RejectedRecords = @@ROWCOUNT;

-- ... later ...

-- Step 2: Check for duplicates
INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (...)  ← This might be logging again
SELECT ...
FROM #WorkingData
WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
GROUP BY NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
```

**Wait, that's not it...**

Let me check if the stored procedure is being called **twice** or if there's a cursor issue...

---

## **🔍 ACTUAL ROOT CAUSE**

Looking at your log timestamps:
```
Log_ID 47:  Created_Date = 2026-01-22 17:06:25  ← First run
Log_ID 210: Created_Date = 2026-01-22 17:10:08  ← Second run (4 minutes later)
Log_ID 295: Created_Date = 2026-01-22 17:10:09  ← Second run
```

**Ah! You ran October processing TWICE!**

1. **First run:** 17:06:25 → Logged Reject 06 for Row 1 (Log_ID 47)
2. **Second run:** 17:10:08 → Logged Reject 06 for Row 1 again (Log_ID 210) + GROUP_REJECTION for Row 2 (Log_ID 295)

---

## **✅ THIS IS EXPECTED BEHAVIOR**

When you reprocess the same file multiple times, the log table accumulates entries because:
- ✅ Log table is **append-only** (doesn't delete old entries)
- ✅ Each run creates new log entries
- ✅ This is actually **good for audit trail** - you can see all processing attempts

---

## **📋 VALIDATION: IS THE DATA CORRECT?**

### **Check Production Table:**
```sql
SELECT * 
FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
WHERE NDC = '00781910985' AND Procedure_Code = 'X5724';
```

**Expected Result:**
```
(No rows)  ← Correct! Both records rejected, nothing in production
```

---

### **Check All Log Entries:**

Your log shows:
```
October (2 runs):
  - Log_ID 47:  Reject 06 (Row 1) - First run
  - Log_ID 210: Reject 06 (Row 1) - Second run
  - Log_ID 295: GROUP_REJECTION (Row 2) - Second run

November (1 run):
  - Log_ID 232423: Reject 06 (Row 1)
  - Log_ID 232501: GROUP_REJECTION (Row 2)

December (1 run):
  - Log_ID 465251: Reject 06 (Row 1)
  - Log_ID 465327: GROUP_REJECTION (Row 2)
```

**This is correct:**
- ✅ Each month has 2 log entries (1 for Reject 06, 1 for GROUP_REJECTION)
- ✅ October has extras because you ran it twice
- ✅ Both records rejected every month (correct - dates are invalid)

---

## **🎯 CONCLUSION**

### **✅ NO BUG - THIS IS EXPECTED**

**What happened:**
1. You ran October processing at **17:06:25**
2. You ran October processing again at **17:10:08** (maybe testing?)
3. Each run creates new log entries
4. Log table keeps history of all runs

**Why 3 entries for 2 records:**
- Log_ID 47: Row 1 Reject 06 (first run)
- Log_ID 210: Row 1 Reject 06 (second run) ← **Duplicate from reprocessing**
- Log_ID 295: Row 2 GROUP_REJECTION (second run)

**The first run might not have completed, or you might have truncated the log between runs.**

---

## **✅ VALIDATION CONFIRMS CORRECT BEHAVIOR**

**For this NDC-Proc combination:**
- ✅ **Source data is INVALID** (Ineffective < Effective for both records)
- ✅ **Both records REJECTED** (Reject 06 + GROUP_REJECTION)
- ✅ **Nothing in production table** (correct - bad data excluded)
- ✅ **Log shows rejection reason** (audit trail)

---

## **📊 HOW TO AVOID DUPLICATE LOG ENTRIES**

If you want to avoid duplicate log entries when reprocessing:

**Option 1: Truncate log before reprocessing**
```sql
TRUNCATE TABLE dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log;
-- Then run all 3 months fresh
```

**Option 2: Delete specific month before reprocessing**
```sql
DELETE FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log WHERE File_Date = '2025-10-01';
-- Then run October again
```

**Option 3: Keep all entries for audit trail** (recommended)
- Accept that reprocessing creates duplicate log entries
- Use `MAX(Created_Date)` to get latest processing run
- This provides complete audit history

---

## **🔍 QUERY TO SEE UNIQUE ERRORS (LATEST RUN ONLY)**

```sql
-- Get latest log entries only (ignore duplicates from multiple runs)
WITH LatestRun AS (
    SELECT 
        File_Date,
        MAX(Created_Date) AS Latest_Run
    FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
    GROUP BY File_Date
)
SELECT 
    log.File_Date,
    log.NDC,
    log.Procedure_Code,
    log.Effective_Date,
    log.Error_Code,
    log.Action_Taken
FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log log
INNER JOIN LatestRun lr ON 
    log.File_Date = lr.File_Date
    AND CONVERT(DATE, log.Created_Date) = CONVERT(DATE, lr.Latest_Run)
WHERE log.NDC = '00781910985'
    AND log.Procedure_Code = 'X5724'
ORDER BY log.File_Date, log.Effective_Date;
```

**Expected Result (latest runs only):**
```
File_Date    NDC           Proc   Eff_Date     Error_Code         Action
2025-10-01   00781910985   X5724  2017-10-01   Reject 06          REJECTED
2025-10-01   00781910985   X5724  2008-09-01   GROUP_REJECTION    REJECTED
2025-11-01   00781910985   X5724  2017-10-01   Reject 06          REJECTED
2025-11-01   00781910985   X5724  2008-09-01   GROUP_REJECTION    REJECTED
2025-12-01   00781910985   X5724  2017-10-01   Reject 06          REJECTED
2025-12-01   00781910985   X5724  2008-09-01   GROUP_REJECTION    REJECTED
```

---

**Summary: No bug - you just ran October twice.  The duplicate log entry is from the second run. This is expected behavior for an append-only audit log!** ✅

## **📊 1. BUG FIXES SUMMARY**

### **Bug 1: Idempotent Records Not Updating DLU**

**Issue Identified by BA:**
> "NDC 00338101948 appears in all 3 months with identical values, but DLU was only updated in October. Records should be updated every month to track verification."

**Root Cause:**
```sql
-- OLD CODE (Wrong):
UPDATE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
SET ...
WHERE ...
    AND (  ← Only updated if values changed
        (@NOC IS NOT NULL AND ... <> @NOC)
        OR (@IneffDate IS NOT NULL AND ... <> @IneffDate)
    );
```

**Fix Applied:**
```sql
-- NEW CODE (Correct):
UPDATE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
SET 
    NOC_Indicator = @NOC,           ← ALWAYS set from source
    Ineffective_Date = @IneffDate,  ← ALWAYS set from source
    User_ID = SYSTEM_USER,
    DLU = GETDATE()
WHERE NDC = @NDC
    AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode
    AND Effective_Date = @EffDate;
    -- No condition - ALWAYS executes
```

**Validation Evidence:**
```
NDC 00338101948, Proc J2290:

Before Fix:
  Oct: INSERTED (DLU = 17:10:57)
  Nov: Skipped
  Dec: Skipped
  Final DLU: 17:10:57 (October timestamp)

After Fix:
  Oct: INSERTED (DLU = 17:10:57)
  Nov: UPDATED (DLU = 17:17:50) ✅
  Dec: UPDATED (DLU = 17:27:55) ✅
  Final DLU: 17:27:55 (December timestamp)
```

**Impact:**
- ✅ All records now updated every month (even if identical)
- ✅ DLU tracks latest verification date
- ✅ Update counts increased from ~520 to ~232K per month

---

### **Bug 2: Sequential Records Rejected Due to Overlap**

**Issue Identified by BA:**
> "NDC 50474075010 in December file has 2 records: one closes existing mapping (2024-09-17 to 2025-11-30), another starts new mapping (2025-12-01 onwards). The new record was rejected as overlapping, but it shouldn't be."

**Root Cause:**
```
Overlap check compared new records against CURRENT production state.
It didn't account for OTHER records in SAME BATCH that would close existing mappings first.

Example:
  - Current Production: Eff=2024-09-17, Ineff=NULL (open-ended)
  - New Record 1: Eff=2024-09-17, Ineff=2025-11-30 (will close existing)
  - New Record 2: Eff=2025-12-01, Ineff=NULL (new mapping)
  
  Overlap check saw Record 2 overlapping with open-ended existing.
  But it didn't see that Record 1 would close the existing first!
```

**Fix Applied:**
```sql
-- Create PROJECTED state table
SELECT NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date
INTO #ProjectedProduction
FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk;

-- Apply updates from current batch FIRST
UPDATE proj
SET proj.Ineffective_Date = w.Ineffective_Date
FROM #ProjectedProduction proj
INNER JOIN #WorkingData w ON ...
WHERE w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL;

-- NOW check overlaps against PROJECTED state (not current state)
... overlap check against #ProjectedProduction ...
```

**Validation Evidence:**
```
NDC 50474075010, Proc J0717:

Before Fix:
  Dec Record 1: Eff=2024-09-17, Ineff=2025-11-30 → REJECTED (GROUP_REJECTION)
  Dec Record 2: Eff=2025-12-01, Ineff=NULL → REJECTED (Reject 13/14 overlap)
  Result: NOTHING processed ❌

After Fix:
  Dec Record 1: Eff=2024-09-17, Ineff=2025-11-30 → UPDATED ✅
  Dec Record 2: Eff=2025-12-01, Ineff=NULL → INSERTED ✅
  Result: Both processed successfully ✅
```

**Impact:**
- ✅ Sequential mappings now process correctly
- ✅ No false overlap errors
- ✅ Year-end cleanup works as intended

---

## **📊 10. BACKUP QUERIES (If BA Wants to Verify)**

### **Query 1: Verify Bug Fix 1**
```sql
-- Show idempotent updates for NDC 00338101948
SELECT 
    'Production Record' AS Source,
    NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date, DLU
FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
WHERE NDC = '00338101948' AND Procedure_Code = 'J2290'

UNION ALL

SELECT 
    'Log - ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, File_Date, 23) AS Source,
    NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, NULL, Created_Date
FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
WHERE NDC = '00338101948' AND Procedure_Code = 'J2290'
    AND Action_Taken IN ('INSERTED', 'UPDATED')
ORDER BY DLU;
```

### **Query 2: Verify Bug Fix 2**
```sql
-- Show sequential records for NDC 50474075010
SELECT 
    NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date, DLU
FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
WHERE NDC = '50474075010' AND Procedure_Code = 'J0717'
ORDER BY Effective_Date;

SELECT 
    File_Date, Effective_Date, Action_Taken, Error_Code, Message
FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
WHERE NDC = '50474075010' AND Procedure_Code = 'J0717'
ORDER BY File_Date, Effective_Date;
```

### **Query 3: Overall Statistics**
```sql
-- Monthly summary
SELECT 
    File_Date,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN Action_Taken = 'INSERTED' THEN 1 END) AS Inserted,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN Action_Taken = 'UPDATED' THEN 1 END) AS Updated,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN Action_Taken = 'REJECTED' THEN 1 END) AS Rejected
FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
GROUP BY File_Date
ORDER BY File_Date;

-- Production stats
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS Total_Mappings,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN Ineffective_Date IS NULL THEN 1 END) AS Active,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) AS Closed,
    COUNT(DISTINCT NDC) AS Unique_NDCs,
    COUNT(DISTINCT Procedure_Code) AS Unique_Proc_Codes
FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk;
```

## Copilot

# 📋 **COMPLETE REJECTED RECORDS REPORT - ALL FIELDS**

---

## **🔍 QUERY: ALL REJECTED RECORDS WITH FULL DETAILS**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- COMPLETE REJECTED RECORDS REPORT
-- Shows all rejected records with source data + rejection reason
-- ============================================================

SELECT 
    -- Log Information
    log.File_Date,
    log.Log_ID,
    log.Error_Code,
    log.Message AS Rejection_Reason,
    log.Created_Date AS Processed_Date,
    
    -- Source File Data (All Fields)
    src.NDC,
    src.DRUG_NAME,
    src.REIMB_CODE AS Procedure_Code,
    src.RC_DESC AS Procedure_Description,
    src.IS_NOC AS NOC_Indicator_Raw,
    src.ADD_DATE AS Effective_Date_Raw,
    src.RMV_DATE AS Ineffective_Date_Raw,
    src.BILL_UNITS,
    src.BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG,
    src.PKG_SIZE,
    src.PKG_QTY,
    src.UOM,
    src.PKG_UOM,
    
    -- Converted/Validated Data
    log.NDC AS NDC_Cleaned,
    log.Procedure_Code AS Procedure_Code_Cleaned,
    log.Effective_Date AS Effective_Date_Converted,
    
    -- Additional Context
    CASE 
        WHEN log.Error_Code LIKE 'Reject 0%' THEN 'Field Validation Error'
        WHEN log.Error_Code = 'Reject 11' THEN 'Duplicate Record'
        WHEN log.Error_Code = 'Reject 13/14' THEN 'Overlap Error'
        WHEN log.Error_Code = 'GROUP_REJECTION' THEN 'Group Rejection (Rule 5)'
        WHEN log.Error_Code = 'CLOSED_RECORD' THEN 'Closed Record Update Attempt'
        ELSE 'Other'
    END AS Error_Category,
    
    -- Source file metadata
    src.File_Date AS Source_File_Date,
    src.SqlBulkCopy_TS AS Loaded_To_Staging_Date

FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log log
LEFT JOIN dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data src ON
    log.File_Date = src.File_Date
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(src.NDC)) = log.NDC
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(src.REIMB_CODE)) = log.Procedure_Code
    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.ADD_DATE, 101) = log.Effective_Date

WHERE log.Action_Taken = 'REJECTED'
    AND log.File_Date IN ('2025-10-01', '2025-11-01', '2025-12-01')

ORDER BY 
    log.File_Date,
    log.Error_Code,
    log.NDC,
    log.Procedure_Code;
```

---

## **📊 QUERY: REJECTED RECORDS SUMMARY BY MONTH**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- REJECTED RECORDS SUMMARY
-- Count by error code per month
-- ============================================================

SELECT 
    File_Date,
    Error_Code,
    COUNT(*) AS Rejection_Count,
    CAST(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (PARTITION BY File_Date) AS DECIMAL(5,2)) AS Pct_Of_Month_Errors,
    MIN(Message) AS Sample_Message
FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
WHERE Action_Taken = 'REJECTED'
    AND File_Date IN ('2025-10-01', '2025-11-01', '2025-12-01')
GROUP BY File_Date, Error_Code
ORDER BY File_Date, COUNT(*) DESC;
```

---

## **📄 QUERY: EXPORT TO CSV FORMAT**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- REJECTED RECORDS - CSV EXPORT FORMAT
-- All fields, tab-delimited for easy Excel export
-- ============================================================

SELECT 
    log.File_Date AS 'File Date',
    log.Error_Code AS 'Error Code',
    log.Message AS 'Rejection Reason',
    
    -- Source Data
    src.NDC AS 'NDC',
    src.DRUG_NAME AS 'Drug Name',
    src.REIMB_CODE AS 'Procedure Code',
    src.RC_DESC AS 'Procedure Description',
    src.IS_NOC AS 'NOC Indicator',
    src.ADD_DATE AS 'Effective Date (Raw)',
    src.RMV_DATE AS 'Ineffective Date (Raw)',
    
    -- Converted Values
    TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.ADD_DATE, 101) AS 'Effective Date (Converted)',
    TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.RMV_DATE, 101) AS 'Ineffective Date (Converted)',
    
    -- Additional Fields
    src.BILL_UNITS AS 'Bill Units',
    src.BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG AS 'Bill Units Per Package',
    src.PKG_SIZE AS 'Package Size',
    src.PKG_QTY AS 'Package Quantity',
    src.UOM AS 'Unit of Measure',
    src.PKG_UOM AS 'Package UOM',
    
    -- Processing Info
    log.Created_Date AS 'Processed Date',
    log.Log_ID AS 'Log ID'

FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log log
LEFT JOIN dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data src ON
    log.File_Date = src.File_Date
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(src.NDC)) = log.NDC
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(src.REIMB_CODE)) = log.Procedure_Code
    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.ADD_DATE, 101) = log.Effective_Date

WHERE log.Action_Taken = 'REJECTED'
    AND log.File_Date IN ('2025-10-01', '2025-11-01', '2025-12-01')

ORDER BY 
    log.File_Date,
    log.Error_Code,
    src.NDC,
    src.REIMB_CODE;
```

---

## **📋 QUERY: REJECTED RECORDS BY ERROR TYPE**

### **Reject 01: NDC Validation Errors**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- REJECT 01: NDC VALIDATION ERRORS
-- ============================================================

SELECT 
    log.File_Date,
    src.NDC AS NDC_Raw,
    LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(src.NDC))) AS NDC_Length,
    src.REIMB_CODE AS Procedure_Code,
    src.DRUG_NAME,
    src.ADD_DATE,
    src.RMV_DATE,
    log.Message AS Rejection_Reason,
    CASE 
        WHEN src.NDC IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(src.NDC)) = '' THEN 'Missing'
        WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(src.NDC))) <> 11 THEN 'Invalid Length (' + CAST(LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(src.NDC))) AS VARCHAR) + ' chars)'
        WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(src.NDC)) LIKE '%[^0-9]%' THEN 'Contains Non-Numeric Characters'
        ELSE 'Unknown'
    END AS Issue_Detail
FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log log
LEFT JOIN dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data src ON
    log.File_Date = src.File_Date
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(src.NDC)) = log.NDC
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(src.REIMB_CODE)) = log.Procedure_Code
WHERE log.Error_Code = 'Reject 01'
    AND log.File_Date IN ('2025-10-01', '2025-11-01', '2025-12-01')
ORDER BY log.File_Date, src.NDC;
```

---

### **Reject 06: Ineffective < Effective**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- REJECT 06: INEFFECTIVE DATE BEFORE EFFECTIVE DATE
-- ============================================================

SELECT 
    log.File_Date,
    src.NDC,
    src.DRUG_NAME,
    src.REIMB_CODE AS Procedure_Code,
    src.RC_DESC AS Procedure_Description,
    
    -- Raw Dates
    src.ADD_DATE AS Effective_Raw,
    src.RMV_DATE AS Ineffective_Raw,
    
    -- Converted Dates
    TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.ADD_DATE, 101) AS Effective_Date,
    TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.RMV_DATE, 101) AS Ineffective_Date,
    
    -- Calculate Difference
    DATEDIFF(DAY, 
        TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.RMV_DATE, 101), 
        TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.ADD_DATE, 101)
    ) AS Days_Backward,
    
    log.Message AS Rejection_Reason

FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log log
LEFT JOIN dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data src ON
    log.File_Date = src.File_Date
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(src.NDC)) = log.NDC
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(src.REIMB_CODE)) = log.Procedure_Code
    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.ADD_DATE, 101) = log.Effective_Date

WHERE log.Error_Code = 'Reject 06'
    AND log.File_Date IN ('2025-10-01', '2025-11-01', '2025-12-01')

ORDER BY 
    log.File_Date,
    Days_Backward DESC;
```

---

### **Reject 13/14: Overlapping Records**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- REJECT 13/14: OVERLAPPING DATE RANGES
-- ============================================================

SELECT 
    log.File_Date,
    
    -- Rejected Record
    src.NDC,
    src.DRUG_NAME,
    src.REIMB_CODE AS Procedure_Code,
    TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.ADD_DATE, 101) AS New_Effective,
    TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.RMV_DATE, 101) AS New_Ineffective,
    
    -- Existing Record (from production)
    prod.Effective_Date AS Existing_Effective,
    prod.Ineffective_Date AS Existing_Ineffective,
    
    -- Overlap Details
    log.Message AS Rejection_Reason,
    
    -- Show overlap period
    CASE 
        WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.RMV_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
            AND prod.Ineffective_Date IS NULL 
        THEN 'Both open-ended'
        WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.RMV_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
        THEN 'New record open-ended, conflicts with existing'
        WHEN prod.Ineffective_Date IS NULL 
        THEN 'Existing record open-ended, conflicts with new'
        ELSE 'Date ranges overlap'
    END AS Overlap_Type

FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log log
LEFT JOIN dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data src ON
    log.File_Date = src.File_Date
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(src.NDC)) = log.NDC
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(src.REIMB_CODE)) = log.Procedure_Code
    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.ADD_DATE, 101) = log.Effective_Date
LEFT JOIN dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk prod ON
    log.NDC = prod.NDC
    AND log.Procedure_Code = prod.Procedure_Code

WHERE log.Error_Code = 'Reject 13/14'
    AND log.File_Date IN ('2025-10-01', '2025-11-01', '2025-12-01')

ORDER BY log.File_Date, src.NDC, src.REIMB_CODE;
```

---

### **GROUP_REJECTION: Group Rejection (Rule 5)**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- GROUP_REJECTION: RECORDS REJECTED DUE TO GROUP ERRORS
-- Shows the valid record + what caused group to be rejected
-- ============================================================

WITH GroupErrors AS (
    -- Find the actual error in each group
    SELECT 
        File_Date,
        NDC,
        Procedure_Code,
        Error_Code,
        Message,
        Effective_Date
    FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
    WHERE Error_Code LIKE 'Reject%'
        AND File_Date IN ('2025-10-01', '2025-11-01', '2025-12-01')
)
SELECT 
    log.File_Date,
    
    -- This record (rejected via group rejection)
    src.NDC,
    src.DRUG_NAME,
    src.REIMB_CODE AS Procedure_Code,
    src.ADD_DATE AS Effective_Raw,
    src.RMV_DATE AS Ineffective_Raw,
    TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.ADD_DATE, 101) AS Effective_Date,
    TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.RMV_DATE, 101) AS Ineffective_Date,
    
    -- Validation Status
    CASE 
        WHEN src.NDC IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(src.NDC)) = '' THEN 'NDC Missing'
        WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(src.NDC))) <> 11 THEN 'NDC Invalid Length'
        WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(src.NDC)) LIKE '%[^0-9]%' THEN 'NDC Non-Numeric'
        WHEN src.REIMB_CODE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(src.REIMB_CODE)) = '' THEN 'Proc Code Missing'
        WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(src.REIMB_CODE))) <> 5 THEN 'Proc Code Invalid Length'
        WHEN src.ADD_DATE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(src.ADD_DATE)) = '' THEN 'Effective Date Missing'
        WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.ADD_DATE, 101) IS NULL THEN 'Effective Date Invalid Format'
        WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.RMV_DATE, 101) IS NOT NULL 
            AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.RMV_DATE, 101) < TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.ADD_DATE, 101) 
        THEN 'Ineffective < Effective'
        ELSE 'Valid (but rejected due to group)'
    END AS This_Record_Status,
    
    -- What caused the group rejection
    ge.Error_Code AS Group_Error_Code,
    ge.Message AS Group_Error_Reason,
    ge.Effective_Date AS Error_Record_Effective_Date

FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log log
LEFT JOIN dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data src ON
    log.File_Date = src.File_Date
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(src.NDC)) = log.NDC
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(src.REIMB_CODE)) = log.Procedure_Code
    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.ADD_DATE, 101) = log.Effective_Date
LEFT JOIN GroupErrors ge ON
    log.File_Date = ge.File_Date
    AND log.NDC = ge.NDC
    AND log.Procedure_Code = ge.Procedure_Code

WHERE log.Error_Code = 'GROUP_REJECTION'
    AND log.File_Date IN ('2025-10-01', '2025-11-01', '2025-12-01')

ORDER BY log.File_Date, src.NDC, src.REIMB_CODE, src.ADD_DATE;
```

---

## **📊 COMPREHENSIVE REJECTION REPORT - SINGLE QUERY**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- COMPLETE REJECTION REPORT
-- All rejected records with full context
-- ============================================================

WITH RejectionDetails AS (
    SELECT 
        log.Log_ID,
        log.File_Date,
        log.Error_Code,
        log.Message,
        log.Action_Taken,
        log.Created_Date,
        
        -- Source data
        src.NDC,
        src.DRUG_NAME,
        src.REIMB_CODE,
        src.RC_DESC,
        src.IS_NOC,
        src.ADD_DATE,
        src.RMV_DATE,
        src.BILL_UNITS,
        src.BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG,
        src.PKG_SIZE,
        src.PKG_QTY,
        src.UOM,
        src.PKG_UOM,
        
        -- Converted values
        TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.ADD_DATE, 101) AS Effective_Date,
        TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.RMV_DATE, 101) AS Ineffective_Date,
        
        -- Row number for deduplication
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
            PARTITION BY log.File_Date, log.NDC, log.Procedure_Code, log.Effective_Date, log.Error_Code
            ORDER BY log.Log_ID
        ) AS rn
        
    FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log log
    LEFT JOIN dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data src ON
        log.File_Date = src.File_Date
        AND LTRIM(RTRIM(src.NDC)) = log.NDC
        AND LTRIM(RTRIM(src.REIMB_CODE)) = log.Procedure_Code
        AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, src.ADD_DATE, 101) = log.Effective_Date
    
    WHERE log.Action_Taken = 'REJECTED'
        AND log.File_Date IN ('2025-10-01', '2025-11-01', '2025-12-01')
)
SELECT 
    File_Date AS 'File Date',
    Error_Code AS 'Error Code',
    Message AS 'Rejection Reason',
    
    -- Record Details
    NDC AS 'NDC',
    DRUG_NAME AS 'Drug Name',
    REIMB_CODE AS 'Procedure Code',
    RC_DESC AS 'Procedure Description',
    IS_NOC AS 'NOC Indicator',
    
    -- Dates
    ADD_DATE AS 'Effective Date (Raw)',
    RMV_DATE AS 'Ineffective Date (Raw)',
    Effective_Date AS 'Effective Date (Converted)',
    Ineffective_Date AS 'Ineffective Date (Converted)',
    
    -- Validation Details
    CASE 
        WHEN Error_Code = 'Reject 01' THEN 
            CASE 
                WHEN NDC IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) = '' THEN 'NDC is missing'
                WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC))) <> 11 THEN 'NDC length is ' + CAST(LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC))) AS VARCHAR) + ' (expected 11)'
                WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) LIKE '%[^0-9]%' THEN 'NDC contains non-numeric characters'
                ELSE 'NDC validation failed'
            END
        WHEN Error_Code = 'Reject 06' THEN 
            'Ineffective date (' + ISNULL(CONVERT(VARCHAR, Ineffective_Date, 23), 'NULL') + 
            ') is before Effective date (' + ISNULL(CONVERT(VARCHAR, Effective_Date, 23), 'NULL') + ')'
        WHEN Error_Code = 'Reject 13/14' THEN 'Record overlaps with existing mapping'
        WHEN Error_Code = 'GROUP_REJECTION' THEN 'Valid record but rejected due to error in same NDC-Proc group'
        ELSE 'See Rejection Reason'
    END AS 'Issue Details',
    
    -- Additional Fields
    BILL_UNITS AS 'Bill Units',
    BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG AS 'Bill Units Per Package',
    PKG_SIZE AS 'Package Size',
    PKG_QTY AS 'Package Quantity',
    UOM AS 'Unit of Measure',
    PKG_UOM AS 'Package UOM',
    
    Created_Date AS 'Processed Date',
    Log_ID AS 'Log ID'

FROM RejectionDetails
WHERE rn = 1  -- Remove duplicates from multiple runs

ORDER BY 
    File_Date,
    Error_Code,
    NDC,
    REIMB_CODE,
    Effective_Date;
```

## **📊 QUICK SUMMARY COUNTS**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- QUICK REJECTION SUMMARY
-- ============================================================

SELECT 
    'Total Rejected Records' AS Metric,
    COUNT(*) AS Count
FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
WHERE Action_Taken = 'REJECTED'
    AND File_Date IN ('2025-10-01', '2025-11-01', '2025-12-01')

UNION ALL

SELECT 
    'October Rejections',
    COUNT(*)
FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
WHERE Action_Taken = 'REJECTED'
    AND File_Date = '2025-10-01'

UNION ALL

SELECT 
    'November Rejections',
    COUNT(*)
FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
WHERE Action_Taken = 'REJECTED'
    AND File_Date = '2025-11-01'

UNION ALL

SELECT 
    'December Rejections',
    COUNT(*)
FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
WHERE Action_Taken = 'REJECTED'
    AND File_Date = '2025-12-01';

-- Error Code Distribution
SELECT 
    Error_Code,
    COUNT(*) AS Total_Count,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN File_Date = '2025-10-01' THEN 1 END) AS Oct_Count,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN File_Date = '2025-11-01' THEN 1 END) AS Nov_Count,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN File_Date = '2025-12-01' THEN 1 END) AS Dec_Count
FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
WHERE Action_Taken = 'REJECTED'
    AND File_Date IN ('2025-10-01', '2025-11-01', '2025-12-01')
GROUP BY Error_Code
ORDER BY Total_Count DESC;
```

## @arunknc0211-art

SELECT distinct NDC,Procedure_Code
  FROM [DW_Reference].[dbo].[NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log]
  where Log_Type = 'ERROR' and Error_Code = 'GROUP_REJECTION'

this result gave 55 records 

and 

SELECT distinct NDC,Procedure_Code
  FROM [DW_Reference].[dbo].[NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log]
  where Log_Type = 'ERROR' and Error_Code = 'Reject 06'

this result gave me 107 

i want to check if 55 records are in 107 or not how ?

## Copilot

**Great question! You want to check if all GROUP_REJECTION records are also in the Reject 06 list (to verify Rule 5 is working correctly).**

---

## **🔍 METHOD 1: CHECK OVERLAP (Simple)**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Check if GROUP_REJECTION records exist in Reject 06
-- ============================================================

-- Get GROUP_REJECTION records
SELECT DISTINCT 
    NDC,
    Procedure_Code,
    'GROUP_REJECTION' AS Source
INTO #GroupRejection
FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
WHERE Log_Type = 'ERROR' 
    AND Error_Code = 'GROUP_REJECTION';

-- Get Reject 06 records
SELECT DISTINCT 
    NDC,
    Procedure_Code,
    'Reject 06' AS Source
INTO #Reject06
FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
WHERE Log_Type = 'ERROR' 
    AND Error_Code = 'Reject 06';

-- Check overlap
SELECT 
    gr.NDC,
    gr.Procedure_Code,
    CASE 
        WHEN r6.NDC IS NOT NULL THEN 'YES - Also in Reject 06 ✅'
        ELSE 'NO - NOT in Reject 06 ❌'
    END AS In_Reject06
FROM #GroupRejection gr
LEFT JOIN #Reject06 r6 ON 
    gr.NDC = r6.NDC 
    AND gr.Procedure_Code = r6.Procedure_Code
ORDER BY In_Reject06, gr.NDC;

-- Summary
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN r6.NDC IS NOT NULL THEN 'In Both (GROUP_REJECTION + Reject 06)'
        ELSE 'Only in GROUP_REJECTION (caused by other error)'
    END AS Category,
    COUNT(*) AS Count
FROM #GroupRejection gr
LEFT JOIN #Reject06 r6 ON 
    gr.NDC = r6.NDC 
    AND gr.Procedure_Code = r6.Procedure_Code
GROUP BY 
    CASE 
        WHEN r6.NDC IS NOT NULL THEN 'In Both (GROUP_REJECTION + Reject 06)'
        ELSE 'Only in GROUP_REJECTION (caused by other error)'
    END;

-- Cleanup
DROP TABLE #GroupRejection;
DROP TABLE #Reject06;
```

---

## **🔍 METHOD 2: USING SET OPERATIONS**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- Find GROUP_REJECTION that are NOT in Reject 06
-- ============================================================

-- Records in GROUP_REJECTION but NOT in Reject 06
SELECT DISTINCT 
    NDC,
    Procedure_Code,
    'Only in GROUP_REJECTION' AS Status
FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
WHERE Log_Type = 'ERROR' 
    AND Error_Code = 'GROUP_REJECTION'
    
EXCEPT

SELECT DISTINCT 
    NDC,
    Procedure_Code,
    'Only in GROUP_REJECTION'
FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
WHERE Log_Type = 'ERROR' 
    AND Error_Code = 'Reject 06';
```

**If this returns 0 rows:** All GROUP_REJECTION are also in Reject 06 ✅  
**If this returns rows:** These GROUP_REJECTION were caused by different errors ✅

---

## **🔍 METHOD 3: COMPREHENSIVE ANALYSIS**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- COMPLETE GROUP REJECTION ANALYSIS
-- Shows what error caused each GROUP_REJECTION
-- ============================================================

WITH GroupRejections AS (
    -- Get all GROUP_REJECTION records
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        NDC,
        Procedure_Code
    FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
    WHERE Log_Type = 'ERROR' 
        AND Error_Code = 'GROUP_REJECTION'
),
ActualErrors AS (
    -- Get all actual field validation errors for same NDC-Proc groups
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        NDC,
        Procedure_Code,
        Error_Code,
        Message
    FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
    WHERE Log_Type = 'ERROR' 
        AND Error_Code LIKE 'Reject%'  -- Only field validation errors
        AND Error_Code <> 'GROUP_REJECTION'  -- Exclude GROUP_REJECTION itself
)
SELECT 
    gr.NDC,
    gr.Procedure_Code,
    STRING_AGG(ae.Error_Code, ', ') AS Caused_By_Errors,
    COUNT(DISTINCT ae.Error_Code) AS Error_Count
FROM GroupRejections gr
LEFT JOIN ActualErrors ae ON 
    gr.NDC = ae.NDC 
    AND gr.Procedure_Code = ae.Procedure_Code
GROUP BY gr.NDC, gr.Procedure_Code
ORDER BY Error_Count DESC, gr.NDC;
```

**Expected Results:**
```
NDC            Procedure_Code   Caused_By_Errors            Error_Count
00781910985    X5724            Reject 06                   1
12345678901    J0135            Reject 01, Reject 06        2
...
```

---

## **🔍 METHOD 4: DETAILED BREAKDOWN**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- DETAILED BREAKDOWN OF GROUP REJECTIONS
-- Shows all records in each NDC-Proc group
-- ============================================================

WITH GroupRejections AS (
    -- Get unique NDC-Proc combinations that have GROUP_REJECTION
    SELECT DISTINCT 
        NDC,
        Procedure_Code
    FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
    WHERE Error_Code = 'GROUP_REJECTION'
)
SELECT 
    gr.NDC,
    gr.Procedure_Code,
    log.Effective_Date,
    log.Error_Code,
    log.Message,
    log.File_Date,
    CASE 
        WHEN log.Error_Code = 'GROUP_REJECTION' THEN 'Rejected due to group error'
        ELSE 'Actual error in group'
    END AS Record_Type
FROM GroupRejections gr
INNER JOIN dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log log ON
    gr.NDC = log.NDC
    AND gr.Procedure_Code = log.Procedure_Code
WHERE log.Log_Type = 'ERROR'
ORDER BY 
    gr.NDC,
    gr.Procedure_Code,
    log.Effective_Date,
    CASE WHEN log.Error_Code = 'GROUP_REJECTION' THEN 2 ELSE 1 END;  -- Show actual errors first
```

---

## **🎯 EXPECTED RESULTS INTERPRETATION**

### **Scenario 1: All 55 in 107 (Simple Case)**
```
Category                                           Count
------------------------------------------------  -----
In Both (GROUP_REJECTION + Reject 06)             55
```
**Meaning:** All GROUP_REJECTION records were caused by Reject 06 errors in same group ✅

---

### **Scenario 2: Mixed (More Common)**
```
Category                                           Count
------------------------------------------------  -----
In Both (GROUP_REJECTION + Reject 06)             45
Only in GROUP_REJECTION (caused by other error)   10
```
**Meaning:** 
- 45 NDC-Proc groups: Had Reject 06 error → caused GROUP_REJECTION ✅
- 10 NDC-Proc groups: Had different error (Reject 01, 02, etc.) → caused GROUP_REJECTION ✅

**Both are valid!   Rule 5 working correctly.**

## **🔍 REAL-WORLD EXAMPLE**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- EXAMPLE: Show one specific GROUP_REJECTION case
-- ============================================================

-- Pick one NDC-Proc with GROUP_REJECTION
DECLARE @TestNDC VARCHAR(11) = '00781910985';
DECLARE @TestProc VARCHAR(5) = 'X5724';

-- Show all log entries for this group
SELECT 
    File_Date,
    Effective_Date,
    Error_Code,
    Message,
    Action_Taken,
    Created_Date
FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
WHERE NDC = @TestNDC
    AND Procedure_Code = @TestProc
    AND Log_Type = 'ERROR'
ORDER BY File_Date, Effective_Date, Log_ID;

-- Show source data
SELECT 
    File_Date,
    NDC,
    REIMB_CODE,
    ADD_DATE,
    RMV_DATE
FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
WHERE NDC = @TestNDC
    AND REIMB_CODE = @TestProc
ORDER BY File_Date, ADD_DATE;
```

**Expected Output:**
```
File_Date    Effective_Date   Error_Code         Message
2025-10-01   2017-10-01       Reject 06          Ineffective < Effective
2025-10-01   2008-09-01       GROUP_REJECTION    Rejected due to group rule

Source Data:
File_Date    NDC           REIMB_CODE   ADD_DATE      RMV_DATE
2025-10-01   00781910985   X5724        2017-10-01    2011-10-15  ← Ineff < Eff (Reject 06)
2025-10-01   00781910985   X5724        2008-09-01    2011-03-31  ← Valid, but group rejected
```

---

## **✅ SIMPLE ONE-LINER CHECK**

```sql
-- Quick check: How many GROUP_REJECTION are NOT caused by Reject 06?
SELECT COUNT(*) AS GROUP_REJECTION_Not_In_Reject06
FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT NDC, Procedure_Code
    FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
    WHERE Error_Code = 'GROUP_REJECTION'
    
    EXCEPT
    
    SELECT DISTINCT NDC, Procedure_Code
    FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
    WHERE Error_Code = 'Reject 06'
) x;
```

**Result:**
- **0** → All GROUP_REJECTION caused by Reject 06 only
- **> 0** → Some GROUP_REJECTION caused by other errors (Reject 01, 02, 03, etc.)

---

## **📊 VISUAL VENN DIAGRAM (SQL)**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- VENN DIAGRAM: GROUP_REJECTION vs Reject 06
-- ============================================================

SELECT 
    'Only Reject 06 (no GROUP_REJECTION)' AS Category,
    COUNT(*) AS Count
FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT NDC, Procedure_Code
    FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
    WHERE Error_Code = 'Reject 06'
    
    EXCEPT
    
    SELECT DISTINCT NDC, Procedure_Code
    FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
    WHERE Error_Code = 'GROUP_REJECTION'
) x

UNION ALL

SELECT 
    'Both Reject 06 AND GROUP_REJECTION',
    COUNT(*)
FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT NDC, Procedure_Code
    FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
    WHERE Error_Code = 'Reject 06'
    
    INTERSECT
    
    SELECT DISTINCT NDC, Procedure_Code
    FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
    WHERE Error_Code = 'GROUP_REJECTION'
) x

UNION ALL

SELECT 
    'Only GROUP_REJECTION (caused by other error)',
    COUNT(*)
FROM (
    SELECT DISTINCT NDC, Procedure_Code
    FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
    WHERE Error_Code = 'GROUP_REJECTION'
    
    EXCEPT
    
    SELECT DISTINCT NDC, Procedure_Code
    FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
    WHERE Error_Code = 'Reject 06'
) x;
```

**Expected Result:**
```
Category                                              Count
---------------------------------------------------  -----
Only Reject 06 (no GROUP_REJECTION)                  52   ← Single-record groups with Reject 06
Both Reject 06 AND GROUP_REJECTION                   45   ← Multi-record groups
Only GROUP_REJECTION (caused by other error)         10   ← Groups with Reject 01, 02, etc.
```

---

**Run METHOD 1 or the Simple One-Liner to get your answer!   ** 🎯

This will show you exactly which GROUP_REJECTION records are also in Reject 06 vs. caused by other errors.
