## **🔧 STEP 1: ALTER PRODUCTION TABLE**

Add new columns to `Procedure_NDC_Xwalk`:

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- ALTER PRODUCTION TABLE - ADD NEW COLUMNS
-- ============================================================

USE DW_Reference;
GO

-- Add new columns to production table
ALTER TABLE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
    ADD Drug_Name VARCHAR(255) NULL,
        RC_Desc VARCHAR(512) NULL,
        Bill_Units DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
        Bill_Units_Per_Pkg DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
        Pkg_Size DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
        Pkg_Qty INT NULL,
        UOM VARCHAR(10) NULL,
        Pkg_UOM VARCHAR(20) NULL,
        File_Date DATE NULL;  

## @arunknc0211-art

great everythings is woking great now 

and this is myproc at present:
USE [DW_Reference]
GO

/****** Object:  StoredProcedure [dbo].[usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk]    Script Date: 1/24/2026 7:56:10 PM ******/
SET ANSI_NULLS ON
GO

SET QUOTED_IDENTIFIER ON
GO




CREATE PROCEDURE [dbo].[usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk]
    @FileDate DATE
AS
BEGIN
    SET NOCOUNT ON;
    
    DECLARE @StartTime DATETIME2 = GETDATE();
    DECLARE @TotalRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @ExcludedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @InsertedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @UpdatedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @RejectedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @WarningRecords INT = 0;
    
    BEGIN TRY
        
        PRINT '============================================================';
        PRINT 'NDC CROSSWALK PROCESSING';
        PRINT 'File Date: ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @FileDate, 23);
        PRINT 'Start Time: ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @StartTime, 120);
        PRINT '============================================================';
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 1: Count total records
        -- =====================================================
        
        SELECT @TotalRecords = COUNT(*)
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate;
        
        PRINT 'Total records in file: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        -- Count excluded records (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01)
        SELECT @ExcludedRecords = COUNT(*)
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate
            AND RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL
            AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> ''
            AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < '2014-01-01';
        
        PRINT 'Excluded records (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01): ' + CAST(@ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Records to process: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        IF (@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords) = 0
        BEGIN
            PRINT 'WARNING: No records to process after exclusions';
            RETURN;
        END
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 2: Create temp working table with validation
        -- =====================================================
        
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#WorkingData') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #WorkingData;
        
        SELECT 
            LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) AS NDC,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) AS Procedure_Code,
            -- Case-insensitive NOC:  y or Y both become 'Y'
            CASE 
                WHEN UPPER(LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC))) = 'Y' THEN 'Y' 
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS NOC_Indicator,
            TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) AS Effective_Date,
            TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) AS Ineffective_Date,
            File_Date,
            
            -- Validation flags
            CAST(CASE 
                -- Reject 01: NDC validations
                WHEN NDC IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC MISSING'
                WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC))) <> 11 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (length)'
                WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) LIKE '%[^0-9]%' 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (non-numeric)'
                
                -- Reject 02: Procedure Code validations
                WHEN REIMB_CODE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE MISSING'
                WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE))) <> 5 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (length)'
                WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) LIKE '%[^A-Z0-9]%' 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (alphanumeric)'
                
                -- Reject 03: NOC Indicator validation (case-insensitive)
                WHEN IS_NOC IS NOT NULL 
                    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC)) <> '' 
                    AND UPPER(LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC))) <> 'Y' 
                    THEN 'Reject 03: NOC INDICATOR UNKNOWN VALUE'
                
                -- Reject 04: Effective Date validations
                WHEN ADD_DATE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(ADD_DATE)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE MISSING'
                WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
                    THEN 'Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT'
                
                -- Reject 05: Ineffective Date validation
                WHEN RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL 
                    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> '' 
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
                    THEN 'Reject 05: INEFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT'
                
                -- Reject 06: Ineffective before Effective
                WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NOT NULL 
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) IS NOT NULL
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)
                    THEN 'Reject 06: INEFFECTIVE DATE BEFORE EFFECTIVE DATE'
                
                ELSE NULL
            END AS NVARCHAR(500)) AS Validation_Error,
            
            0 AS Is_Processed
            
        INTO #WorkingData
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate
            -- =====================================================
            -- NEW EXCLUSION FILTER: Exclude RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01
            -- =====================================================
            AND (
                -- Include if RMV_DATE is NULL or empty (open-ended records)
                RMV_DATE IS NULL 
                OR LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) = ''
                -- OR include if RMV_DATE conversion fails (will be caught by validation)
                OR TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NULL
                -- OR include if RMV_DATE >= 2014-01-01 (recent closed mappings)
                OR TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) >= '2014-01-01'
            );
        
	DECLARE @WorkingCount INT = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM #WorkingData);
        PRINT 'Working data loaded: ' + CAST(@WorkingCount AS VARCHAR) + ' records';
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 3: Log field validation errors
        -- =====================================================
        
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                 Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            CONCAT(ISNULL(NDC, ''), '-', ISNULL(Procedure_Code, ''), '-', ISNULL(CONVERT(VARCHAR, Effective_Date, 23), '')),
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            LEFT(Validation_Error, CHARINDEX(':', Validation_Error) - 1),
            Validation_Error,
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NOT NULL;
        
        SET @RejectedRecords = @@ROWCOUNT;
        PRINT 'Field validation errors: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 4: Check for duplicates (Reject 11)
        -- =====================================================
        
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                 Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            CONCAT(NDC, '-', Procedure_Code, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, Effective_Date, 23)),
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'Reject 11',
            'DUPLICATE RECORDS:  Key appears ' + CAST(COUNT(*) AS VARCHAR) + ' times',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
        GROUP BY NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date
        HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
        
        -- Mark duplicates with error
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'Reject 11: DUPLICATE RECORDS'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM #WorkingData w2
                WHERE w2.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND w2.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND w2.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
                    AND w2.Validation_Error IS NULL
                GROUP BY w2.NDC, w2.Procedure_Code, w2.Effective_Date
                HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
            );
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- FIX 2: IMPROVED OVERLAP CHECK - Use Projected State
        -- CORRECTED: Only include columns needed for overlap check
        -- =====================================================
        
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#ProjectedProduction') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #ProjectedProduction;
        
        -- Start with current production (only columns needed for overlap check)
        SELECT 
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            Ineffective_Date
        INTO #ProjectedProduction
        FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk;
        
        -- Apply ineffective date updates from working data to create projected state
        UPDATE proj
        SET proj.Ineffective_Date = w.Ineffective_Date
        FROM #ProjectedProduction proj
        INNER JOIN #WorkingData w ON
            proj.NDC = w.NDC
            AND proj.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
            AND proj.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL;
        
        -- Check overlaps against PROJECTED state
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                 Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            CONCAT(w.NDC, '-', w. Procedure_Code, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Effective_Date, 23)),
            w.NDC,
            w.Procedure_Code,
            w.Effective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'Reject 13/14',
            'OVERLAP:  New (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Effective_Date, 23) + ' to ' + 
                ISNULL(CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Ineffective_Date, 23), 'open') + ') overlaps existing (' +
                CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Effective_Date, 23) + ' to ' + 
                ISNULL(CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Ineffective_Date, 23), 'open') + ')',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN #ProjectedProduction p ON
            w.NDC = p.NDC
            AND w.Procedure_Code = p.Procedure_Code
            AND w.Effective_Date <> p.Effective_Date  -- Different effective dates
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND (
                -- New starts during existing period
                (w. Effective_Date >= p.Effective_Date 
                    AND (p.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Effective_Date <= p.Ineffective_Date))
                OR
                -- New ends during existing period
                (w. Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL 
                    AND w.Ineffective_Date >= p.Effective_Date
                    AND (p.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w. Ineffective_Date <= p.Ineffective_Date))
                OR
                -- New encompasses existing
                (w.Effective_Date <= p.Effective_Date
                    AND (w.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date >= p.Effective_Date))
            );
        
        -- Mark overlaps with error
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'Reject 13/14: OVERLAP'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log log
                WHERE log.File_Date = @FileDate
                    AND log.Error_Code = 'Reject 13/14'
                    AND log.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND log.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND log.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
            );
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 6: Group Rejection Rule (Rule 5)
        -- =====================================================
        
        -- Find groups with errors
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#ErrorGroups') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #ErrorGroups;
        
        SELECT DISTINCT NDC, Procedure_Code
        INTO #ErrorGroups
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NOT NULL;
        
        -- Reject all records in error groups
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'GROUP_REJECTION'  
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN #ErrorGroups eg ON w.NDC = eg.NDC AND w.Procedure_Code = eg.Procedure_Code
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL;
        
        -- Log group rejections
        INSERT INTO dbo. NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                 Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            CONCAT(NDC, '-', Procedure_Code, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, Effective_Date, 23)),
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'GROUP_REJECTION',
            'Rejected due to group rejection rule (Rule 5)',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error = 'GROUP_REJECTION';
        
        DECLARE @GroupRejected INT = @@ROWCOUNT;
        SET @RejectedRecords = @RejectedRecords + @GroupRejected;
        
        PRINT 'Group rejection additional errors: ' + CAST(@GroupRejected AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Total rejected records: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 7: Detect Warnings (Warning 15 - Retro Records)
        -- =====================================================
        
        INSERT INTO dbo. NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                 Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            CONCAT(w.NDC, '-', w.Procedure_Code, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Effective_Date, 23)),
            w.NDC,
            w. Procedure_Code,
            w. Effective_Date,
            'WARNING',
            'Warning 15',
            'RETRO RECORD: Ineffective date (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Ineffective_Date, 23) + 
                ') earlier than existing effective date (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Effective_Date, 23) + ')',
            'WILL_INSERT'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk p ON
            w.NDC = p.NDC
            AND w.Procedure_Code = p.Procedure_Code
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL
            AND w. Ineffective_Date < p. Effective_Date
            AND NOT EXISTS (
                -- Ensure no overlap
                SELECT 1
                FROM dbo. Procedure_NDC_Xwalk p2
                WHERE p2.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND p2.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND (
                        (w.Effective_Date >= p2.Effective_Date 
                            AND (p2.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Effective_Date <= p2.Ineffective_Date))
                        OR
                        (w.Ineffective_Date >= p2.Effective_Date
                            AND (p2.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date <= p2.Ineffective_Date))
                    )
            );
        
        SET @WarningRecords = @@ROWCOUNT;
        PRINT 'Warnings detected: ' + CAST(@WarningRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 8: Sort and Process Valid Records (Rule 3 & 4)
        -- =====================================================
        
        PRINT 'Processing valid records...';
        
        -- Create sorted cursor for processing (oldest first)
        DECLARE @NDC VARCHAR(11), @ProcCode VARCHAR(5), @NOC CHAR(1);
        DECLARE @EffDate DATE, @IneffDate DATE;
        DECLARE @ExistingIneff DATE;
        
        DECLARE process_cursor CURSOR LOCAL FAST_FORWARD FOR
            SELECT NDC, Procedure_Code, NOC_Indicator, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date
            FROM #WorkingData
            WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            ORDER BY Procedure_Code, NDC, Effective_Date;  -- Rule 3: Sort by Proc, NDC, Eff Date
        
        OPEN process_cursor;
        FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO @NDC, @ProcCode, @NOC, @EffDate, @IneffDate;
        
        WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0
        BEGIN
            
            -- =====================================================
            -- NEW:  Check for ATTEMPT TO UPDATE CLOSED RECORD
            -- =====================================================
            
            SET @ExistingIneff = NULL;
            
            SELECT @ExistingIneff = Ineffective_Date
            FROM dbo. Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
            WHERE NDC = @NDC
                AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode
                AND Effective_Date = @EffDate;
            
            -- If record exists and already has ineffective date
            IF @ExistingIneff IS NOT NULL 
                AND @IneffDate IS NOT NULL 
                AND @IneffDate <> @ExistingIneff
            BEGIN
                -- Log error:  Attempting to update closed record
                INSERT INTO dbo. NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                         Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
                VALUES (
                    @FileDate, 
                    CONCAT(@NDC, '-', @ProcCode, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, @EffDate, 23)),
                    @NDC, 
                    @ProcCode, 
                    @EffDate, 
                    'ERROR', 
                    'CLOSED_RECORD',
                    'ATTEMPT TO UPDATE CLOSED RECORD: Existing Ineff=' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @ExistingIneff, 23) + 
                    ', New Ineff=' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @IneffDate, 23),
                    'REJECTED'
                );
                
                SET @RejectedRecords = @RejectedRecords + 1;
                
                -- Skip to next record
                FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO @NDC, @ProcCode, @NOC, @EffDate, @IneffDate;
                CONTINUE;
            END
            
            -- =====================================================
            -- Check if record exists
            -- =====================================================
            
            IF EXISTS (
                SELECT 1 
                FROM dbo. Procedure_NDC_Xwalk 
                WHERE NDC = @NDC 
                    AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode 
                    AND Effective_Date = @EffDate
            )
            BEGIN
                -- =====================================================
                -- FIX 1: ALWAYS UPDATE ALL FIELDS (no ISNULL logic)
                -- =====================================================
                UPDATE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
                SET 
                    NOC_Indicator = @NOC,           -- ← ALWAYS set from source (even if NULL)
                    Ineffective_Date = @IneffDate,  -- ← ALWAYS set from source (even if NULL)
                    User_ID = SYSTEM_USER,
                    DLU = GETDATE()
                WHERE NDC = @NDC
                    AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode
                    AND Effective_Date = @EffDate;
                    -- NO condition - ALWAYS execute UPDATE
                
                SET @UpdatedRecords = @UpdatedRecords + 1;
                
                INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                         Log_Type, Message, Action_Taken)
                VALUES (@FileDate, CONCAT(@NDC, '-', @ProcCode, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, @EffDate, 23)),
                        @NDC, @ProcCode, @EffDate, 'INFO', 'Record updated', 'UPDATED');
            END
            ELSE
            BEGIN
                -- INSERT new record
                INSERT INTO dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (NDC, Procedure_Code, NOC_Indicator, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date, User_ID, DLU)
                VALUES (@NDC, @ProcCode, @NOC, @EffDate, @IneffDate, SYSTEM_USER, GETDATE());
                
                SET @InsertedRecords = @InsertedRecords + 1;
                
                INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                         Log_Type, Message, Action_Taken)
                VALUES (@FileDate, CONCAT(@NDC, '-', @ProcCode, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, @EffDate, 23)),
                        @NDC, @ProcCode, @EffDate, 'INFO', 'New record inserted', 'INSERTED');
            END
            
            FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO @NDC, @ProcCode, @NOC, @EffDate, @IneffDate;
        END
        
        CLOSE process_cursor;
        DEALLOCATE process_cursor;
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- FINAL SUMMARY
        -- =====================================================
        
        PRINT '============================================================';
        PRINT 'PROCESSING COMPLETED';
        PRINT 'Total Records in File: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Excluded (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01): ' + CAST(@ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Processed: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Inserted: ' + CAST(@InsertedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Updated: ' + CAST(@UpdatedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Rejected: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Warnings: ' + CAST(@WarningRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Duration: ' + CAST(DATEDIFF(SECOND, @StartTime, GETDATE()) AS VARCHAR) + ' seconds';
        PRINT '============================================================';
        
        -- Return summary
        SELECT 
            @FileDate AS File_Date,
            @TotalRecords AS Total_Records_In_File,
            @ExcludedRecords AS Excluded_Pre2014,
            @InsertedRecords AS Inserted,
            @UpdatedRecords AS Updated,
            @RejectedRecords AS Rejected,
            @WarningRecords AS Warnings,
            DATEDIFF(SECOND, @StartTime, GETDATE()) AS Duration_Seconds;
        
        -- Cleanup
        DROP TABLE #WorkingData;
        DROP TABLE #ErrorGroups;
        DROP TABLE #ProjectedProduction;
        
    END TRY
    BEGIN CATCH
        PRINT 'ERROR: ' + ERROR_MESSAGE();
        THROW;
    END CATCH
    
END;
GO




but i have one more requirement

## Copilot

**Perfect! Let's enhance the table structure to match MMIS standards and add a File_Date column for better tracking.**

---

## **🔧 ENHANCED TABLE STRUCTURE**

### **Current Structure:**
```sql
Procedure_NDC_Xwalk:
- Xwalk_ID (PK, Identity)
- NDC
- Procedure_Code
- NOC_Indicator
- Effective_Date
- Ineffective_Date
- User_ID
- DLU
```

### **Enhanced Structure (All Source Fields):**
```sql
Procedure_NDC_Xwalk:
- Xwalk_ID (PK, Identity)
- NDC
- DRUG_NAME              ← NEW (from source)
- Procedure_Code
- RC_DESC                ← NEW (from source - procedure code description)
- NOC_Indicator
- Effective_Date
- Ineffective_Date
- BILL_UNITS             ← NEW (from source)
- BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG     ← NEW (from source)
- PKG_SIZE               ← NEW (from source)
- PKG_QTY                ← NEW (from source)
- UOM                    ← NEW (from source - unit of measure)
- PKG_UOM                ← NEW (from source - package unit of measure)
- User_ID
- DLU                    ← Processing timestamp
- File_Date              ← NEW - Source file date
```

---

## **📊 STEP 1: ALTER TABLE TO ADD NEW COLUMNS**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- ALTER TABLE: Add new columns from source + File_Date
-- ============================================================

USE DW_Reference;
GO

-- Add new columns
ALTER TABLE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
ADD 
    DRUG_NAME VARCHAR(255) NULL,
    RC_DESC VARCHAR(512) NULL,
    BILL_UNITS DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
    BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
    PKG_SIZE DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
    PKG_QTY INT NULL,
    UOM VARCHAR(10) NULL,
    PKG_UOM VARCHAR(20) NULL,
    File_Date DATE NULL;

PRINT 'Table altered successfully - new columns added';
GO

-- Verify new structure
SELECT 
    COLUMN_NAME,
    DATA_TYPE,
    CHARACTER_MAXIMUM_LENGTH,
    NUMERIC_PRECISION,
    NUMERIC_SCALE,
    IS_NULLABLE
FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS
WHERE TABLE_NAME = 'Procedure_NDC_Xwalk'
ORDER BY ORDINAL_POSITION;
GO
```

---

## **📊 STEP 2: UPDATED STORED PROCEDURE WITH ALL FIELDS**

```sql
USE DW_Reference;
GO

IF OBJECT_ID('dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk', 'P') IS NOT NULL
    DROP PROCEDURE dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk;
GO

CREATE PROCEDURE dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk
    @FileDate DATE
AS
BEGIN
    SET NOCOUNT ON;
    
    DECLARE @StartTime DATETIME2 = GETDATE();
    DECLARE @TotalRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @ExcludedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @InsertedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @UpdatedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @RejectedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @WarningRecords INT = 0;
    
    BEGIN TRY
        
        PRINT '============================================================';
        PRINT 'NDC CROSSWALK PROCESSING';
        PRINT 'File Date: ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @FileDate, 23);
        PRINT 'Start Time: ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @StartTime, 120);
        PRINT '============================================================';
        
        -- Count total records
        SELECT @TotalRecords = COUNT(*)
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate;
        
        PRINT 'Total records in file: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        -- Count excluded records (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01)
        SELECT @ExcludedRecords = COUNT(*)
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate
            AND RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL
            AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> ''
            AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < '2014-01-01';
        
        PRINT 'Excluded records (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01): ' + CAST(@ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Records to process: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        IF (@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords) = 0
        BEGIN
            PRINT 'WARNING: No records to process after exclusions';
            RETURN;
        END
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 2: Create temp working table with ALL SOURCE FIELDS
        -- =====================================================
        
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#WorkingData') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #WorkingData;
        
        SELECT 
            -- Core identification fields
            LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) AS NDC,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(DRUG_NAME)) AS DRUG_NAME,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) AS Procedure_Code,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(RC_DESC)) AS RC_DESC,
            
            -- NOC Indicator (case-insensitive)
            CASE 
                WHEN UPPER(LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC))) = 'Y' THEN 'Y' 
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS NOC_Indicator,
            
            -- Date fields
            TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) AS Effective_Date,
            TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) AS Ineffective_Date,
            
            -- Billing unit fields (with implied decimals after 6th digit)
            CASE 
                WHEN BILL_UNITS IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(BILL_UNITS) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(BILL_UNITS AS DECIMAL(12,6))
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS BILL_UNITS,
            
            CASE 
                WHEN BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG AS DECIMAL(12,6))
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG,
            
            CASE 
                WHEN PKG_SIZE IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(PKG_SIZE) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(PKG_SIZE AS DECIMAL(12,6))
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS PKG_SIZE,
            
            -- Package quantity
            CASE 
                WHEN PKG_QTY IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(PKG_QTY) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(PKG_QTY AS INT)
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS PKG_QTY,
            
            -- Unit of measure fields
            LTRIM(RTRIM(UOM)) AS UOM,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(PKG_UOM)) AS PKG_UOM,
            
            -- File date
            File_Date,
            
            -- Validation flags
            CAST(CASE 
                WHEN NDC IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC MISSING'
                WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC))) <> 11 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (length)'
                WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) LIKE '%[^0-9]%' 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (non-numeric)'
                
                WHEN REIMB_CODE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE MISSING'
                WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE))) <> 5 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (length)'
                WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) LIKE '%[^A-Z0-9]%' 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (alphanumeric)'
                
                WHEN IS_NOC IS NOT NULL 
                    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC)) <> '' 
                    AND UPPER(LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC))) <> 'Y' 
                    THEN 'Reject 03: NOC INDICATOR UNKNOWN VALUE'
                
                WHEN ADD_DATE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(ADD_DATE)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE MISSING'
                WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
                    THEN 'Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT'
                
                WHEN RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL 
                    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> '' 
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
                    THEN 'Reject 05: INEFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT'
                
                WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NOT NULL 
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) IS NOT NULL
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)
                    THEN 'Reject 06: INEFFECTIVE DATE BEFORE EFFECTIVE DATE'
                
                ELSE NULL
            END AS NVARCHAR(500)) AS Validation_Error,
            
            0 AS Is_Processed
            
        INTO #WorkingData
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate
            AND (
                RMV_DATE IS NULL 
                OR LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) = ''
                OR TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NULL
                OR TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) >= '2014-01-01'
            );
        
        DECLARE @WorkingCount INT = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM #WorkingData);
        PRINT 'Working data loaded: ' + CAST(@WorkingCount AS VARCHAR) + ' records';
        
        -- [Keep all validation logic the same - Field validation, Duplicates, Overlaps, Group Rejection, Warnings]
        -- ... [Previous validation code stays the same] ...
        
        -- Field validation errors
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                 Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            CONCAT(ISNULL(NDC, ''), '-', ISNULL(Procedure_Code, ''), '-', ISNULL(CONVERT(VARCHAR, Effective_Date, 23), '')),
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            LEFT(Validation_Error, CHARINDEX(':', Validation_Error) - 1),
            Validation_Error,
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NOT NULL;
        
        SET @RejectedRecords = @@ROWCOUNT;
        PRINT 'Field validation errors: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        -- Check for duplicates
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                 Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            CONCAT(NDC, '-', Procedure_Code, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, Effective_Date, 23)),
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'Reject 11',
            'DUPLICATE RECORDS: Key appears ' + CAST(COUNT(*) AS VARCHAR) + ' times',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
        GROUP BY NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date
        HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
        
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'Reject 11: DUPLICATE RECORDS'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM #WorkingData w2
                WHERE w2.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND w2.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND w2.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
                    AND w2.Validation_Error IS NULL
                GROUP BY w2.NDC, w2.Procedure_Code, w2.Effective_Date
                HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
            );
        
        -- Projected state for overlap check
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#ProjectedProduction') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #ProjectedProduction;
        
        SELECT 
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            Ineffective_Date
        INTO #ProjectedProduction
        FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk;
        
        UPDATE proj
        SET proj.Ineffective_Date = w.Ineffective_Date
        FROM #ProjectedProduction proj
        INNER JOIN #WorkingData w ON
            proj.NDC = w.NDC
            AND proj.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
            AND proj.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL;
        
        -- Check overlaps
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                 Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            CONCAT(w.NDC, '-', w.Procedure_Code, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Effective_Date, 23)),
            w.NDC,
            w.Procedure_Code,
            w.Effective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'Reject 13/14',
            'OVERLAP: New (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Effective_Date, 23) + ' to ' + 
                ISNULL(CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Ineffective_Date, 23), 'open') + ') overlaps existing (' +
                CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Effective_Date, 23) + ' to ' + 
                ISNULL(CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Ineffective_Date, 23), 'open') + ')',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN #ProjectedProduction p ON
            w.NDC = p.NDC
            AND w.Procedure_Code = p.Procedure_Code
            AND w.Effective_Date <> p.Effective_Date
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND (
                (w.Effective_Date >= p.Effective_Date 
                    AND (p.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Effective_Date <= p.Ineffective_Date))
                OR
                (w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL 
                    AND w.Ineffective_Date >= p.Effective_Date
                    AND (p.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date <= p.Ineffective_Date))
                OR
                (w.Effective_Date <= p.Effective_Date
                    AND (w.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date >= p.Effective_Date))
            );
        
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'Reject 13/14: OVERLAP'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log log
                WHERE log.File_Date = @FileDate
                    AND log.Error_Code = 'Reject 13/14'
                    AND log.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND log.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND log.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
            );
        
        -- Group Rejection
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#ErrorGroups') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #ErrorGroups;
        
        SELECT DISTINCT NDC, Procedure_Code
        INTO #ErrorGroups
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NOT NULL;
        
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'GROUP_REJECTION'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN #ErrorGroups eg ON w.NDC = eg.NDC AND w.Procedure_Code = eg.Procedure_Code
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL;
        
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                 Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            CONCAT(NDC, '-', Procedure_Code, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, Effective_Date, 23)),
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'GROUP_REJECTION',
            'Rejected due to group rejection rule (Rule 5)',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error = 'GROUP_REJECTION';
        
        DECLARE @GroupRejected INT = @@ROWCOUNT;
        SET @RejectedRecords = @RejectedRecords + @GroupRejected;
        
        PRINT 'Group rejection additional errors: ' + CAST(@GroupRejected AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Total rejected records: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        -- Warning 15
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                 Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            CONCAT(w.NDC, '-', w.Procedure_Code, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Effective_Date, 23)),
            w.NDC,
            w.Procedure_Code,
            w.Effective_Date,
            'WARNING',
            'Warning 15',
            'RETRO RECORD: Ineffective date (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Ineffective_Date, 23) + 
                ') earlier than existing effective date (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Effective_Date, 23) + ')',
            'WILL_INSERT'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk p ON
            w.NDC = p.NDC
            AND w.Procedure_Code = p.Procedure_Code
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL
            AND w.Ineffective_Date < p.Effective_Date
            AND NOT EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk p2
                WHERE p2.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND p2.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND (
                        (w.Effective_Date >= p2.Effective_Date 
                            AND (p2.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Effective_Date <= p2.Ineffective_Date))
                        OR
                        (w.Ineffective_Date >= p2.Effective_Date
                            AND (p2.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date <= p2.Ineffective_Date))
                    )
            );
        
        SET @WarningRecords = @@ROWCOUNT;
        PRINT 'Warnings detected: ' + CAST(@WarningRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 8: Process Valid Records with ALL FIELDS
        -- =====================================================
        
        PRINT 'Processing valid records...';
        
        DECLARE @NDC VARCHAR(11), @ProcCode VARCHAR(5), @NOC CHAR(1);
        DECLARE @DrugName VARCHAR(255), @RCDesc VARCHAR(512);
        DECLARE @EffDate DATE, @IneffDate DATE;
        DECLARE @BillUnits DECIMAL(12,6), @BillUnitsPkg DECIMAL(12,6);
        DECLARE @PkgSize DECIMAL(12,6), @PkgQty INT;
        DECLARE @UOM VARCHAR(10), @PkgUOM VARCHAR(20);
        DECLARE @FileDate_Value DATE;
        DECLARE @ExistingIneff DATE;
        
        DECLARE process_cursor CURSOR LOCAL FAST_FORWARD FOR
            SELECT 
                NDC, DRUG_NAME, Procedure_Code, RC_DESC, NOC_Indicator, 
                Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
                BILL_UNITS, BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG, PKG_SIZE, PKG_QTY,
                UOM, PKG_UOM, File_Date
            FROM #WorkingData
            WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            ORDER BY Procedure_Code, NDC, Effective_Date;
        
        OPEN process_cursor;
        FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO 
            @NDC, @DrugName, @ProcCode, @RCDesc, @NOC, 
            @EffDate, @IneffDate,
            @BillUnits, @BillUnitsPkg, @PkgSize, @PkgQty,
            @UOM, @PkgUOM, @FileDate_Value;
        
        WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0
        BEGIN
            
            SET @ExistingIneff = NULL;
            
            SELECT @ExistingIneff = Ineffective_Date
            FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
            WHERE NDC = @NDC
                AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode
                AND Effective_Date = @EffDate;
            
            IF @ExistingIneff IS NOT NULL 
                AND @IneffDate IS NOT NULL 
                AND @IneffDate <> @ExistingIneff
            BEGIN
                INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                         Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken)
                VALUES (
                    @FileDate, 
                    CONCAT(@NDC, '-', @ProcCode, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, @EffDate, 23)),
                    @NDC, 
                    @ProcCode, 
                    @EffDate, 
                    'ERROR', 
                    'CLOSED_RECORD',
                    'ATTEMPT TO UPDATE CLOSED RECORD: Existing Ineff=' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @ExistingIneff, 23) + 
                    ', New Ineff=' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @IneffDate, 23),
                    'REJECTED'
                );
                
                SET @RejectedRecords = @RejectedRecords + 1;
                FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO 
                    @NDC, @DrugName, @ProcCode, @RCDesc, @NOC, 
                    @EffDate, @IneffDate,
                    @BillUnits, @BillUnitsPkg, @PkgSize, @PkgQty,
                    @UOM, @PkgUOM, @FileDate_Value;
                CONTINUE;
            END
            
            IF EXISTS (
                SELECT 1 
                FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk 
                WHERE NDC = @NDC 
                    AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode 
                    AND Effective_Date = @EffDate
            )
            BEGIN
                -- UPDATE with ALL FIELDS
                UPDATE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
                SET 
                    DRUG_NAME = @DrugName,
                    RC_DESC = @RCDesc,
                    NOC_Indicator = @NOC,
                    Ineffective_Date = @IneffDate,
                    BILL_UNITS = @BillUnits,
                    BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG = @BillUnitsPkg,
                    PKG_SIZE = @PkgSize,
                    PKG_QTY = @PkgQty,
                    UOM = @UOM,
                    PKG_UOM = @PkgUOM,
                    User_ID = SYSTEM_USER,
                    DLU = GETDATE(),
                    File_Date = @FileDate_Value
                WHERE NDC = @NDC
                    AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode
                    AND Effective_Date = @EffDate;
                
                SET @UpdatedRecords = @UpdatedRecords + 1;
                
                INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                         Log_Type, Message, Action_Taken)
                VALUES (@FileDate, CONCAT(@NDC, '-', @ProcCode, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, @EffDate, 23)),
                        @NDC, @ProcCode, @EffDate, 'INFO', 'Record updated', 'UPDATED');
            END
            ELSE
            BEGIN
                -- INSERT with ALL FIELDS
                INSERT INTO dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (
                    NDC, DRUG_NAME, Procedure_Code, RC_DESC, NOC_Indicator, 
                    Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
                    BILL_UNITS, BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG, PKG_SIZE, PKG_QTY,
                    UOM, PKG_UOM,
                    User_ID, DLU, File_Date
                )
                VALUES (
                    @NDC, @DrugName, @ProcCode, @RCDesc, @NOC, 
                    @EffDate, @IneffDate,
                    @BillUnits, @BillUnitsPkg, @PkgSize, @PkgQty,
                    @UOM, @PkgUOM,
                    SYSTEM_USER, GETDATE(), @FileDate_Value
                );
                
                SET @InsertedRecords = @InsertedRecords + 1;
                
                INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, 
                                                         Log_Type, Message, Action_Taken)
                VALUES (@FileDate, CONCAT(@NDC, '-', @ProcCode, '-', CONVERT(VARCHAR, @EffDate, 23)),
                        @NDC, @ProcCode, @EffDate, 'INFO', 'New record inserted', 'INSERTED');
            END
            
            FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO 
                @NDC, @DrugName, @ProcCode, @RCDesc, @NOC, 
                @EffDate, @IneffDate,
                @BillUnits, @BillUnitsPkg, @PkgSize, @PkgQty,
                @UOM, @PkgUOM, @FileDate_Value;
        END
        
        CLOSE process_cursor;
        DEALLOCATE process_cursor;
        
        -- Final summary
        PRINT '============================================================';
        PRINT 'PROCESSING COMPLETED';
        PRINT 'Total Records in File: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Excluded (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01): ' + CAST(@ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Processed: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Inserted: ' + CAST(@InsertedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Updated: ' + CAST(@UpdatedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Rejected: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Warnings: ' + CAST(@WarningRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Duration: ' + CAST(DATEDIFF(SECOND, @StartTime, GETDATE()) AS VARCHAR) + ' seconds';
        PRINT '============================================================';
        
        SELECT 
            @FileDate AS File_Date,
            @TotalRecords AS Total_Records_In_File,
            @ExcludedRecords AS Excluded_Pre2014,
            @InsertedRecords AS Inserted,
            @UpdatedRecords AS Updated,
            @RejectedRecords AS Rejected,
            @WarningRecords AS Warnings,
            DATEDIFF(SECOND, @StartTime, GETDATE()) AS Duration_Seconds;
        
        DROP TABLE #WorkingData;
        DROP TABLE #ErrorGroups;
        DROP TABLE #ProjectedProduction;
        
    END TRY
    BEGIN CATCH
        PRINT 'ERROR: ' + ERROR_MESSAGE();
        THROW;
    END CATCH
    
END;
GO

PRINT 'Stored procedure updated with all source fields + File_Date!';
GO
```

---

## **✅ SUMMARY OF CHANGES**

### **1. New Columns Added to Table:**
- `DRUG_NAME` - Drug description
- `RC_DESC` - Procedure code description
- `BILL_UNITS` - Billing units
- `BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG` - Billing units per package
- `PKG_SIZE` - Package size
- `PKG_QTY` - Package quantity
- `UOM` - Unit of measure
- `PKG_UOM` - Package unit of measure
- `File_Date` - Source file date

### **2. Benefits:**
✅ **Complete data for claims adjudication** (matches MMIS structure)  
✅ **File_Date** tracks which file last updated the record  
✅ **DLU** tracks when the record was last processed  
✅ **All source fields** preserved for reporting and analysis

Want me to create a test script to validate the new fields are being populated correctly?

if i do this

-- ============================================================

    DRUG_NAME VARCHAR(255) NULL,
    RC_DESC VARCHAR(512) NULL,
    BILL_UNITS DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
    BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
    PKG_SIZE DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,

    UOM VARCHAR(10) NULL,

i wont be seing the columns in particlar and clear order 


Procedure_NDC_Xwalk:
- Xwalk_ID (PK, Identity)
- NDC
- DRUG_NAME              ← NEW (from source)

- RC_DESC                ← NEW (from source - procedure code description)

- PKG_QTY                ← NEW (from source)
- UOM                    ← NEW (from source - unit of measure)

- File_Date              ← NEW - Source file date
- DLU                    ← Processing timestamp

## Copilot

**Yes! Creating a fresh table with the proper column order is the best approach. Here's the complete script:**

---

## **🔧 COMPLETE TABLE RECREATION SCRIPT**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- RECREATE TABLE WITH PROPER COLUMN ORDER
-- ============================================================

USE DW_Reference;
GO

-- =====================================================
-- STEP 1: Backup existing data (if any)
-- =====================================================

IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#Procedure_NDC_Xwalk_Backup') IS NOT NULL 
    DROP TABLE #Procedure_NDC_Xwalk_Backup;

IF OBJECT_ID('dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk') IS NOT NULL
BEGIN
    PRINT 'Backing up existing data...';
    
    SELECT * 
    INTO #Procedure_NDC_Xwalk_Backup
    FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk;
    
    DECLARE @BackupCount INT = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM #Procedure_NDC_Xwalk_Backup);
    PRINT 'Backed up ' + CAST(@BackupCount AS VARCHAR) + ' existing records';
END

-- =====================================================
-- STEP 2: Drop existing table
-- =====================================================

IF OBJECT_ID('dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk') IS NOT NULL
BEGIN
    PRINT 'Dropping existing table...';
    DROP TABLE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk;
    PRINT 'Table dropped successfully';
END

-- =====================================================
-- STEP 3: Create new table with proper column order
-- =====================================================

PRINT 'Creating new table with proper column order...';

CREATE TABLE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (
    -- Primary Key
    Xwalk_ID INT IDENTITY(1,1) NOT NULL,
    
    -- Core identification fields
    NDC VARCHAR(11) NOT NULL,
    DRUG_NAME VARCHAR(255) NULL,
    Procedure_Code VARCHAR(5) NOT NULL,
    RC_DESC VARCHAR(512) NULL,
    
    -- NOC Indicator
    NOC_Indicator CHAR(1) NULL,
    
    -- Date fields (key for matching)
    Effective_Date DATE NOT NULL,
    Ineffective_Date DATE NULL,
    
    -- Billing unit fields
    BILL_UNITS DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
    BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
    PKG_SIZE DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
    PKG_QTY INT NULL,
    
    -- Unit of measure fields
    UOM VARCHAR(10) NULL,
    PKG_UOM VARCHAR(20) NULL,
    
    -- Audit fields
    User_ID VARCHAR(128) NULL,
    File_Date DATE NULL,
    DLU DATETIME2 NOT NULL DEFAULT GETDATE(),
    
    -- Primary Key constraint
    CONSTRAINT PK_Procedure_NDC_Xwalk PRIMARY KEY CLUSTERED (Xwalk_ID)
);

PRINT 'Table created successfully';

-- =====================================================
-- STEP 4: Create indexes for performance
-- =====================================================

PRINT 'Creating indexes...';

-- Index on NDC-Procedure-Effective (most common lookup)
CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_Procedure_NDC_Xwalk_Key 
ON dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date)
INCLUDE (Ineffective_Date, NOC_Indicator);

-- Index on Procedure Code (for claims lookups)
CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_Procedure_NDC_Xwalk_ProcCode 
ON dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (Procedure_Code, Effective_Date)
INCLUDE (NDC, Ineffective_Date);

-- Index on effective dates (for date range queries)
CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_Procedure_NDC_Xwalk_Dates 
ON dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date)
INCLUDE (NDC, Procedure_Code);

-- Index on File_Date (for tracking which file updated records)
CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_Procedure_NDC_Xwalk_FileDate 
ON dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (File_Date)
INCLUDE (NDC, Procedure_Code, DLU);

PRINT 'Indexes created successfully';

-- =====================================================
-- STEP 5: Restore data if backup exists (optional)
-- =====================================================

IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#Procedure_NDC_Xwalk_Backup') IS NOT NULL
BEGIN
    PRINT 'Restoring backed up data...';
    
    -- Note: This will only work if old table had same columns
    -- Since we're adding new columns, this step is optional
    -- You can skip this and reprocess from source files instead
    
    /*
    INSERT INTO dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (
        NDC, Procedure_Code, NOC_Indicator, 
        Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
        User_ID, DLU
    )
    SELECT 
        NDC, Procedure_Code, NOC_Indicator,
        Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
        User_ID, DLU
    FROM #Procedure_NDC_Xwalk_Backup;
    
    PRINT 'Data restored successfully';
    */
    
    PRINT 'NOTE: Old data backed up but not restored (new columns added)';
    PRINT 'Recommend reprocessing from source files to populate all fields';
END

-- =====================================================
-- STEP 6: Verify new structure
-- =====================================================

PRINT '';
PRINT '============================================================';
PRINT 'NEW TABLE STRUCTURE:';
PRINT '============================================================';

SELECT 
    ORDINAL_POSITION AS Col_Order,
    COLUMN_NAME,
    DATA_TYPE,
    CASE 
        WHEN CHARACTER_MAXIMUM_LENGTH IS NOT NULL 
        THEN DATA_TYPE + '(' + CAST(CHARACTER_MAXIMUM_LENGTH AS VARCHAR) + ')'
        WHEN NUMERIC_PRECISION IS NOT NULL
        THEN DATA_TYPE + '(' + CAST(NUMERIC_PRECISION AS VARCHAR) + ',' + CAST(NUMERIC_SCALE AS VARCHAR) + ')'
        ELSE DATA_TYPE
    END AS Full_Data_Type,
    IS_NULLABLE,
    COLUMN_DEFAULT
FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS
WHERE TABLE_NAME = 'Procedure_NDC_Xwalk'
ORDER BY ORDINAL_POSITION;

PRINT '';
PRINT '============================================================';
PRINT 'TABLE RECREATION COMPLETE';
PRINT 'Ready for data processing!';
PRINT '============================================================';

GO
```

1. ✅ **Backs up existing data** (into temp table)
2. ✅ **Drops old table**
3. ✅ **Creates new table** with proper column order
4. ✅ **Creates performance indexes** for claims lookups
5. ✅ **Shows final structure** in correct order
6. ✅ **Ready for reprocessing** with all new fields

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- REPROCESS ALL MONTHS WITH NEW TABLE STRUCTURE
-- ============================================================

USE DW_Reference;
GO

-- Clean log table

PRINT 'Log table cleaned';
PRINT '';

-- Process October
PRINT '--- PROCESSING OCTOBER ---';
EXEC dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk @FileDate = '2025-10-01';
PRINT '';

-- Process November
PRINT '--- PROCESSING NOVEMBER ---';
EXEC dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk @FileDate = '2025-11-01';
PRINT '';

-- Process December
PRINT '--- PROCESSING DECEMBER ---';
EXEC dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk @FileDate = '2025-12-01';
PRINT '';

-- Verify all fields populated
PRINT '============================================================';
PRINT 'VERIFICATION: Check if all fields populated correctly';
PRINT '============================================================';

SELECT TOP 5
    Xwalk_ID,
    NDC,
    DRUG_NAME,
    Procedure_Code,
    RC_DESC,
    NOC_Indicator,
    Effective_Date,
    Ineffective_Date,
    BILL_UNITS,
    BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG,
    PKG_SIZE,
    PKG_QTY,
    UOM,
    PKG_UOM,
    User_ID,
    File_Date,
    DLU
FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
ORDER BY DLU DESC;

-- Count records with populated fields
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS Total_Records,
    COUNT(DRUG_NAME) AS Has_Drug_Name,
    COUNT(RC_DESC) AS Has_RC_Desc,
    COUNT(BILL_UNITS) AS Has_Bill_Units,
    COUNT(File_Date) AS Has_File_Date,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN DRUG_NAME IS NOT NULL THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS Pct_With_Drug_Name
FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk;

PRINT '';
PRINT '============================================================';
PRINT 'REPROCESSING COMPLETE WITH ALL FIELDS!';
PRINT '============================================================';
```

## **📋 RECOMMENDED LOG TABLE STRUCTURE**

Based on best practices, here's what the log table should have:

```
NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log:
- Log_ID (PK, Identity)                  ← Unique log entry ID
- File_Date                               ← Which file was processed
- Process_Date                            ← When processing occurred
- Record_Key                              ← NDC-Proc-EffDate for reference
- NDC                                     ← For filtering/reporting
- Procedure_Code                          ← For filtering/reporting
- Effective_Date                          ← For filtering/reporting
- Log_Type                                ← INFO, WARNING, ERROR
- Error_Code                              ← Reject 01, Reject 06, etc.
- Message                                 ← Detailed error message
- Action_Taken                            ← INSERTED, UPDATED, REJECTED
- Created_Date                            ← Timestamp of log entry
```

---

## **✅ IF YOUR CURRENT LOG TABLE MATCHES THIS, YOU'RE GOOD!**

**However, I recommend adding one enhancement:**

### **Add `Duration_Seconds` column for performance tracking**

This would track how long each file took to process.

---

## **🔧 ENHANCED LOG TABLE (OPTIONAL BUT RECOMMENDED)**

If you want to recreate the log table with better structure:

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- RECREATE LOG TABLE WITH ENHANCEMENTS (OPTIONAL)
-- ============================================================

USE DW_Reference;
GO

-- =====================================================
-- STEP 1: Drop existing log table
-- =====================================================

IF OBJECT_ID('dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log') IS NOT NULL
BEGIN
    PRINT 'Dropping existing log table...';
    DROP TABLE dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log;
    PRINT 'Log table dropped successfully';
END

-- =====================================================
-- STEP 2: Create new log table with enhancements
-- =====================================================

PRINT 'Creating enhanced log table...';

CREATE TABLE dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
    -- Primary Key
    Log_ID INT IDENTITY(1,1) NOT NULL,
    
    -- File processing info
    File_Date DATE NOT NULL,
    Process_Date DATETIME2 NULL,
    
    -- Record identification
    Record_Key VARCHAR(100) NULL,
    NDC VARCHAR(11) NULL,
    Procedure_Code VARCHAR(5) NULL,
    Effective_Date DATE NULL,
    
    -- Log details
    Log_Type VARCHAR(20) NULL,              -- INFO, WARNING, ERROR
    Error_Code VARCHAR(50) NULL,            -- Reject 01, Reject 06, etc.
    Message NVARCHAR(1000) NULL,            -- Detailed message
    Action_Taken VARCHAR(20) NULL,          -- INSERTED, UPDATED, REJECTED
    
    -- Audit fields
    Created_Date DATETIME2 NOT NULL DEFAULT GETDATE(),
    
    -- Primary Key constraint
    CONSTRAINT PK_NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log PRIMARY KEY CLUSTERED (Log_ID)
);

PRINT 'Log table created successfully';

-- =====================================================
-- STEP 3: Create indexes for querying/reporting
-- =====================================================

PRINT 'Creating log table indexes...';

-- Index on File_Date (most common filter)
CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log_FileDate 
ON dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Log_Type)
INCLUDE (Error_Code, Action_Taken);

-- Index on NDC-Proc for tracking specific records
CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log_NDC_Proc 
ON dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (NDC, Procedure_Code, File_Date)
INCLUDE (Log_Type, Error_Code, Message);

-- Index on Error_Code for error analysis
CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log_ErrorCode 
ON dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (Error_Code, File_Date)
INCLUDE (NDC, Procedure_Code, Message);

-- Index on Action_Taken for summary reports
CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log_Action 
ON dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (Action_Taken, File_Date)
INCLUDE (Log_Type, Error_Code);

PRINT 'Log table indexes created successfully';

-- =====================================================
-- STEP 4: Verify structure
-- =====================================================

PRINT '';
PRINT '============================================================';
PRINT 'NEW LOG TABLE STRUCTURE:';
PRINT '============================================================';

SELECT 
    ORDINAL_POSITION AS Col_Order,
    COLUMN_NAME,
    DATA_TYPE,
    CASE 
        WHEN CHARACTER_MAXIMUM_LENGTH IS NOT NULL 
        THEN DATA_TYPE + '(' + CAST(CHARACTER_MAXIMUM_LENGTH AS VARCHAR) + ')'
        WHEN NUMERIC_PRECISION IS NOT NULL
        THEN DATA_TYPE + '(' + CAST(NUMERIC_PRECISION AS VARCHAR) + ',' + CAST(NUMERIC_SCALE AS VARCHAR) + ')'
        ELSE DATA_TYPE
    END AS Full_Data_Type,
    IS_NULLABLE,
    COLUMN_DEFAULT
FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS
WHERE TABLE_NAME = 'NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log'
ORDER BY ORDINAL_POSITION;

PRINT '';
PRINT '============================================================';
PRINT 'LOG TABLE RECREATION COMPLETE';
PRINT '============================================================';

GO
```

## **📊 OPTIONAL: ADD SUMMARY LOG TABLE**

For tracking overall file processing statistics, you could also create a summary table:

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- CREATE SUMMARY LOG TABLE (OPTIONAL)
-- ============================================================

USE DW_Reference;
GO

IF OBJECT_ID('dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Summary') IS NOT NULL
    DROP TABLE dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Summary;

CREATE TABLE dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Summary (
    Summary_ID INT IDENTITY(1,1) NOT NULL,
    File_Date DATE NOT NULL,
    Process_Start_Date DATETIME2 NULL,
    Process_End_Date DATETIME2 NULL,
    Duration_Seconds INT NULL,
    
    -- Counts
    Total_Records_In_File INT NULL,
    Excluded_Pre2014 INT NULL,
    Records_Processed INT NULL,
    Inserted_Count INT NULL,
    Updated_Count INT NULL,
    Rejected_Count INT NULL,
    Warning_Count INT NULL,
    
    -- Success rate
    Success_Rate DECIMAL(5,2) NULL,
    
    -- Audit
    Created_Date DATETIME2 NOT NULL DEFAULT GETDATE(),
    Created_By VARCHAR(128) NULL,
    
    CONSTRAINT PK_NDC_Xwalk_Process_Summary PRIMARY KEY CLUSTERED (Summary_ID)
);

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IX_NDC_Xwalk_Process_Summary_FileDate 
ON dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Summary (File_Date);

PRINT 'Summary log table created successfully';
GO
```

Then add this at the end of your stored procedure:

```sql
-- Insert summary record
INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Summary (
    File_Date,
    Process_Start_Date,
    Process_End_Date,
    Duration_Seconds,
    Total_Records_In_File,
    Excluded_Pre2014,
    Records_Processed,
    Inserted_Count,
    Updated_Count,
    Rejected_Count,
    Warning_Count,
    Success_Rate,
    Created_By
)
VALUES (
    @FileDate,
    @StartTime,
    GETDATE(),
    DATEDIFF(SECOND, @StartTime, GETDATE()),
    @TotalRecords,
    @ExcludedRecords,
    @TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords,
    @InsertedRecords,
    @UpdatedRecords,
    @RejectedRecords,
    @WarningRecords,
    CASE 
        WHEN (@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords) > 0 
        THEN CAST((@InsertedRecords + @UpdatedRecords) * 100.0 / (@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords) AS DECIMAL(5,2))
        ELSE 0 
    END,
    SYSTEM_USER
);
```

---

## **🎯 MY RECOMMENDATION**

### **Minimum (Required):**
✅ Keep your current log table structure (it's probably fine)
✅ Just make sure it has all the columns the stored procedure expects

### **Recommended (Better):**
✅ Recreate log table with indexes (for faster querying)
✅ This improves performance when analyzing errors

### **Optional (Best Practice):**
✅ Add summary log table
✅ Tracks high-level statistics for each file processed
✅ Great for dashboards and reporting

**If your current log table already has these columns:**
```
Log_ID, File_Date, Process_Date, Record_Key, NDC, Procedure_Code, 
Effective_Date, Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken, Created_Date
```

**Then:** ✅ **You're good! No changes needed.**

**If it's missing any columns or you want indexes:** Run the enhanced log table script above.

## **🔧 SCRIPT 1: CREATE PRODUCTION TABLE**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- CREATE PROCEDURE_NDC_XWALK TABLE (FRESH)
-- ============================================================

USE DW_Reference;
GO

-- Drop if exists
IF OBJECT_ID('dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk') IS NOT NULL
    DROP TABLE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk;
GO

PRINT 'Creating Procedure_NDC_Xwalk table...';

CREATE TABLE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (
    -- Primary Key
    Xwalk_ID INT IDENTITY(1,1) NOT NULL,
    
    -- Core identification fields
    NDC VARCHAR(11) NOT NULL,
    DRUG_NAME VARCHAR(255) NULL,
    Procedure_Code VARCHAR(5) NOT NULL,
    RC_DESC VARCHAR(512) NULL,
    
    -- NOC Indicator
    NOC_Indicator CHAR(1) NULL,
    
    -- Date fields (key for matching)
    Effective_Date DATE NOT NULL,
    Ineffective_Date DATE NULL,
    
    -- Billing unit fields
    BILL_UNITS DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
    BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
    PKG_SIZE DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
    PKG_QTY INT NULL,
    
    -- Unit of measure fields
    UOM VARCHAR(10) NULL,
    PKG_UOM VARCHAR(20) NULL,
    
    -- Audit fields
    User_ID VARCHAR(128) NULL,
    File_Date DATE NULL,
    DLU DATETIME2 NOT NULL DEFAULT GETDATE(),
    
    -- Primary Key constraint
    CONSTRAINT PK_Procedure_NDC_Xwalk PRIMARY KEY CLUSTERED (Xwalk_ID)
);

PRINT 'Procedure_NDC_Xwalk table created';

-- Create performance indexes
PRINT 'Creating indexes...';

-- Index on NDC-Procedure-Effective (most common lookup)
CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_Procedure_NDC_Xwalk_Key 
ON dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date)
INCLUDE (Ineffective_Date, NOC_Indicator, DRUG_NAME);

-- Index on Procedure Code (for claims lookups)
CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_Procedure_NDC_Xwalk_ProcCode 
ON dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (Procedure_Code, Effective_Date)
INCLUDE (NDC, Ineffective_Date, BILL_UNITS);

-- Index on effective dates (for date range queries)
CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_Procedure_NDC_Xwalk_Dates 
ON dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date)
INCLUDE (NDC, Procedure_Code);

-- Index on File_Date (for tracking which file updated records)
CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_Procedure_NDC_Xwalk_FileDate 
ON dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (File_Date)
INCLUDE (NDC, Procedure_Code, DLU);

PRINT 'Indexes created successfully';
PRINT 'Procedure_NDC_Xwalk table ready!';
PRINT '';

GO
```

## **🔧 SCRIPT 2: CREATE LOG TABLE**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- CREATE NDC_XWALK_PROCESS_LOG TABLE (FRESH)
-- ============================================================

USE DW_Reference;
GO

-- Drop if exists
IF OBJECT_ID('dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log') IS NOT NULL
    DROP TABLE dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log;
GO

PRINT 'Creating NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log table...';

CREATE TABLE dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
    -- Primary Key
    Log_ID INT IDENTITY(1,1) NOT NULL,
    
    -- File processing info
    File_Date DATE NOT NULL,
    Process_Date DATETIME2 NULL,
    
    -- Record identification
    Record_Key VARCHAR(100) NULL,
    NDC VARCHAR(11) NULL,
    Procedure_Code VARCHAR(5) NULL,
    Effective_Date DATE NULL,
    
    -- Log details
    Log_Type VARCHAR(20) NULL,              -- INFO, WARNING, ERROR
    Error_Code VARCHAR(50) NULL,            -- Reject 01, Reject 06, etc.
    Message NVARCHAR(1000) NULL,            -- Detailed message
    Action_Taken VARCHAR(20) NULL,          -- INSERTED, UPDATED, REJECTED
    
    -- Audit fields
    Created_Date DATETIME2 NOT NULL DEFAULT GETDATE(),
    
    -- Primary Key constraint
    CONSTRAINT PK_NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log PRIMARY KEY CLUSTERED (Log_ID)
);

PRINT 'NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log table created';

-- Create indexes for querying/reporting
PRINT 'Creating log table indexes...';

-- Index on File_Date (most common filter)
CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log_FileDate 
ON dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (File_Date, Log_Type)
INCLUDE (Error_Code, Action_Taken);

-- Index on NDC-Proc for tracking specific records
CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log_NDC_Proc 
ON dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (NDC, Procedure_Code, File_Date)
INCLUDE (Log_Type, Error_Code, Message);

-- Index on Error_Code for error analysis
CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log_ErrorCode 
ON dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (Error_Code, File_Date)
INCLUDE (NDC, Procedure_Code, Message);

-- Index on Action_Taken for summary reports
CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log_Action 
ON dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (Action_Taken, File_Date)
INCLUDE (Log_Type, Error_Code);

PRINT 'Log table indexes created successfully';
PRINT 'NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log table ready!';
PRINT '';

GO
```

---

## **🔧 SCRIPT 3: CREATE STORED PROCEDURE (COMPLETE)**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- CREATE STORED PROCEDURE (COMPLETE WITH ALL FIELDS)
-- ============================================================

USE DW_Reference;
GO

IF OBJECT_ID('dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk', 'P') IS NOT NULL
    DROP PROCEDURE dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk;
GO

CREATE PROCEDURE dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk
    @FileDate DATE
AS
BEGIN
    SET NOCOUNT ON;
    
    DECLARE @StartTime DATETIME2 = GETDATE();
    DECLARE @TotalRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @ExcludedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @InsertedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @UpdatedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @RejectedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @WarningRecords INT = 0;
    
    BEGIN TRY
        
        PRINT '============================================================';
        PRINT 'NDC CROSSWALK PROCESSING';
        PRINT 'File Date: ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @FileDate, 23);
        PRINT 'Start Time: ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @StartTime, 120);
        PRINT '============================================================';
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 1: Count total records
        -- =====================================================
        
        SELECT @TotalRecords = COUNT(*)
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate;
        
        PRINT 'Total records in file: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        -- Count excluded records (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01)
        SELECT @ExcludedRecords = COUNT(*)
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate
            AND RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL
            AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> ''
            AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < '2014-01-01';
        
        PRINT 'Excluded records (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01): ' + CAST(@ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Records to process: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        IF (@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords) = 0
        BEGIN
            PRINT 'WARNING: No records to process after exclusions';
            RETURN;
        END
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 2: Create temp working table with ALL SOURCE FIELDS
        -- =====================================================
        
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#WorkingData') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #WorkingData;
        
        SELECT 
            -- Core identification fields
            LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) AS NDC,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(DRUG_NAME)) AS DRUG_NAME,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) AS Procedure_Code,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(RC_DESC)) AS RC_DESC,
            
            -- NOC Indicator (case-insensitive)
            CASE 
                WHEN UPPER(LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC))) = 'Y' THEN 'Y' 
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS NOC_Indicator,
            
            -- Date fields
            TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) AS Effective_Date,
            CASE 
                WHEN RMV_DATE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) = '' THEN NULL
                ELSE TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101)
            END AS Ineffective_Date,
            
            -- Billing unit fields
            CASE 
                WHEN BILL_UNITS IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(BILL_UNITS) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(BILL_UNITS AS DECIMAL(12,6))
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS BILL_UNITS,
            
            CASE 
                WHEN BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG AS DECIMAL(12,6))
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG,
            
            CASE 
                WHEN PKG_SIZE IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(PKG_SIZE) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(PKG_SIZE AS DECIMAL(12,6))
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS PKG_SIZE,
            
            CASE 
                WHEN PKG_QTY IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(PKG_QTY) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(PKG_QTY AS INT)
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS PKG_QTY,
            
            -- Unit of measure fields
            LTRIM(RTRIM(UOM)) AS UOM,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(PKG_UOM)) AS PKG_UOM,
            
            -- File date
            File_Date,
            
            -- Validation flags
            CAST(CASE 
                -- Reject 01: NDC validations
                WHEN NDC IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC MISSING'
                WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC))) <> 11 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (length)'
                WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) LIKE '%[^0-9]%' 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (non-numeric)'
                
                -- Reject 02: Procedure Code validations
                WHEN REIMB_CODE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE MISSING'
                WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE))) <> 5 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (length)'
                WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) LIKE '%[^A-Z0-9]%' 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (alphanumeric)'
                
                -- Reject 03: NOC Indicator validation
                WHEN IS_NOC IS NOT NULL 
                    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC)) <> '' 
                    AND UPPER(LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC))) <> 'Y' 
                    THEN 'Reject 03: NOC INDICATOR UNKNOWN VALUE'
                
                -- Reject 04: Effective Date validations
                WHEN ADD_DATE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(ADD_DATE)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE MISSING'
                WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
                    THEN 'Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT'
                
                -- Reject 05: Ineffective Date validation
                WHEN RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL 
                    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> '' 
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
                    THEN 'Reject 05: INEFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT'
                
                -- Reject 06: Ineffective before Effective
                WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NOT NULL 
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) IS NOT NULL
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)
                    THEN 'Reject 06: INEFFECTIVE DATE BEFORE EFFECTIVE DATE'
                
                ELSE NULL
            END AS NVARCHAR(500)) AS Validation_Error,
            
            0 AS Is_Processed
            
        INTO #WorkingData
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate
            -- Exclusion filter: Exclude RMV_DATE < 2014

## Copilot

**Perfect! Your existing tables are actually excellent. Let me simplify everything.**

---

## **✅ ANSWERS TO YOUR QUESTIONS**

### **Q1: Do we need all those indexes now?**

**Answer: NO - Start without them!**

**Why:**
- ✅ Your table already has a **UNIQUE constraint** on `(NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date)` which creates an index automatically
- ✅ The **PRIMARY KEY** on `Xwalk_ID` is already indexed
- ✅ Additional indexes can **slow down inserts/updates** during initial data load
- ✅ You can add indexes **AFTER** data is loaded if queries are slow

**Recommendation:** 
- Start with **NO additional indexes**
- Monitor query performance
- Add indexes only if needed for specific slow queries

---

### **Q2: Can we use your existing table structure?**

**Answer: YES! Just add the new columns to what you have.**

---

## **🔧 SIMPLE ALTER TABLE SCRIPT (Add New Columns Only)**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- ALTER EXISTING TABLE: Add new columns only
-- ============================================================

USE DW_Reference;
GO

-- Add new columns to existing Procedure_NDC_Xwalk table
ALTER TABLE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
ADD 
    DRUG_NAME VARCHAR(255) NULL,
    RC_DESC VARCHAR(512) NULL,
    BILL_UNITS DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
    BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
    PKG_SIZE DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
    PKG_QTY INT NULL,
    UOM VARCHAR(10) NULL,
    PKG_UOM VARCHAR(20) NULL,
    File_Date DATE NULL;

PRINT 'New columns added to Procedure_NDC_Xwalk table';
GO

-- Verify structure
SELECT 
    ORDINAL_POSITION AS Col_Order,
    COLUMN_NAME,
    DATA_TYPE,
    IS_NULLABLE
FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.COLUMNS
WHERE TABLE_NAME = 'Procedure_NDC_Xwalk'
ORDER BY ORDINAL_POSITION;
GO
```
