## **🔧 CORRECTED SCRIPTS (EXACTLY AS YOU REQUESTED)**

### **SCRIPT 1: LOG TABLE - SIMPLIFIED (Remove Process_Date column entirely, rename Created_Date to Process_Date, use DATETIME)**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- OPTIMIZED LOG TABLE (Your specifications)
-- ============================================================

USE DW_Reference;
GO

IF OBJECT_ID('dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log') IS NOT NULL
BEGIN
    PRINT 'Dropping existing NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log table...';
    DROP TABLE dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log;
END
GO

PRINT 'Creating optimized NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log table...';

CREATE TABLE dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
    -- Primary Key
    Log_ID INT IDENTITY(1,1) NOT NULL,
    
    -- File info
    File_Date DATE NOT NULL,
    
    -- Record identification (NO Record_Key column)
    NDC VARCHAR(11) NULL,
    Procedure_Code VARCHAR(5) NULL,
    Effective_Date DATE NULL,
    Ineffective_Date DATE NULL,
    
    -- Log details
    Log_Type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    Error_Code VARCHAR(50) NULL,
    Message NVARCHAR(1000) NULL,
    Action_Taken VARCHAR(50) NULL,
    
    -- Single timestamp column (renamed from Created_Date, using DATETIME)
    Process_Date DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (GETDATE()),
    
    -- Constraints
    CONSTRAINT PK_NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log PRIMARY KEY CLUSTERED (Log_ID)
);

PRINT 'Optimized log table created!';
GO
```

---

### **SCRIPT 2: MAIN TABLE - LOGICAL COLUMN ORDER (NDC → DRUG_NAME → Procedure_Code → RC_DESC)**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- OPTIMIZED MAIN TABLE (Your specifications)
-- ============================================================

USE DW_Reference;
GO

IF OBJECT_ID('dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk') IS NOT NULL
BEGIN
    PRINT 'Dropping existing Procedure_NDC_Xwalk table...';
    DROP TABLE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk;
END
GO

PRINT 'Creating optimized Procedure_NDC_Xwalk table...';

CREATE TABLE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (
    -- Primary Key
    Xwalk_ID INT IDENTITY(1,1) NOT NULL,
    
    -- Drug information FIRST (NDC then DRUG_NAME)
    NDC VARCHAR(11) NOT NULL,
    DRUG_NAME VARCHAR(255) NULL,
    
    -- Procedure information AFTER drug info (Procedure_Code then RC_DESC)
    Procedure_Code VARCHAR(5) NOT NULL,
    RC_DESC VARCHAR(512) NULL,
    NOC_Indicator CHAR(1) NULL,
    
    -- Date fields
    Effective_Date DATE NOT NULL,
    Ineffective_Date DATE NULL,
    
    -- Billing/Package information
    BILL_UNITS DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
    BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
    PKG_SIZE DECIMAL(12,6) NULL,
    PKG_QTY INT NULL,
    UOM VARCHAR(10) NULL,
    PKG_UOM VARCHAR(20) NULL,
    
    -- Audit fields (using DATETIME instead of DATETIME2)
    User_ID VARCHAR(100) NULL DEFAULT (SUSER_SNAME()),
    File_Date DATE NULL,
    DLU DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (GETDATE()),
    
    -- Constraints
    CONSTRAINT PK_Procedure_NDC_Xwalk PRIMARY KEY CLUSTERED (Xwalk_ID),
    CONSTRAINT UQ_NDC_Proc_EffDate UNIQUE NONCLUSTERED (NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date),
    CONSTRAINT CK_NOC CHECK (NOC_Indicator IS NULL OR NOC_Indicator = 'Y')
);

PRINT 'Optimized main table created!';
GO
```

---

### **SCRIPT 3: UPDATED STORED PROCEDURE (Matches new table structure)**

```sql
-- ============================================================
-- UPDATED STORED PROCEDURE (Optimized for new structure)
-- ============================================================

USE DW_Reference;
GO

IF OBJECT_ID('dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk', 'P') IS NOT NULL
    DROP PROCEDURE dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk;
GO

CREATE PROCEDURE dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk
    @FileDate DATE
AS
BEGIN
    SET NOCOUNT ON;
    
    DECLARE @StartTime DATETIME = GETDATE();
    DECLARE @TotalRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @ExcludedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @InsertedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @UpdatedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @RejectedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @WarningRecords INT = 0;
    
    BEGIN TRY
        
        PRINT '============================================================';
        PRINT 'NDC CROSSWALK PROCESSING';
        PRINT 'File Date: ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @FileDate, 23);
        PRINT 'Start Time: ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @StartTime, 120);
        PRINT '============================================================';
        
        SELECT @TotalRecords = COUNT(*)
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate;
        
        PRINT 'Total records in file: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        SELECT @ExcludedRecords = COUNT(*)
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate
            AND RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL
            AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> ''
            AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < '2014-01-01';
        
        PRINT 'Excluded records (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01): ' + CAST(@ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Records to process: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        IF (@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords) = 0
        BEGIN
            PRINT 'WARNING: No records to process after exclusions';
            RETURN;
        END
        
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#WorkingData') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #WorkingData;
        
        SELECT 
            LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) AS NDC,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(DRUG_NAME)) AS DRUG_NAME,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) AS Procedure_Code,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(RC_DESC)) AS RC_DESC,
            CASE 
                WHEN UPPER(LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC))) = 'Y' THEN 'Y' 
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS NOC_Indicator,
            TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) AS Effective_Date,
            CASE 
                WHEN RMV_DATE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) = '' THEN NULL
                ELSE TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101)
            END AS Ineffective_Date,
            CASE 
                WHEN BILL_UNITS IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(BILL_UNITS) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(BILL_UNITS AS DECIMAL(12,6))
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS BILL_UNITS,
            CASE 
                WHEN BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG AS DECIMAL(12,6))
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG,
            CASE 
                WHEN PKG_SIZE IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(PKG_SIZE) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(PKG_SIZE AS DECIMAL(12,6))
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS PKG_SIZE,
            CASE 
                WHEN PKG_QTY IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(PKG_QTY) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(PKG_QTY AS INT)
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS PKG_QTY,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(UOM)) AS UOM,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(PKG_UOM)) AS PKG_UOM,
            File_Date,
            CAST(CASE 
                WHEN NDC IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC MISSING'
                WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC))) <> 11 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (length)'
                WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) LIKE '%[^0-9]%' 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (non-numeric)'
                WHEN REIMB_CODE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE MISSING'
                WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE))) <> 5 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (length)'
                WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) LIKE '%[^A-Z0-9]%' 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (alphanumeric)'
                WHEN IS_NOC IS NOT NULL 
                    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC)) <> '' 
                    AND UPPER(LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC))) <> 'Y' 
                    THEN 'Reject 03: NOC INDICATOR UNKNOWN VALUE'
                WHEN ADD_DATE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(ADD_DATE)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE MISSING'
                WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
                    THEN 'Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT'
                WHEN RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL 
                    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> '' 
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
                    THEN 'Reject 05: INEFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT'
                WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NOT NULL 
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) IS NOT NULL
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)
                    THEN 'Reject 06: INEFFECTIVE DATE BEFORE EFFECTIVE DATE'
                ELSE NULL
            END AS NVARCHAR(500)) AS Validation_Error,
            0 AS Is_Processed
        INTO #WorkingData
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate
            AND (
                RMV_DATE IS NULL 
                OR LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) = ''
                OR TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NULL
                OR TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) >= '2014-01-01'
            );
        
        DECLARE @WorkingCount INT = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM #WorkingData);
        PRINT 'Working data loaded: ' + CAST(@WorkingCount AS VARCHAR) + ' records';
        
        -- Log field validation errors (NO Record_Key column)
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
            File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
            Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
        )
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            Ineffective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            LEFT(Validation_Error, CHARINDEX(':', Validation_Error) - 1),
            Validation_Error,
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NOT NULL;
        
        SET @RejectedRecords = @@ROWCOUNT;
        PRINT 'Field validation errors: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
            File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
            Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
        )
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            Ineffective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'Reject 11',
            'DUPLICATE RECORDS: Key appears ' + CAST(COUNT(*) AS VARCHAR) + ' times',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
        GROUP BY NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date
        HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
        
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'Reject 11: DUPLICATE RECORDS'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM #WorkingData w2
                WHERE w2.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND w2.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND w2.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
                    AND w2.Validation_Error IS NULL
                GROUP BY w2.NDC, w2.Procedure_Code, w2.Effective_Date
                HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
            );
        
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#ProjectedProduction') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #ProjectedProduction;
        
        SELECT NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date
        INTO #ProjectedProduction
        FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk;
        
        UPDATE proj
        SET proj.Ineffective_Date = w.Ineffective_Date
        FROM #ProjectedProduction proj
        INNER JOIN #WorkingData w ON
            proj.NDC = w.NDC
            AND proj.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
            AND proj.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL;
        
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
            File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
            Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
        )
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            w.NDC,
            w.Procedure_Code,
            w.Effective_Date,
            w.Ineffective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'Reject 13/14',
            'OVERLAP: New (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Effective_Date, 23) + ' to ' + 
                ISNULL(CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Ineffective_Date, 23), 'open') + ') overlaps existing (' +
                CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Effective_Date, 23) + ' to ' + 
                ISNULL(CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Ineffective_Date, 23), 'open') + ')',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN #ProjectedProduction p ON
            w.NDC = p.NDC
            AND w.Procedure_Code = p.Procedure_Code
            AND w.Effective_Date <> p.Effective_Date
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND (
                (w.Effective_Date >= p.Effective_Date 
                    AND (p.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Effective_Date <= p.Ineffective_Date))
                OR
                (w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL 
                    AND w.Ineffective_Date >= p.Effective_Date
                    AND (p.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date <= p.Ineffective_Date))
                OR
                (w.Effective_Date <= p.Effective_Date
                    AND (w.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date >= p.Effective_Date))
            );
        
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'Reject 13/14: OVERLAP'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log log
                WHERE log.File_Date = @FileDate
                    AND log.Error_Code = 'Reject 13/14'
                    AND log.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND log.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND log.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
            );
        
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#ErrorGroups') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #ErrorGroups;
        
        SELECT DISTINCT NDC, Procedure_Code
        INTO #ErrorGroups
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NOT NULL;
        
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'GROUP_REJECTION'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN #ErrorGroups eg ON w.NDC = eg.NDC AND w.Procedure_Code = eg.Procedure_Code
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL;
        
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
            File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
            Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
        )
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            Ineffective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'GROUP_REJECTION',
            'Rejected due to group rejection rule (Rule 5)',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error = 'GROUP_REJECTION';
        
        DECLARE @GroupRejected INT = @@ROWCOUNT;
        SET @RejectedRecords = @RejectedRecords + @GroupRejected;
        
        PRINT 'Group rejection additional errors: ' + CAST(@GroupRejected AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Total rejected records: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
            File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
            Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
        )
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            w.NDC,
            w.Procedure_Code,
            w.Effective_Date,
            w.Ineffective_Date,
            'WARNING',
            'Warning 15',
            'RETRO RECORD: Ineffective date (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Ineffective_Date, 23) + 
                ') earlier than existing effective date (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Effective_Date, 23) + ')',
            'WILL_INSERT'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk p ON
            w.NDC = p.NDC
            AND w.Procedure_Code = p.Procedure_Code
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL
            AND w.Ineffective_Date < p.Effective_Date
            AND NOT EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk p2
                WHERE p2.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND p2.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND (
                        (w.Effective_Date >= p2.Effective_Date 
                            AND (p2.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Effective_Date <= p2.Ineffective_Date))
                        OR
                        (w.Ineffective_Date >= p2.Effective_Date
                            AND (p2.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date <= p2.Ineffective_Date))
                    )
            );
        
        SET @WarningRecords = @@ROWCOUNT;
        PRINT 'Warnings detected: ' + CAST(@WarningRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        PRINT 'Processing valid records...';
        
        DECLARE @NDC VARCHAR(11), @ProcCode VARCHAR(5), @NOC CHAR(1);
        DECLARE @DrugName VARCHAR(255), @RCDesc VARCHAR(512);
        DECLARE @EffDate DATE, @IneffDate DATE;
        DECLARE @BillUnits DECIMAL(12,6), @BillUnitsPkg DECIMAL(12,6);
        DECLARE @PkgSize DECIMAL(12,6), @PkgQty INT;
        DECLARE @UOM VARCHAR(10), @PkgUOM VARCHAR(20);
        DECLARE @FileDate_Value DATE;
        DECLARE @ExistingIneff DATE;
        
        DECLARE process_cursor CURSOR LOCAL FAST_FORWARD FOR
            SELECT 
                NDC, DRUG_NAME, Procedure_Code, RC_DESC, NOC_Indicator, 
                Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
                BILL_UNITS, BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG, PKG_SIZE, PKG_QTY,
                UOM, PKG_UOM, File_Date
            FROM #WorkingData
            WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            ORDER BY Procedure_Code, NDC, Effective_Date;
        
        OPEN process_cursor;
        FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO 
            @NDC, @DrugName, @ProcCode, @RCDesc, @NOC, 
            @EffDate, @IneffDate,
            @BillUnits, @BillUnitsPkg, @PkgSize, @PkgQty,
            @UOM, @PkgUOM, @FileDate_Value;
        
        WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0
        BEGIN
            
            SET @ExistingIneff = NULL;
            
            SELECT @ExistingIneff = Ineffective_Date
            FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
            WHERE NDC = @NDC
                AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode
                AND Effective_Date = @EffDate;
            
            IF @ExistingIneff IS NOT NULL 
                AND @IneffDate IS NOT NULL 
                AND @IneffDate <> @ExistingIneff
            BEGIN
                INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
                    File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
                    Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
                )
                VALUES (
                    @FileDate, 
                    @NDC, 
                    @ProcCode, 
                    @EffDate, 
                    @IneffDate,
                    'ERROR', 
                    'CLOSED_RECORD',
                    'ATTEMPT TO UPDATE CLOSED RECORD: Existing Ineff=' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @ExistingIneff, 23) + 
                    ', New Ineff=' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @IneffDate, 23),
                    'REJECTED'
                );
                
                SET @RejectedRecords = @RejectedRecords + 1;
                FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO 
                    @NDC, @DrugName, @ProcCode, @RCDesc, @NOC, 
                    @EffDate, @IneffDate,
                    @BillUnits, @BillUnitsPkg, @PkgSize, @PkgQty,
                    @UOM, @PkgUOM, @FileDate_Value;
                CONTINUE;
            END
            
            IF EXISTS (
                SELECT 1 
                FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk 
                WHERE NDC = @NDC 
                    AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode 
                    AND Effective_Date = @EffDate
            )
            BEGIN
                UPDATE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
                SET 
                    DRUG_NAME = @DrugName,
                    RC_DESC = @RCDesc,
                    NOC_Indicator = @NOC,
                    Ineffective_Date = @IneffDate,
                    BILL_UNITS = @BillUnits,
                    BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG = @BillUnitsPkg,
                    PKG_SIZE = @PkgSize,
                    PKG_QTY = @PkgQty,
                    UOM = @UOM,
                    PKG_UOM = @PkgUOM,
                    User_ID = SYSTEM_USER,
                    DLU = GETDATE(),
                    File_Date = @FileDate_Value
                WHERE NDC = @NDC
                    AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode
                    AND Effective_Date = @EffDate;
                
                SET @UpdatedRecords = @UpdatedRecords + 1;
                
                INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
                    File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
                    Log_Type, Message, Action_Taken
                )
                VALUES (@FileDate, @NDC, @ProcCode, @EffDate, @IneffDate, 'INFO', 'Record updated', 'UPDATED');
            END
            ELSE
            BEGIN
                INSERT INTO dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (
                    NDC, DRUG_NAME, Procedure_Code, RC_DESC, NOC_Indicator, 
                    Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
                    BILL_UNITS, BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG, PKG_SIZE, PKG_QTY,
                    UOM, PKG_UOM,
                    User_ID, DLU, File_Date
                )
                VALUES (
                    @NDC, @DrugName, @ProcCode, @RCDesc, @NOC, 
                    @EffDate, @IneffDate,
                    @BillUnits, @BillUnitsPkg, @PkgSize, @PkgQty,
                    @UOM, @PkgUOM,
                    SYSTEM_USER, GETDATE(), @FileDate_Value
                );
                
                SET @InsertedRecords = @InsertedRecords + 1;
                
                INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
                    File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
                    Log_Type, Message, Action_Taken
                )
                VALUES (@FileDate, @NDC, @ProcCode, @EffDate, @IneffDate, 'INFO', 'New record inserted', 'INSERTED');
            END
            
            FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO 
                @NDC, @DrugName, @ProcCode, @RCDesc, @NOC, 
                @EffDate, @IneffDate,
                @BillUnits, @BillUnitsPkg, @PkgSize, @PkgQty,
                @UOM, @PkgUOM, @FileDate_Value;
        END
        
        CLOSE process_cursor;
        DEALLOCATE process_cursor;
        
        PRINT '============================================================';
        PRINT 'PROCESSING COMPLETED';
        PRINT 'Total Records in File: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Excluded (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01): ' + CAST(@ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Processed: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Inserted: ' + CAST(@InsertedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Updated: ' + CAST(@UpdatedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Rejected: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Warnings: ' + CAST(@WarningRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Duration: ' + CAST(DATEDIFF(SECOND, @StartTime, GETDATE()) AS VARCHAR) + ' seconds';
        PRINT '============================================================';
        
        SELECT 
            @FileDate AS File_Date,
            @TotalRecords AS Total_Records_In_File,
            @ExcludedRecords AS Excluded_Pre2014,
            @InsertedRecords AS Inserted,
            @UpdatedRecords AS Updated,
            @RejectedRecords AS Rejected,
            @WarningRecords AS Warnings,
            DATEDIFF(SECOND, @StartTime, GETDATE()) AS Duration_Seconds;
        
        DROP TABLE #WorkingData;
        DROP TABLE #ErrorGroups;
        DROP TABLE #ProjectedProduction;
        
    END TRY
    BEGIN CATCH
        PRINT 'ERROR: ' + ERROR_MESSAGE();
        THROW;
    END CATCH
    
END;
GO

PRINT 'Stored procedure created successfully!';
GO
```

---

## **✅ SUMMARY OF CHANGES (EXACTLY AS YOU REQUESTED)**

### **Log Table:**
1. ✅ **Removed** `Record_Key` column (redundant)
2. ✅ **Removed** duplicate `Process_Date` column at position 3
3. ✅ **Renamed** `Created_Date` → `Process_Date` (now at last position)
4. ✅ **Changed** `DATETIME2` → `DATETIME` (simpler)
5. ✅ **Added** `Ineffective_Date` column

### **Main Table:**
1. ✅ **Column order:** NDC → DRUG_NAME → Procedure_Code → RC_DESC (logical grouping)
2. ✅ **Changed** `DATETIME2` → `DATETIME` for DLU

---

**Now run these 3 scripts in order!** 🚀

## Copilot

**Perfect! You're right to keep NDC and Procedure_Code together - that makes sense for the business key.**

---

## **🔧 CHANGES NEEDED IN STORED PROCEDURE**

Since you changed the column order in the main table to:
```
NDC → Procedure_Code → DRUG_NAME → RC_DESC → ...
```

You **DON'T need to change anything in the stored procedure!**

Here's why:
- ✅ The **INSERT** statement uses **column names explicitly**, not column positions
- ✅ The **UPDATE** statement uses **column names explicitly**, not column positions
- ✅ SQL doesn't care about column order when you use named columns

---

## **✅ VERIFY IT'S CORRECT**

The stored procedure already has this for **INSERT**:
```sql
INSERT INTO dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (
    NDC, DRUG_NAME, Procedure_Code, RC_DESC, NOC_Indicator,  -- Column names!
    Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
    BILL_UNITS, BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG, PKG_SIZE, PKG_QTY,
    UOM, PKG_UOM,
    User_ID, DLU, File_Date
)
VALUES (
    @NDC, @DrugName, @ProcCode, @RCDesc, @NOC, 
    @EffDate, @IneffDate,
    @BillUnits, @BillUnitsPkg, @PkgSize, @PkgQty,
    @UOM, @PkgUOM,
    SYSTEM_USER, GETDATE(), @FileDate_Value
);
```

And for **UPDATE**:
```sql
UPDATE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
SET 
    DRUG_NAME = @DrugName,
    RC_DESC = @RCDesc,
    NOC_Indicator = @NOC,
    Ineffective_Date = @IneffDate,
    ...
WHERE NDC = @NDC
    AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode
    AND Effective_Date = @EffDate;
```

**Both use column names, so the physical order doesn't matter!**

## Copilot

**Excellent question! Log table growth is a critical consideration for production. Let's discuss strategies.**

---

## **📊 CURRENT LOG TABLE GROWTH ANALYSIS**

### **Your Numbers:**
- **Monthly records:** ~200K processed
- **Log entries per month:** ~200K (1 entry per record minimum)
- **Annual growth:** ~2.4 million log entries per year
- **5-year projection:** ~12 million log entries

### **Log Entry Breakdown Per Month:**
```
October Example:
- INSERTED: 180,028 entries
- REJECTED: 184 entries
- Total: 180,212 log entries

November Example:
- INSERTED: 597 entries
- UPDATED: 180,028 entries  ← Idempotent updates add entries!
- REJECTED: 184 entries
- Total: 180,809 log entries

Annual Total: ~2.1 million log entries
```

---

## **🤔 IS THIS A PROBLEM?**

### **Storage Impact:**
```
Estimated row size: ~200 bytes per log entry
Annual storage: 2.4M rows × 200 bytes = ~480 MB/year
5-year storage: ~2.4 GB
```

**Verdict:** ✅ **Storage is NOT a concern** (very small)

### **Performance Impact:**
```
Query performance degrades with large tables:
- 2.4M rows: Fast (< 1 second with proper indexes)
- 12M rows: Still manageable (1-2 seconds)
- 50M+ rows: Slow (> 5 seconds without partitioning)
```

**Verdict:** ⚠️ **Could become a concern after 3-5 years**

---

## **💡 RECOMMENDED STRATEGIES**

### **OPTION 1: ARCHIVE OLD LOGS (Recommended)**

**Strategy:** Keep recent logs (e.g., last 13 months) in main table, archive older logs

**Implementation:**

```sql
-- Create archive table (identical structure)
CREATE TABLE dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log_Archive (
    Log_ID INT IDENTITY(1,1) NOT NULL,
    File_Date DATE NOT NULL,
    NDC VARCHAR(11) NULL,
    Procedure_Code VARCHAR(5) NULL,
    Effective_Date DATE NULL,
    Ineffective_Date DATE NULL,
    Log_Type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
    Error_Code VARCHAR(50) NULL,
    Message NVARCHAR(1000) NULL,
    Action_Taken VARCHAR(50) NULL,
    Process_Date DATETIME NOT NULL,
    Archive_Date DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (GETDATE()),
    CONSTRAINT PK_NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log_Archive PRIMARY KEY CLUSTERED (Log_ID)
);

-- Monthly archive job (run at start of each month)
CREATE PROCEDURE dbo.usp_Archive_NDC_Logs
    @RetentionMonths INT = 13  -- Keep last 13 months
AS
BEGIN
    DECLARE @ArchiveBefore DATE = DATEADD(MONTH, -@RetentionMonths, GETDATE());
    
    -- Move old logs to archive
    INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log_Archive (
        Log_ID, File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
        Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken, Process_Date
    )
    SELECT 
        Log_ID, File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
        Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken, Process_Date
    FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
    WHERE File_Date < @ArchiveBefore;
    
    -- Delete archived logs from main table
    DELETE FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log
    WHERE File_Date < @ArchiveBefore;
    
    PRINT 'Archived logs older than ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @ArchiveBefore, 23);
END;
```

**Pros:**
- ✅ Keeps main table small and fast
- ✅ Historical data preserved for auditing
- ✅ Easy to implement

**Cons:**
- ⚠️ Need to query both tables for full history
- ⚠️ Requires scheduled job

---

### **OPTION 2: LOG ONLY ERRORS/WARNINGS (Most Efficient)**

**Strategy:** Only log rejections, errors, and warnings. Don't log successful inserts/updates.

**Change in stored procedure:**

```sql
-- CURRENT: Log every record
INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (...)
VALUES (..., 'INFO', 'Record updated', 'UPDATED');

-- NEW: Only log errors/warnings
-- Remove all logging for successful operations
-- Keep only:
-- - Field validation errors
-- - Reject 11 (duplicates)
-- - Reject 13/14 (overlaps)
-- - GROUP_REJECTION
-- - Warning 15
-- - CLOSED_RECORD errors
```

**Impact:**
```
Before: 180K log entries per month (all records)
After: ~200 log entries per month (only errors)
Reduction: 99.9%
Annual: ~2,400 log entries (tiny!)
```

**Pros:**
- ✅ **Massive reduction** in log size (99.9%)
- ✅ Focuses on what matters (errors)
- ✅ No archiving needed for years
- ✅ Faster queries (small table)

**Cons:**
- ⚠️ No audit trail for successful operations
- ⚠️ Can't track "which file last updated record" in logs (but you have File_Date in main table!)

---

### **OPTION 3: SUMMARY LOGGING + DETAILED ERROR LOGGING**

**Strategy:** Create two tables:
1. **Summary table:** One row per file processed (high-level stats)
2. **Detail table:** Only errors and warnings

**Implementation:**

```sql
-- Summary table (one row per file)
CREATE TABLE dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Summary (
    Summary_ID INT IDENTITY(1,1) NOT NULL,
    File_Date DATE NOT NULL,
    Process_Date DATETIME NOT NULL,
    Total_Records INT,
    Excluded_Pre2014 INT,
    Inserted_Count INT,
    Updated_Count INT,
    Rejected_Count INT,
    Warning_Count INT,
    Duration_Seconds INT,
    User_ID VARCHAR(100),
    CONSTRAINT PK_NDC_Xwalk_Process_Summary PRIMARY KEY (Summary_ID)
);

-- Detail table (only errors/warnings)
CREATE TABLE dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
    -- Same structure as now, but only stores errors/warnings
    ...
);
```

**Impact:**
```
Summary table: 12 rows/year (one per month)
Detail table: ~200 rows/month = 2,400/year (only errors)
Total: ~2,412 rows/year (vs 2.4 million currently)
```

**Pros:**
- ✅ Best of both worlds
- ✅ High-level stats always available
- ✅ Detailed error tracking
- ✅ Minimal storage

**Cons:**
- ⚠️ Two tables to manage
- ⚠️ More complex implementation

---

## **🎯 MY RECOMMENDATION**

### **For Production: OPTION 2 (Log Only Errors) + Summary Table**

**Why:**
1. ✅ **You already track successful operations** in the main table (`File_Date` column shows which file last updated each record)
2. ✅ **99.9% reduction** in log table size
3. ✅ **Focuses on actionable data** (what failed and why)
4. ✅ **Add summary table** for high-level monitoring
5. ✅ **No maintenance needed** (no archiving jobs)

**What to log:**
- ✅ All validation errors (Reject 01-06, 11, 13/14)
- ✅ All group rejections
- ✅ All warnings
- ✅ Processing summary (in summary table)
- ❌ Successful inserts (tracked in main table via File_Date)
- ❌ Successful updates (tracked in main table via DLU + File_Date)

---

## **📊 COMPARISON TABLE**

| Strategy | Annual Log Rows | Storage (5yr) | Maintenance | Audit Trail |
|----------|----------------|---------------|-------------|-------------|
| **Current** | 2.4M | 2.4 GB | None | Full |
| **Option 1: Archive** | 2.4M total (archive old) | 2.4 GB (split) | Monthly job | Full |
| **Option 2: Errors Only** | 2,400 | 2.4 MB | None | Errors only |
| **Option 3: Summary + Errors** | 2,412 | 2.4 MB | None | Stats + Errors |

---

## **🤔 QUESTIONS FOR YOU**

**Before I provide updated scripts:**

1. **Do you need to track successful operations in logs?**
   - You already have `File_Date` and `DLU` in main table showing when/which file updated each record
   - Is that sufficient for audit purposes?

2. **What's your primary use case for logs?**
   - Troubleshooting errors? → Option 2 or 3
   - Full audit trail (compliance)? → Option 1
   - Performance monitoring? → Option 3

3. **How long do you need to keep logs?**
   - 1 year? → Current approach is fine
   - 5+ years? → Need Option 2 or 3

---

**Tell me your preference and I'll provide the updated stored procedure!** 🎯

For production, I'd recommend **Option 2 (Errors Only) + Summary Table** for simplicity and efficiency, but it's your call based on business requirements.

## **📝 COMPLETE MODIFIED STORED PROCEDURE**

Here's your proc with the 2 sections commented out:

```sql
USE [DW_Reference]
GO

IF OBJECT_ID('dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk', 'P') IS NOT NULL
    DROP PROCEDURE dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk;
GO

CREATE PROCEDURE [dbo].[usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk]
    @FileDate DATE
AS
BEGIN
    SET NOCOUNT ON;
    
    DECLARE @StartTime DATETIME2 = GETDATE();
    DECLARE @TotalRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @ExcludedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @InsertedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @UpdatedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @RejectedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @WarningRecords INT = 0;
    
    BEGIN TRY
        
        PRINT '============================================================';
        PRINT 'NDC CROSSWALK PROCESSING';
        PRINT 'File Date: ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @FileDate, 23);
        PRINT 'Start Time: ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @StartTime, 120);
        PRINT '============================================================';
        
        SELECT @TotalRecords = COUNT(*)
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate;
        
        PRINT 'Total records in file: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        SELECT @ExcludedRecords = COUNT(*)
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate
            AND RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL
            AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> ''
            AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < '2014-01-01';
        
        PRINT 'Excluded records (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01): ' + CAST(@ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Records to process: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        IF (@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords) = 0
        BEGIN
            PRINT 'WARNING: No records to process after exclusions';
            RETURN;
        END
        
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#WorkingData') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #WorkingData;
        
        SELECT 
            LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) AS NDC,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(DRUG_NAME)) AS DRUG_NAME,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) AS Procedure_Code,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(RC_DESC)) AS RC_DESC,
            CASE 
                WHEN UPPER(LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC))) = 'Y' THEN 'Y' 
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS NOC_Indicator,
            TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) AS Effective_Date,
            CASE 
                WHEN RMV_DATE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) = '' THEN NULL
                ELSE TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101)
            END AS Ineffective_Date,
            CASE 
                WHEN BILL_UNITS IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(BILL_UNITS) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(BILL_UNITS AS DECIMAL(12,6))
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS BILL_UNITS,
            CASE 
                WHEN BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG AS DECIMAL(12,6))
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG,
            CASE 
                WHEN PKG_SIZE IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(PKG_SIZE) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(PKG_SIZE AS DECIMAL(12,6))
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS PKG_SIZE,
            CASE 
                WHEN PKG_QTY IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(PKG_QTY) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(PKG_QTY AS INT)
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS PKG_QTY,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(UOM)) AS UOM,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(PKG_UOM)) AS PKG_UOM,
            File_Date,
            CAST(CASE 
                WHEN NDC IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC MISSING'
                WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC))) <> 11 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (length)'
                WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) LIKE '%[^0-9]%' 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (non-numeric)'
                WHEN REIMB_CODE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE MISSING'
                WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE))) <> 5 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (length)'
                WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) LIKE '%[^A-Z0-9]%' 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (alphanumeric)'
                WHEN IS_NOC IS NOT NULL 
                    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC)) <> '' 
                    AND UPPER(LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC))) <> 'Y' 
                    THEN 'Reject 03: NOC INDICATOR UNKNOWN VALUE'
                WHEN ADD_DATE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(ADD_DATE)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE MISSING'
                WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
                    THEN 'Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT'
                WHEN RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL 
                    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> '' 
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
                    THEN 'Reject 05: INEFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT'
                WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NOT NULL 
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) IS NOT NULL
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)
                    THEN 'Reject 06: INEFFECTIVE DATE BEFORE EFFECTIVE DATE'
                ELSE NULL
            END AS NVARCHAR(500)) AS Validation_Error,
            0 AS Is_Processed
        INTO #WorkingData
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate
            AND (
                RMV_DATE IS NULL 
                OR LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) = ''
                OR TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NULL
                OR TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) >= '2014-01-01'
            );
        
        DECLARE @WorkingCount INT = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM #WorkingData);
        PRINT 'Working data loaded: ' + CAST(@WorkingCount AS VARCHAR) + ' records';
        
        -- ✅ KEEP: Log field validation errors
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
            File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
            Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
        )
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            Ineffective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            LEFT(Validation_Error, CHARINDEX(':', Validation_Error) - 1),
            Validation_Error,
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NOT NULL;
        
        SET @RejectedRecords = @@ROWCOUNT;
        PRINT 'Field validation errors: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        -- ✅ KEEP: Log duplicate errors
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
            File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
            Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
        )
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            Ineffective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'Reject 11',
            'DUPLICATE RECORDS: Key appears ' + CAST(COUNT(*) AS VARCHAR) + ' times',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
        GROUP BY NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date
        HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
        
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'Reject 11: DUPLICATE RECORDS'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM #WorkingData w2
                WHERE w2.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND w2.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND w2.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
                    AND w2.Validation_Error IS NULL
                GROUP BY w2.NDC, w2.Procedure_Code, w2.Effective_Date
                HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
            );
        
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#ProjectedProduction') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #ProjectedProduction;
        
        SELECT NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date
        INTO #ProjectedProduction
        FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk;
        
        UPDATE proj
        SET proj.Ineffective_Date = w.Ineffective_Date
        FROM #ProjectedProduction proj
        INNER JOIN #WorkingData w ON
            proj.NDC = w.NDC
            AND proj.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
            AND proj.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL;
        
        -- ✅ KEEP: Log overlap errors
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
            File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
            Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
        )
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            w.NDC,
            w.Procedure_Code,
            w.Effective_Date,
            w.Ineffective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'Reject 13/14',
            'OVERLAP: New (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Effective_Date, 23) + ' to ' + 
                ISNULL(CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Ineffective_Date, 23), 'open') + ') overlaps existing (' +
                CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Effective_Date, 23) + ' to ' + 
                ISNULL(CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Ineffective_Date, 23), 'open') + ')',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN #ProjectedProduction p ON
            w.NDC = p.NDC
            AND w.Procedure_Code = p.Procedure_Code
            AND w.Effective_Date <> p.Effective_Date
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND (
                (w.Effective_Date >= p.Effective_Date 
                    AND (p.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Effective_Date <= p.Ineffective_Date))
                OR
                (w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL 
                    AND w.Ineffective_Date >= p.Effective_Date
                    AND (p.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date <= p.Ineffective_Date))
                OR
                (w.Effective_Date <= p.Effective_Date
                    AND (w.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date >= p.Effective_Date))
            );
        
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'Reject 13/14: OVERLAP'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log log
                WHERE log.File_Date = @FileDate
                    AND log.Error_Code = 'Reject 13/14'
                    AND log.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND log.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND log.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
            );
        
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#ErrorGroups') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #ErrorGroups;
        
        SELECT DISTINCT NDC, Procedure_Code
        INTO #ErrorGroups
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NOT NULL;
        
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'GROUP_REJECTION'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN #ErrorGroups eg ON w.NDC = eg.NDC AND w.Procedure_Code = eg.Procedure_Code
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL;
        
        -- ✅ KEEP: Log group rejection errors
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
            File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
            Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
        )
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            Ineffective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'GROUP_REJECTION',
            'Rejected due to group rejection rule (Rule 5)',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error = 'GROUP_REJECTION';
        
        DECLARE @GroupRejected INT = @@ROWCOUNT;
        SET @RejectedRecords = @RejectedRecords + @GroupRejected;
        
        PRINT 'Group rejection additional errors: ' + CAST(@GroupRejected AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Total rejected records: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        -- ✅ KEEP: Log warnings
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
            File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
            Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
        )
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            w.NDC,
            w.Procedure_Code,
            w.Effective_Date,
            w.Ineffective_Date,
            'WARNING',
            'Warning 15',
            'RETRO RECORD: Ineffective date (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Ineffective_Date, 23) + 
                ') earlier than existing effective date (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Effective_Date, 23) + ')',
            'WILL_INSERT'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk p ON
            w.NDC = p.NDC
            AND w.Procedure_Code = p.Procedure_Code
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL
            AND w.Ineffective_Date < p.Effective_Date
            AND NOT EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk p2
                WHERE p2.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND p2.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND (
                        (w.Effective_Date >= p2.Effective_Date 
                            AND (p2.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Effective_Date <= p2.Ineffective_Date))
                        OR
                        (w.Ineffective_Date >= p2.Effective_Date
                            AND (p2.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date <= p2.Ineffective_Date))
                    )
            );
        
        SET @WarningRecords = @@ROWCOUNT;
        PRINT 'Warnings detected: ' + CAST(@WarningRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        PRINT 'Processing valid records...';
        
        DECLARE @NDC VARCHAR(11), @ProcCode VARCHAR(5), @NOC CHAR(1);
        DECLARE @DrugName VARCHAR(255), @RCDesc VARCHAR(512);
        DECLARE @EffDate DATE, @IneffDate DATE;
        DECLARE @BillUnits DECIMAL(12,6), @BillUnitsPkg DECIMAL(12,6);
        DECLARE @PkgSize DECIMAL(12,6), @PkgQty INT;
        DECLARE @UOM VARCHAR(10), @PkgUOM VARCHAR(20);
        DECLARE @FileDate_Value DATE;
        DECLARE @ExistingIneff DATE;
        
        DECLARE process_cursor CURSOR LOCAL FAST_FORWARD FOR
            SELECT 
                NDC, DRUG_NAME, Procedure_Code, RC_DESC, NOC_Indicator, 
                Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
                BILL_UNITS, BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG, PKG_SIZE, PKG_QTY,
                UOM, PKG_UOM, File_Date
            FROM #WorkingData
            WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            ORDER BY Procedure_Code, NDC, Effective_Date;
        
        OPEN process_cursor;
        FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO 
            @NDC, @DrugName, @ProcCode, @RCDesc, @NOC, 
            @EffDate, @IneffDate,
            @BillUnits, @BillUnitsPkg, @PkgSize, @PkgQty,
            @UOM, @PkgUOM, @FileDate_Value;
        
        WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0
        BEGIN
            
            SET @ExistingIneff = NULL;
            
            SELECT @ExistingIneff = Ineffective_Date
            FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
            WHERE NDC = @NDC
                AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode
                AND Effective_Date = @EffDate;
            
            IF @ExistingIneff IS NOT NULL 
                AND @IneffDate IS NOT NULL 
                AND @IneffDate <> @ExistingIneff
            BEGIN
                -- ✅ KEEP: Log closed record errors
                INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
                    File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
                    Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
                )
                VALUES (
                    @FileDate, 
                    @NDC, 
                    @ProcCode, 
                    @EffDate, 
                    @IneffDate,
                    'ERROR', 
                    'CLOSED_RECORD',
                    'ATTEMPT TO UPDATE CLOSED RECORD: Existing Ineff=' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @ExistingIneff, 23) + 
                    ', New Ineff=' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @IneffDate, 23),
                    'REJECTED'
                );
                
                SET @RejectedRecords = @RejectedRecords + 1;
                FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO 
                    @NDC, @DrugName, @ProcCode, @RCDesc, @NOC, 
                    @EffDate, @IneffDate,
                    @BillUnits, @BillUnitsPkg, @PkgSize, @PkgQty,
                    @UOM, @PkgUOM, @FileDate_Value;
                CONTINUE;
            END
            
            IF EXISTS (
                SELECT 1 
                FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk 
                WHERE NDC = @NDC 
                    AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode 
                    AND Effective_Date = @EffDate
            )
            BEGIN
                UPDATE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
                SET 
                    DRUG_NAME = @DrugName,
                    RC_DESC = @RCDesc,
                    NOC_Indicator = @NOC,
                    Ineffective_Date = @IneffDate,
                    BILL_UNITS = @BillUnits,
                    BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG = @BillUnitsPkg,
                    PKG_SIZE = @PkgSize,
                    PKG_QTY = @PkgQty,
                    UOM = @UOM,
                    PKG_UOM = @PkgUOM,
                    [User_ID] = SYSTEM_USER,
                    DLU = GETDATE(),
                    File_Date = @FileDate_Value
                WHERE NDC = @NDC
                    AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode
                    AND Effective_Date = @EffDate;
                
                SET @UpdatedRecords = @UpdatedRecords + 1;
                
                -- =====================================================
                -- ❌ COMMENTED OUT: Log only errors/warnings (not successful updates)
                -- Uncomment below to log all successful updates
                -- =====================================================
                /*
                INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
                    File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
                    Log_Type, Message, Action_Taken
                )
                VALUES (@FileDate, @NDC, @ProcCode, @EffDate, @IneffDate, 'INFO', 'Record updated', 'UPDATED');
                */
            END
            ELSE
            BEGIN
                INSERT INTO dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (
                    NDC, DRUG_NAME, Procedure_Code, RC_DESC, NOC_Indicator, 
                    Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
                    BILL_UNITS, BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG, PKG_SIZE, PKG_QTY,
                    UOM, PKG_UOM,
                    [User_ID], DLU, File_Date
                )
                VALUES (
                    @NDC, @DrugName, @ProcCode, @RCDesc, @NOC, 
                    @EffDate, @IneffDate,
                    @BillUnits, @BillUnitsPkg, @PkgSize, @PkgQty,
                    @UOM, @PkgUOM,
                    SYSTEM_USER, GETDATE(), @FileDate_Value
                );
                
                SET @InsertedRecords = @InsertedRecords + 1;
                
                -- =====================================================
                -- ❌ COMMENTED OUT: Log only errors/warnings (not successful inserts)
                -- Uncomment below to log all successful inserts
                -- =====================================================
                /*
                INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
                    File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
                    Log_Type, Message, Action_Taken
                )
                VALUES (@FileDate, @NDC, @ProcCode, @EffDate, @IneffDate, 'INFO', 'New record inserted', 'INSERTED');
                */
            END
            
            FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO 
                @NDC, @DrugName, @ProcCode, @RCDesc, @NOC, 
                @EffDate, @IneffDate,
                @BillUnits, @BillUnitsPkg, @PkgSize, @PkgQty,
                @UOM, @PkgUOM, @FileDate_Value;
        END
        
        CLOSE process_cursor;
        DEALLOCATE process_cursor;
        
        PRINT '============================================================';
        PRINT 'PROCESSING COMPLETED';
        PRINT 'Total Records in File: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Excluded (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01): ' + CAST(@ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Processed: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Inserted: ' + CAST(@InsertedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Updated: ' + CAST(@UpdatedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Rejected: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Warnings: ' + CAST(@WarningRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Duration: ' + CAST(DATEDIFF(SECOND, @StartTime, GETDATE()) AS VARCHAR) + ' seconds';
        PRINT '============================================================';
        
        SELECT 
            @FileDate AS File_Date,
            @TotalRecords AS Total_Records_In_File,
            @ExcludedRecords AS Excluded_Pre2014,
            @InsertedRecords AS Inserted,
            @UpdatedRecords AS Updated,
            @RejectedRecords AS Rejected,
            @WarningRecords AS Warnings,
            DATEDIFF(SECOND, @StartTime, GETDATE()) AS Duration_Seconds;
        
        DROP TABLE #WorkingData;
        DROP TABLE #ErrorGroups;
        DROP TABLE #ProjectedProduction;
        
    END TRY
    BEGIN CATCH
        PRINT 'ERROR: ' + ERROR_MESSAGE();
        THROW;
    END CATCH
    
END;
GO
```

---

## **✅ SUMMARY**

**Only 2 sections commented out:**
1. Line ~330: `INSERT...UPDATED` (keep @UpdatedRecords counter)
2. Line ~350: `INSERT...INSERTED` (keep @InsertedRecords counter)

**All error/warning logging kept:**
- ✅ Field validation errors
- ✅ Duplicate errors
- ✅ Overlap errors
- ✅ Group rejection
- ✅ Warning 15
- ✅ Closed record errors

**To re-enable full logging in future:**
Just remove the `/*` and `*/` comment markers!

---

**Deploy this version and you'll log only ~200 errors per month instead of 200K+ entries!** 🚀

great 
and i processed 2025-10-01
and then 2025-11-01


what will happen if i try to process 2025-11-01?


*System: User retried message*

## Copilot

**Great question! Let's analyze what happens when you reprocess the same month.**

---

## **🔍 SCENARIO: REPROCESSING 2025-11-01 (Second Time)**

### **Current State After First Processing:**
```
Production Table (dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk):
- ~180K records with File_Date = '2025-10-01' (from October)
- ~597 records with File_Date = '2025-11-01' (new from November)
- ~180K records updated with File_Date = '2025-11-01' (idempotent updates)

Log Table:
- ~184 error entries for October
- ~184 error entries for November
```

---

## **📊 WHAT WILL HAPPEN IF YOU REPROCESS 2025-11-01:**

### **Step-by-Step Execution:**

**1. Load Data from Source**
```sql
SELECT ... FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data WHERE File_Date = '2025-11-01'
```
✅ Loads the **same 232,809 records** from November file again

**2. Validation**
- ✅ Same ~184 records will be rejected (same validation errors)
- ✅ No duplicates within the file (assuming clean source)

**3. Overlap Check (Projected State)**
- ✅ Creates projected state from **current production** (which already has November data)
- ✅ Checks November records against this projected state
- ⚠️ **No overlap errors** (because records already exist with same keys)

**4. Group Rejection**
- ✅ Same error groups rejected

**5. Process Valid Records (Cursor Loop)**

For each valid record:

**Case A: Record already exists (most records - ~180K)**
```sql
IF EXISTS (... WHERE NDC = @NDC AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode AND Effective_Date = @EffDate)
BEGIN
    -- UPDATE executes
    UPDATE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
    SET 
        DRUG_NAME = @DrugName,
        RC_DESC = @RCDesc,
        NOC_Indicator = @NOC,
        Ineffective_Date = @IneffDate,
        BILL_UNITS = @BillUnits,
        ...
        DLU = GETDATE(),              ← Updates to current timestamp
        File_Date = '2025-11-01'       ← Stays November (same value)
    WHERE NDC = @NDC AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode AND Effective_Date = @EffDate;
    
    @UpdatedRecords incremented
END
```

**Case B: Record doesn't exist (unlikely - only if deleted)**
```sql
ELSE
BEGIN
    INSERT INTO dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (...)
    VALUES (...);
    
    @InsertedRecords incremented
END
```

---

## **✅ EXPECTED RESULTS (Reprocessing November):**

```
============================================================
NDC CROSSWALK PROCESSING
File Date: 2025-11-01
============================================================
Total records in file: 232,809
Excluded (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01): 52,000
Records to process: 180,809
Working data loaded: 180,809 records

Field validation errors: 0        ← Already rejected first time
Group rejection errors: 0         ← Already rejected first time
Total rejected records: 0         ← No NEW rejections
Warnings detected: 0

Processing valid records...

============================================================
PROCESSING COMPLETED
Total Records in File: 232,809
Excluded (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01): 52,000
Processed: 180,809
Inserted: 0                       ← All records already exist
Updated: 180,625                  ← All existing records updated again
Rejected: 0                       ← No new rejections
Warnings: 0
Duration: ~300 seconds
============================================================
```

### **Log Table After Rerun:**
```
File_Date    Log_Type    Count
2025-10-01   ERROR       184
2025-11-01   ERROR       184   ← From first run (no new entries)
```
**No new log entries** because you're only logging errors/warnings, and the same records are rejected silently.

---

## **🔍 KEY OBSERVATIONS:**

### **✅ SAFE BEHAVIORS:**

1. **Idempotency Works**
   - All records are **updated** with same values
   - DLU updated to reprocessing timestamp
   - File_Date remains '2025-11-01'

2. **No Duplicate Errors**
   - Records already exist, so no duplicate key violations
   - UNIQUE constraint on (NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date) prevents duplicates

3. **Performance**
   - Takes ~5 minutes (same as first run)
   - All ~180K records updated (even with identical values)

---

### **⚠️ POTENTIAL ISSUES:**

**Issue 1: DLU Timestamp Changes**
```
Before Rerun:
  DLU = 2026-01-24 19:00:00  (first November run)

After Rerun:
  DLU = 2026-01-24 21:30:00  (reprocessing timestamp)
```
**Impact:** Audit trail shows "updated" even though data unchanged

---

**Issue 2: File_Date Ambiguity**
```
If you look at File_Date = '2025-11-01', you can't tell:
- Was it processed once?
- Was it reprocessed multiple times?
```
**Solution:** You'd need a process log summary table to track processing history

---

**Issue 3: No Log Entries for Reprocessing**
```
Log table shows:
- 184 errors from first run
- 0 new entries from rerun

You won't know the file was reprocessed unless you check DLU timestamps.
```

---

## **🎯 BEST PRACTICES FOR PRODUCTION**

### **Option 1: Prevent Reprocessing (Recommended)**

Add a check at the start of the stored procedure:

```sql
-- Add after parameter declaration
DECLARE @AlreadyProcessed INT;

SELECT @AlreadyProcessed = COUNT(*)
FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
WHERE File_Date = @FileDate;

IF @AlreadyProcessed > 0
BEGIN
    PRINT 'ERROR: File date ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @FileDate, 23) + ' already processed';
    PRINT 'Found ' + CAST(@AlreadyProcessed AS VARCHAR) + ' existing records with this file date';
    PRINT 'Aborting to prevent duplicate processing';
    RETURN;
END
```

---

### **Option 2: Add Processing Summary Table**

Track each processing run:

```sql
CREATE TABLE dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Summary (
    Summary_ID INT IDENTITY(1,1) NOT NULL,
    File_Date DATE NOT NULL,
    Process_Date DATETIME NOT NULL DEFAULT (GETDATE()),
    Total_Records INT,
    Inserted INT,
    Updated INT,
    Rejected INT,
    Duration_Seconds INT,
    Status VARCHAR(20),  -- SUCCESS, REPROCESSED, FAILED
    CONSTRAINT PK_Summary PRIMARY KEY (Summary_ID)
);

-- At end of stored procedure:
INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Summary (...)
VALUES (@FileDate, GETDATE(), @TotalRecords, @InsertedRecords, ...);
```

Then query shows:
```
File_Date    Process_Date         Status         Inserted  Updated
2025-11-01   2026-01-24 19:00:00  SUCCESS        597       180,028
2025-11-01   2026-01-24 21:30:00  REPROCESSED    0         180,625  ← Rerun
```

---

### **Option 3: Add Reprocessing Flag**

Allow controlled reprocessing:

```sql
ALTER PROCEDURE dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk
    @FileDate DATE,
    @AllowReprocessing BIT = 0  -- Default: don't allow
AS
BEGIN
    IF @AllowReprocessing = 0
    BEGIN
        -- Check if already processed
        IF EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk WHERE File_Date = @FileDate)
        BEGIN
            PRINT 'ERROR: File already processed. Use @AllowReprocessing = 1 to override';
            RETURN;
        END
    END
    
    -- Rest of procedure...
END

-- Normal processing:
EXEC dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk @FileDate = '2025-11-01';

-- Reprocessing (explicit):
EXEC dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk @FileDate = '2025-11-01', @AllowReprocessing = 1;
```

---

## **📋 MY RECOMMENDATION**

**For Production: Use Option 1 (Prevent Reprocessing) + Option 2 (Summary Table)**

**Why:**
1. ✅ Prevents accidental reprocessing
2. ✅ Clear audit trail of all processing attempts
3. ✅ Easy to detect and investigate issues
4. ✅ Can still manually reprocess if needed (delete File_Date records first)

---

## **🔧 QUICK FIX (Add to Your Stored Procedure)**

Add this right after the `BEGIN TRY` block:

```sql
BEGIN TRY
    
    PRINT '============================================================';
    PRINT 'NDC CROSSWALK PROCESSING';
    PRINT 'File Date: ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @FileDate, 23);
    PRINT 'Start Time: ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @StartTime, 120);
    PRINT '============================================================';
    
    -- =====================================================
    -- CHECK: Prevent reprocessing
    -- =====================================================
    DECLARE @ExistingRecords INT;
    
    SELECT @ExistingRecords = COUNT(*)
    FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
    WHERE File_Date = @FileDate;
    
    IF @ExistingRecords > 0
    BEGIN
        PRINT '============================================================';
        PRINT 'WARNING: FILE ALREADY PROCESSED';
        PRINT 'File Date: ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @FileDate, 23);
        PRINT 'Existing records with this file date: ' + CAST(@ExistingRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT '============================================================';
        PRINT 'To reprocess:';
        PRINT '1. Delete existing records: DELETE FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk WHERE File_Date = ''' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @FileDate, 23) + '''';
        PRINT '2. Delete existing logs: DELETE FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log WHERE File_Date = ''' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @FileDate, 23) + '''';
        PRINT '3. Re-run stored procedure';
        PRINT '============================================================';
        RETURN;
    END
    
    -- Rest of procedure...
```

---

**Want me to provide the complete updated stored procedure with the reprocessing check?** 🚀

## @arunknc0211-art

instead can we setup like 
2025-11-01 file already processed ?


and if we want to reprocess same file 
then they should pass a parameter saying reprocess = 1
by default it will be zero which wont allow reprocessing 

can we do this just discuss then we can do implementation


## Copilot

**Excellent idea! That's exactly Option 3 I mentioned - it's the most flexible and production-ready approach. Let's discuss the design.**

---

## **🎯 DESIGN: REPROCESSING CONTROL WITH PARAMETER**

### **Proposed Stored Procedure Signature:**

```sql
CREATE PROCEDURE dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk
    @FileDate DATE,
    @AllowReprocessing BIT = 0  -- Default: 0 (don't allow)
AS
```

---

## **📋 BEHAVIOR DESIGN**

### **Scenario 1: Normal Processing (First Time)**

```sql
EXEC dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk @FileDate = '2025-11-01';
-- or
EXEC dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk @FileDate = '2025-11-01', @AllowReprocessing = 0;
```

**What happens:**
1. ✅ Check if file already processed
2. ❌ No existing records found
3. ✅ Continue with normal processing
4. ✅ Insert/update records

**Result:** SUCCESS

---

### **Scenario 2: Accidental Reprocessing Attempt (Default)**

```sql
EXEC dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk @FileDate = '2025-11-01';
```

**What happens:**
1. ✅ Check if file already processed
2. ✅ Found 180,625 existing records with File_Date = '2025-11-01'
3. ⚠️ **@AllowReprocessing = 0** (default)
4. ❌ **ABORT with warning message**

**Output:**
```
============================================================
WARNING: FILE ALREADY PROCESSED
============================================================
File Date: 2025-11-01
Existing records: 180,625
Last processed: 2026-01-24 19:00:00 (DLU of recent record)

To reprocess this file, run:
EXEC dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk @FileDate = '2025-11-01', @AllowReprocessing = 1

Note: Reprocessing will:
- Update all existing records (DLU timestamps refreshed)
- Not create duplicates
- Take approximately 5 minutes

Processing ABORTED to prevent accidental reprocessing.
============================================================
```

**Result:** ABORTED (safe default)

---

### **Scenario 3: Intentional Reprocessing (Explicit Permission)**

```sql
EXEC dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk @FileDate = '2025-11-01', @AllowReprocessing = 1;
```

**What happens:**
1. ✅ Check if file already processed
2. ✅ Found 180,625 existing records
3. ✅ **@AllowReprocessing = 1** (explicitly allowed)
4. ⚠️ **Show warning but continue**
5. ✅ Reprocess all records

**Output:**
```
============================================================
REPROCESSING MODE ENABLED
============================================================
File Date: 2025-11-01
Existing records: 180,625
AllowReprocessing: TRUE

Continuing with reprocessing...
All existing records will be updated with fresh data.
============================================================

... (normal processing output) ...

============================================================
PROCESSING COMPLETED (REPROCESSED)
Total Records in File: 232,809
Inserted: 0
Updated: 180,625  ← All records updated
Rejected: 184
============================================================
```

**Result:** SUCCESS (with clear indication of reprocessing)

---

## **🤔 DESIGN QUESTIONS TO DISCUSS**

### **Question 1: What Should Reprocessing Do?**

**Option A: Update Existing Records (Recommended)**
```
- Keeps existing records
- Updates all fields from source
- Refreshes DLU timestamps
- Keeps same File_Date
```
**Pros:**
- ✅ Safe (no data loss)
- ✅ Idempotent (same result every time)
- ✅ Fast (updates only)

**Cons:**
- ⚠️ DLU shows reprocessing time (not original)

---

**Option B: Delete and Reload**
```
1. DELETE FROM Procedure_NDC_Xwalk WHERE File_Date = @FileDate
2. DELETE FROM NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log WHERE File_Date = @FileDate
3. Process fresh (as if first time)
```
**Pros:**
- ✅ Clean slate
- ✅ DLU shows actual processing time
- ✅ Log entries fresh

**Cons:**
- ⚠️ Brief window where data doesn't exist (during delete)
- ⚠️ Loses historical log entries
- ⚠️ Slower (delete + insert vs update)

---

**My Recommendation: Option A (Update Existing)**
- More efficient
- No data loss risk
- Better for production systems with concurrent queries

---

### **Question 2: How to Detect "Already Processed"?**

**Option A: Check by File_Date in Production Table**
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk 
WHERE File_Date = @FileDate;
```
**Pros:**
- ✅ Simple
- ✅ Direct check

**Cons:**
- ⚠️ If someone manually updates File_Date, detection breaks

---

**Option B: Use Processing Summary Table**
```sql
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Summary 
WHERE File_Date = @FileDate AND Status = 'SUCCESS';
```
**Pros:**
- ✅ Reliable audit trail
- ✅ Can track multiple runs
- ✅ Shows processing history

**Cons:**
- ⚠️ Requires new table

---

**My Recommendation: Option A now, add Option B later**
- Start simple (check production table)
- Add summary table in next phase for better tracking

---

### **Question 3: What About Partial Failures?**

**Scenario:**
```
First run: Processing starts
           50% complete
           ERROR occurs (network issue, server crash)
           Result: Some records inserted, some not
```

**What should happen on retry?**

**Option A: Allow reprocessing with @AllowReprocessing = 1**
```
- Detects existing records
- Requires explicit permission
- Updates existing, inserts missing
```

**Option B: Smart detection - if count mismatch, auto-allow**
```sql
IF @ExistingRecords < (@TotalRecords * 0.9)  -- Less than 90% loaded
BEGIN
    PRINT 'Partial load detected - allowing reprocessing';
    SET @AllowReprocessing = 1;
END
```

**My Recommendation: Option A (keep it simple)**
- Let user decide with @AllowReprocessing = 1
- Don't auto-reprocess (safer)

---

### **Question 4: Should We Log the Reprocessing Attempt?**

**Option A: Log even when blocked**
```sql
-- Even if @AllowReprocessing = 0 and aborted
INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Summary (...)
VALUES (@FileDate, GETDATE(), NULL, NULL, NULL, 0, 'BLOCKED_REPROCESSING');
```

**Option B: Only log actual processing**
```sql
-- Only log if processing actually happened
-- Don't log blocked attempts
```

**My Recommendation: Option A**
- Track all processing attempts (including blocked)
- Helps detect mistakes/confusion
- Better audit trail

---

## **🎯 RECOMMENDED IMPLEMENTATION DESIGN**

### **Summary of Recommendations:**

1. **Reprocessing Behavior:** Update existing records (no delete/reload)
2. **Detection Method:** Check production table for File_Date
3. **Partial Failures:** Require explicit @AllowReprocessing = 1
4. **Logging:** Log all attempts (including blocked)
5. **User Feedback:** Clear messages explaining what happened

---

### **Stored Procedure Logic Flow:**

```
START
  ↓
Get @FileDate and @AllowReprocessing parameters
  ↓
Check: Does File_Date exist in production?
  ↓
  ├─ NO → Continue normal processing ✅
  │         ↓
  │       Load data, validate, process
  │         ↓
  │       Insert/Update records
  │         ↓
  │       END (SUCCESS)
  │
  └─ YES → Check @AllowReprocessing
            ↓
            ├─ 0 (default) → Show warning message ⚠️
            │                  ↓
            │                Log attempt as BLOCKED
            │                  ↓
            │                ABORT (SAFE EXIT)
            │
            └─ 1 (explicit) → Show reprocessing message ⚠️
                               ↓
                             Continue processing
                               ↓
                             Update all existing records
                               ↓
                             Log as REPROCESSED
                               ↓
                             END (SUCCESS - REPROCESSED)
```

---

## **📋 ADDITIONAL FEATURES TO CONSIDER**

### **Feature 1: Show Processing History**

Add this query to the warning message:

```sql
-- Show when file was last processed
SELECT TOP 3
    File_Date,
    MAX(DLU) AS Last_Processed,
    COUNT(*) AS Record_Count
FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
WHERE File_Date = @FileDate
GROUP BY File_Date
ORDER BY File_Date DESC;
```

Output:
```
File_Date    Last_Processed          Record_Count
2025-11-01   2026-01-24 19:00:00     180,625
```

```sql
ALTER PROCEDURE dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk

    @DryRun BIT = 0  -- NEW: Just validate, don't commit
```

**Usage:**
```sql
-- See what would happen without actually doing it
EXEC dbo.usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk 
    @FileDate = '2025-11-01', 
    @AllowReprocessing = 1,
    @DryRun = 1;
```

```sql
-- If @AllowReprocessing = 1, send email alert
IF @AllowReprocessing = 1
BEGIN
    EXEC msdb.dbo.sp_send_dbmail
        @recipients = 'dba-team@company.com',
        @subject = 'NDC Xwalk: File Reprocessed',
        @body = 'File 2025-11-01 was reprocessed by user XYZ';

```
