stored proc:
USE [DW_Reference]
GO

/****** Object:  StoredProcedure [dbo].[usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk]    Script Date: 1/27/2026 2:58:04 PM ******/
SET ANSI_NULLS ON
GO

SET QUOTED_IDENTIFIER ON
GO




CREATE PROCEDURE [dbo].[usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk]
    @FileDate DATE
AS
BEGIN
    SET NOCOUNT ON;
    
    DECLARE @StartTime DATETIME2 = GETDATE();
    DECLARE @TotalRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @ExcludedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @InsertedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @UpdatedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @RejectedRecords INT = 0;
    DECLARE @WarningRecords INT = 0;
    
    BEGIN TRY
        
        PRINT '============================================================';
        PRINT 'NDC CROSSWALK PROCESSING';
        PRINT 'File Date: ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @FileDate, 23);
        PRINT 'Start Time: ' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @StartTime, 120);
        PRINT '============================================================';
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 1: Count total records
        -- =====================================================
        
        SELECT @TotalRecords = COUNT(*)
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate;
        
        PRINT 'Total records in file: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        -- Count excluded records (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01)
        SELECT @ExcludedRecords = COUNT(*)
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate
            AND RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL
            AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> ''
            AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < '2014-01-01';
        
        PRINT 'Excluded records (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01): ' + CAST(@ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Records to process: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        IF (@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords) = 0
        BEGIN
            PRINT 'WARNING: No records to process after exclusions';
            RETURN;
        END
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 2: Create temp working table with validation
        -- =====================================================
        
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#WorkingData') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #WorkingData;
        
        SELECT 
            LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) AS NDC,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(DRUG_NAME)) AS DRUG_NAME,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) AS Procedure_Code,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(RC_DESC)) AS RC_DESC,
            CASE 
                WHEN UPPER(LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC))) = 'Y' THEN 'Y' 
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS NOC_Indicator,
            TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) AS Effective_Date,
            CASE 
                WHEN RMV_DATE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) = '' THEN NULL
                ELSE TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101)
            END AS Ineffective_Date,
            CASE 
                WHEN BILL_UNITS IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(BILL_UNITS) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(BILL_UNITS AS DECIMAL(12,6))
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS BILL_UNITS,
            CASE 
                WHEN BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG AS DECIMAL(12,6))
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG,
            CASE 
                WHEN PKG_SIZE IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(PKG_SIZE) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(PKG_SIZE AS DECIMAL(12,6))
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS PKG_SIZE,
            CASE 
                WHEN PKG_QTY IS NOT NULL AND ISNUMERIC(PKG_QTY) = 1 
                THEN TRY_CAST(PKG_QTY AS INT)
                ELSE NULL 
            END AS PKG_QTY,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(UOM)) AS UOM,
            LTRIM(RTRIM(PKG_UOM)) AS PKG_UOM,
            File_Date,
            
            -- Validation flags
            CAST(CASE 
                -- Reject 01: NDC validations
                WHEN NDC IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC MISSING'
                WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC))) <> 11 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (length)'
                WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) LIKE '%[^0-9]%' 
                    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (non-numeric)'
                
                -- Reject 02: Procedure Code validations
                WHEN REIMB_CODE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE MISSING'
                WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE))) <> 5 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (length)'
                WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) LIKE '%[^A-Z0-9]%' 
                    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (alphanumeric)'
                
                -- Reject 03: NOC Indicator validation (case-insensitive)
                WHEN IS_NOC IS NOT NULL 
                    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC)) <> '' 
                    AND UPPER(LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC))) <> 'Y' 
                    THEN 'Reject 03: NOC INDICATOR UNKNOWN VALUE'
                
                -- Reject 04: Effective Date validations
                WHEN ADD_DATE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(ADD_DATE)) = '' 
                    THEN 'Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE MISSING'
                WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
                    THEN 'Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT'
                
                -- Reject 05: Ineffective Date validation
                WHEN RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL 
                    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> '' 
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
                    THEN 'Reject 05: INEFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT'
                
                -- Reject 06: Ineffective before Effective
                WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NOT NULL 
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) IS NOT NULL
                    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)
                    THEN 'Reject 06: INEFFECTIVE DATE BEFORE EFFECTIVE DATE'
                
                ELSE NULL
            END AS NVARCHAR(500)) AS Validation_Error,
            
            0 AS Is_Processed
            
        INTO #WorkingData
        FROM dbo.RJHealth_Drug_Data
        WHERE File_Date = @FileDate
            -- =====================================================
            -- NEW EXCLUSION FILTER: Exclude RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01
            -- =====================================================
            AND (
                -- Include if RMV_DATE is NULL or empty (open-ended records)
                RMV_DATE IS NULL 
                OR LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) = ''
                -- OR include if RMV_DATE conversion fails (will be caught by validation)
                OR TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NULL
                -- OR include if RMV_DATE >= 2014-01-01 (recent closed mappings)
                OR TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) >= '2014-01-01'
            );
        
	DECLARE @WorkingCount INT = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM #WorkingData);
        PRINT 'Working data loaded: ' + CAST(@WorkingCount AS VARCHAR) + ' records';
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 3: Log field validation errors
        -- =====================================================
        
        -- Log field validation errors
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
            File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
            Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
        )
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            Ineffective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            LEFT(Validation_Error, CHARINDEX(':', Validation_Error) - 1),
            Validation_Error,
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NOT NULL;
        
        SET @RejectedRecords = @@ROWCOUNT;
        PRINT 'Field validation errors: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
            File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
            Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
        )
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            Ineffective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'Reject 11',
            'DUPLICATE RECORDS: Key appears ' + CAST(COUNT(*) AS VARCHAR) + ' times',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
        GROUP BY NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date
        HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
        
        -- Mark duplicates with error
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'Reject 11: DUPLICATE RECORDS'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM #WorkingData w2
                WHERE w2.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND w2.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND w2.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
                    AND w2.Validation_Error IS NULL
                GROUP BY w2.NDC, w2.Procedure_Code, w2.Effective_Date
                HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
            );
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- FIX 2: IMPROVED OVERLAP CHECK - Use Projected State
        -- CORRECTED: Only include columns needed for overlap check
        -- =====================================================
        
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#ProjectedProduction') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #ProjectedProduction;
        
        SELECT NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date
        INTO #ProjectedProduction
        FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk;
        
        -- Apply ineffective date updates from working data to create projected state
        UPDATE proj
        SET proj.Ineffective_Date = w.Ineffective_Date
        FROM #ProjectedProduction proj
        INNER JOIN #WorkingData w ON
            proj.NDC = w.NDC
            AND proj.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
            AND proj.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL;
        
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
            File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
            Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
        )
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            w.NDC,
            w.Procedure_Code,
            w.Effective_Date,
            w.Ineffective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'Reject 13/14',
            'OVERLAP: New (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Effective_Date, 23) + ' to ' + 
                ISNULL(CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Ineffective_Date, 23), 'open') + ') overlaps existing (' +
                CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Effective_Date, 23) + ' to ' + 
                ISNULL(CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Ineffective_Date, 23), 'open') + ')',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN #ProjectedProduction p ON
            w.NDC = p.NDC
            AND w.Procedure_Code = p.Procedure_Code
            AND w.Effective_Date <> p.Effective_Date  -- Different effective dates
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND (
                -- New starts during existing period
                (w. Effective_Date >= p.Effective_Date 
                    AND (p.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Effective_Date <= p.Ineffective_Date))
                OR
                -- New ends during existing period
                (w. Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL 
                    AND w.Ineffective_Date >= p.Effective_Date
                    AND (p.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w. Ineffective_Date <= p.Ineffective_Date))
                OR
                -- New encompasses existing
                (w.Effective_Date <= p.Effective_Date
                    AND (w.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date >= p.Effective_Date))
            );
        
        -- Mark overlaps with error
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'Reject 13/14: OVERLAP'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND EXISTS (
                SELECT 1
                FROM dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log log
                WHERE log.File_Date = @FileDate
                    AND log.Error_Code = 'Reject 13/14'
                    AND log.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND log.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND log.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
            );
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 6: Group Rejection Rule (Rule 5)
        -- =====================================================
        
        -- Find groups with errors
        IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#ErrorGroups') IS NOT NULL DROP TABLE #ErrorGroups;
        
        SELECT DISTINCT NDC, Procedure_Code
        INTO #ErrorGroups
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error IS NOT NULL;
        
        -- Reject all records in error groups
        UPDATE w
        SET Validation_Error = 'GROUP_REJECTION'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN #ErrorGroups eg ON w.NDC = eg.NDC AND w.Procedure_Code = eg.Procedure_Code
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL;
        
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
            File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
            Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
        )
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            NDC,
            Procedure_Code,
            Effective_Date,
            Ineffective_Date,
            'ERROR',
            'GROUP_REJECTION',
            'Rejected due to group rejection rule (Rule 5)',
            'REJECTED'
        FROM #WorkingData
        WHERE Validation_Error = 'GROUP_REJECTION';
        
        DECLARE @GroupRejected INT = @@ROWCOUNT;
        SET @RejectedRecords = @RejectedRecords + @GroupRejected;
        
        PRINT 'Group rejection additional errors: ' + CAST(@GroupRejected AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Total rejected records: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
            File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
            Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
        )
        SELECT 
            @FileDate,
            w.NDC,
            w.Procedure_Code,
            w.Effective_Date,
            w.Ineffective_Date,
            'WARNING',
            'Warning 15',
            'RETRO RECORD: Ineffective date (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Ineffective_Date, 23) + 
                ') earlier than existing effective date (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Effective_Date, 23) + ')',
            'WILL_INSERT'
        FROM #WorkingData w
        INNER JOIN dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk p ON
            w.NDC = p.NDC
            AND w.Procedure_Code = p.Procedure_Code
        WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
            AND w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL
            AND w. Ineffective_Date < p. Effective_Date
            AND NOT EXISTS (
                -- Ensure no overlap
                SELECT 1
                FROM dbo. Procedure_NDC_Xwalk p2
                WHERE p2.NDC = w.NDC
                    AND p2.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
                    AND (
                        (w.Effective_Date >= p2.Effective_Date 
                            AND (p2.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Effective_Date <= p2.Ineffective_Date))
                        OR
                        (w.Ineffective_Date >= p2.Effective_Date
                            AND (p2.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date <= p2.Ineffective_Date))
                    )
            );
        
        SET @WarningRecords = @@ROWCOUNT;
        PRINT 'Warnings detected: ' + CAST(@WarningRecords AS VARCHAR);
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- STEP 8: Sort and Process Valid Records (Rule 3 & 4)
        -- =====================================================
        
        PRINT 'Processing valid records...';
        
        -- Create sorted cursor for processing (oldest first)
        DECLARE @NDC VARCHAR(11), @ProcCode VARCHAR(5), @NOC CHAR(1);
        DECLARE @DrugName VARCHAR(255), @RCDesc VARCHAR(512);
        DECLARE @EffDate DATE, @IneffDate DATE;
        DECLARE @BillUnits DECIMAL(12,6), @BillUnitsPkg DECIMAL(12,6);
        DECLARE @PkgSize DECIMAL(12,6), @PkgQty INT;
        DECLARE @UOM VARCHAR(10), @PkgUOM VARCHAR(20);
        DECLARE @FileDate_Value DATE;
        DECLARE @ExistingIneff DATE;
        
        DECLARE process_cursor CURSOR LOCAL FAST_FORWARD FOR
            SELECT 
                NDC, DRUG_NAME, Procedure_Code, RC_DESC, NOC_Indicator, 
                Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
                BILL_UNITS, BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG, PKG_SIZE, PKG_QTY,
                UOM, PKG_UOM, File_Date
            FROM #WorkingData
            WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
            ORDER BY Procedure_Code, NDC, Effective_Date;  -- Rule 3: Sort by Proc, NDC, Eff Date
        
        OPEN process_cursor;
        FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO 
            @NDC, @DrugName, @ProcCode, @RCDesc, @NOC, 
            @EffDate, @IneffDate,
            @BillUnits, @BillUnitsPkg, @PkgSize, @PkgQty,
            @UOM, @PkgUOM, @FileDate_Value;
        
        WHILE @@FETCH_STATUS = 0
        BEGIN
            
            -- =====================================================
            -- NEW:  Check for ATTEMPT TO UPDATE CLOSED RECORD
            -- =====================================================
            
            SET @ExistingIneff = NULL;
            
            SELECT @ExistingIneff = Ineffective_Date
            FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
            WHERE NDC = @NDC
                AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode
                AND Effective_Date = @EffDate;
            
            -- If record exists and already has ineffective date
            IF @ExistingIneff IS NOT NULL 
                AND @IneffDate IS NOT NULL 
                AND @IneffDate <> @ExistingIneff
            BEGIN
	    	-- Log error:  Attempting to update closed record
                INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
                    File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
                    Log_Type, Error_Code, Message, Action_Taken
                )
                VALUES (
                    @FileDate, 
                    @NDC, 
                    @ProcCode, 
                    @EffDate, 
                    @IneffDate,
                    'ERROR', 
                    'CLOSED_RECORD',
                    'ATTEMPT TO UPDATE CLOSED RECORD: Existing Ineff=' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @ExistingIneff, 23) + 
                    ', New Ineff=' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @IneffDate, 23),
                    'REJECTED'
                );
                
                SET @RejectedRecords = @RejectedRecords + 1;
		-- Skip to next record
                FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO 
                    @NDC, @DrugName, @ProcCode, @RCDesc, @NOC, 
                    @EffDate, @IneffDate,
                    @BillUnits, @BillUnitsPkg, @PkgSize, @PkgQty,
                    @UOM, @PkgUOM, @FileDate_Value;
                CONTINUE;
            END
            
            -- =====================================================
            -- Check if record exists
            -- =====================================================
            
            IF EXISTS (
                SELECT 1 
                FROM dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk 
                WHERE NDC = @NDC 
                    AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode 
                    AND Effective_Date = @EffDate
            )
            BEGIN
                -- =====================================================
                -- FIX 1: ALWAYS UPDATE ALL FIELDS (no ISNULL logic)
                -- =====================================================
                UPDATE dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
                SET 
                    DRUG_NAME = @DrugName,
                    RC_DESC = @RCDesc,
                    NOC_Indicator = @NOC,
                    Ineffective_Date = @IneffDate,
                    BILL_UNITS = @BillUnits,
                    BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG = @BillUnitsPkg,
                    PKG_SIZE = @PkgSize,
                    PKG_QTY = @PkgQty,
                    UOM = @UOM,
                    PKG_UOM = @PkgUOM,
                    [User_ID] = SYSTEM_USER,
                    DLU = GETDATE(),
                    File_Date = @FileDate_Value
                WHERE NDC = @NDC
                    AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode
                    AND Effective_Date = @EffDate;
                    -- NO condition - ALWAYS execute UPDATE
                
                SET @UpdatedRecords = @UpdatedRecords + 1;
			
			---- UNCOMMENT TO PUSH LOGS FOR UPDATED ROWS 
                INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
                    File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
                    Log_Type, Message, Action_Taken
                )
                VALUES (@FileDate, @NDC, @ProcCode, @EffDate, @IneffDate, 'INFO', 'Record updated', 'UPDATED');
            END
            ELSE
            BEGIN
                -- INSERT with ALL FIELDS
                INSERT INTO dbo.Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (
                    NDC, DRUG_NAME, Procedure_Code, RC_DESC, NOC_Indicator, 
                    Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
                    BILL_UNITS, BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG, PKG_SIZE, PKG_QTY,
                    UOM, PKG_UOM,
                    [User_ID], DLU, File_Date
                )
                VALUES (
                    @NDC, @DrugName, @ProcCode, @RCDesc, @NOC, 
                    @EffDate, @IneffDate,
                    @BillUnits, @BillUnitsPkg, @PkgSize, @PkgQty,
                    @UOM, @PkgUOM,
                    SYSTEM_USER, GETDATE(), @FileDate_Value
                );
                
                SET @InsertedRecords = @InsertedRecords + 1;
                
			---- UNCOMMENT TO PUSH LOGS FOR INSERT ROWS 
                INSERT INTO dbo.NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (
                    File_Date, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
                    Log_Type, Message, Action_Taken
                )
                VALUES (@FileDate, @NDC, @ProcCode, @EffDate, @IneffDate, 'INFO', 'New record inserted', 'INSERTED');
            END
            
            FETCH NEXT FROM process_cursor INTO 
                @NDC, @DrugName, @ProcCode, @RCDesc, @NOC, 
                @EffDate, @IneffDate,
                @BillUnits, @BillUnitsPkg, @PkgSize, @PkgQty,
                @UOM, @PkgUOM, @FileDate_Value;
        END
        
        CLOSE process_cursor;
        DEALLOCATE process_cursor;
        
        -- =====================================================
        -- FINAL SUMMARY
        -- =====================================================
        
        PRINT '============================================================';
        PRINT 'PROCESSING COMPLETED';
        PRINT 'Total Records in File: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Excluded (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01): ' + CAST(@ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Processed: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Inserted: ' + CAST(@InsertedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Updated: ' + CAST(@UpdatedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Rejected: ' + CAST(@RejectedRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Warnings: ' + CAST(@WarningRecords AS VARCHAR);
        PRINT 'Duration: ' + CAST(DATEDIFF(SECOND, @StartTime, GETDATE()) AS VARCHAR) + ' seconds';
        PRINT '============================================================';
        
        -- Return summary
        SELECT 
            @FileDate AS File_Date,
            @TotalRecords AS Total_Records_In_File,
            @ExcludedRecords AS Excluded_Pre2014,
            @InsertedRecords AS Inserted,
            @UpdatedRecords AS Updated,
            @RejectedRecords AS Rejected,
            @WarningRecords AS Warnings,
            DATEDIFF(SECOND, @StartTime, GETDATE()) AS Duration_Seconds;
        
        -- Cleanup
        DROP TABLE #WorkingData;
        DROP TABLE #ErrorGroups;
        DROP TABLE #ProjectedProduction;
        
    END TRY
    BEGIN CATCH
        PRINT 'ERROR: ' + ERROR_MESSAGE();
        THROW;
    END CATCH
    
END;
GO




now give me proper rules logic design document 
to understand for BA and to anyone who sees the document even when they validate with data 






## Copilot

# **NDC Crosswalk Load Logic - Design Documentation**

**Version:** 1.0  
**Last Updated:** January 27, 2026  
**Database:** DW_Reference  
**Primary Procedure:** usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk  

---

## **Table of Contents**

1. [Executive Summary](#1-executive-summary)
2. [Data Flow Architecture](#2-data-flow-architecture)
3. [Table Specifications](#3-table-specifications)
4. [Business Rules & Validation Logic](#4-business-rules--validation-logic)
5. [Load Logic Rules](#5-load-logic-rules)
6. [Processing Workflow](#6-processing-workflow)
7. [Error Handling & Logging Strategy](#7-error-handling--logging-strategy)
8. [Test Scenarios & Examples](#8-test-scenarios--examples)
9. [Operational Procedures](#9-operational-procedures)
10. [Performance Metrics](#10-performance-metrics)
11. [Change History](#11-change-history)

---

## **1. Executive Summary**

### **1.1 Purpose**
The NDC (National Drug Code) Crosswalk process maintains a reference table that maps pharmaceutical NDC codes to medical procedure billing codes. This mapping is essential for healthcare claims adjudication and billing operations.

### **1.2 Business Context**
- **Source:** RJ Health monthly data files containing NDC-to-Procedure Code mappings
- **Frequency:** Monthly processing (typically first week of month)
- **Volume:** ~200,000+ records per monthly file
- **Criticality:** High - directly impacts claims processing and billing accuracy

### **1.3 Key Stakeholders**
- **Business Analyst:** Validates business rules and data quality
- **Claims Processing Team:** Primary consumer of crosswalk data
- **Billing Department:** Uses for procedure code determination
- **IT Operations:** Executes and monitors monthly loads
- **Data Governance:** Ensures data quality and audit compliance

### **1.4 Processing Summary**
```
Monthly File (200K+ records)
    ↓
Pre-2014 Exclusion (~0 records excluded)
    ↓
Field Validation (99.9% pass rate)
    ↓
Overlap Detection (projected state logic)
    ↓
Group Rejection Rule (fail together)
    ↓
Production Table Update
    ↓
~180K inserts (first month) + ~180K updates (subsequent months)
```

---

## **2. Data Flow Architecture**

### **2.1 High-Level Data Flow**

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  SOURCE: RJ Health Monthly File                             │
│  Format: Flat file → SQL Bulk Load → RJHealth_Drug_Data    │
│  Volume: ~200,000-235,000 records per month                 │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  EXCLUSION FILTER                                            │
│  Remove: Records with RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01                 │
│  Reason: Historical data quality issues + not needed        │
│  Impact: ~0 records excluded (modern data only)             │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  STAGING: #WorkingData (Temp Table)                         │
│  - Data type conversions                                     │
│  - Field trimming & cleaning                                 │
│  - Initial validation flags                                  │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  VALIDATION PHASE                                            │
│  1. Field Validations (Reject 01-06)                        │
│  2. Duplicate Detection (Reject 11)                         │
│  3. Overlap Detection (Reject 13/14) - Projected State     │
│  4. Group Rejection (Rule 5)                                │
│  5. Retro Warning Detection (Warning 15)                    │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  PROCESSING PHASE                                            │
│  - Sort by Procedure_Code, NDC, Effective_Date              │
│  - Insert new records                                        │
│  - Update existing records (idempotent)                     │
│  - Closed record protection                                  │
└────────────────────┬────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  TARGET: Procedure_NDC_Xwalk (Production Table)             │
│  - Complete drug and procedure information                  │
│  - Temporal validity (Effective/Ineffective dates)          │
│  - Audit fields (User_ID, File_Date, DLU)                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                     │
                     ↓
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  LOGGING: NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log                             │
│  - Errors and warnings only (production mode)               │
│  - OR all transactions (if uncommented for debugging)       │
│  - ~200 error records per month (0.1% error rate)          │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### **2.2 System Context Diagram**

```
┌──────────────────┐
│   RJ Health      │
│  External Feed   │──┐
└──────────────────┘  │
                      │ Monthly File
                      │ (CSV/Flat File)
                      ↓
              ┌───────────────────┐
              │  SSIS/Bulk Load   │
              │   ETL Process     │
              └─────────┬─────────┘
                        │
                        ↓
              ┌───────────────────────┐
              │ RJHealth_Drug_Data    │
              │  (Staging Table)      │
              └─────────┬─────────────┘
                        │
                        ↓
              ┌───────────────────────────┐
              │ usp_Process_NDC_Xwalk     │
              │   (Main Processor)        │
              └─────┬───────────┬─────────┘
                    │           │
         ┌──────────┘           └──────────┐
         ↓                                  ↓
┌──────────────────────┐    ┌────────────────────────┐
│ Procedure_NDC_Xwalk  │    │ NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log  │
│  (Production Table)  │    │     (Audit Log)        │
└──────────┬───────────┘    └────────────────────────┘
           │
           │ Used By
           ↓
┌────────────────────────────────────┐
│  Downstream Consumers:             │
│  - Claims Adjudication System      │
│  - Billing Engine                  │
│  - Reporting & Analytics           │
└────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## **3. Table Specifications**

### **3.1 Source Table: RJHealth_Drug_Data**

**Purpose:** Staging table for monthly RJ Health data files

**Table Structure:**

| Column Name | Data Type | Nullable | Description | Example |
|-------------|-----------|----------|-------------|---------|
| NDC | VARCHAR(20) | Yes | National Drug Code (11 digits expected) | 00338101948 |
| DRUG_NAME | VARCHAR(255) | Yes | Drug product name | DEXAMETHASONE SODIUM PHOSPHATE |
| REIMB_CODE | VARCHAR(20) | Yes | Reimbursement/Procedure Code (5 chars expected) | J2290 |
| RC_DESC | VARCHAR(MAX) | Yes | Procedure code description | Dexamethasone sodium phosphate, per mg |
| IS_NOC | CHAR(1) | Yes | Not Otherwise Classified indicator ('Y' or NULL) | Y |
| ADD_DATE | DATE | Yes | Effective date for mapping | 2025-01-01 |
| RMV_DATE | DATE | Yes | Ineffective/removal date (NULL = open-ended) | 2025-09-30 |
| BILL_UNITS | FLOAT | Yes | Billing units | 300.000000 |
| BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG | FLOAT | Yes | Billing units per package | 300.000000 |
| PKG_SIZE | FLOAT | Yes | Package size | 30.000000 |
| PKG_QTY | FLOAT | Yes | Package quantity | 10 |
| UOM | VARCHAR(50) | Yes | Unit of measure | ML |
| PKG_UOM | VARCHAR(50) | Yes | Package unit of measure | Vial |
| File_Date | DATE | Yes | Source file date (business key) | 2025-10-01 |
| SqlBulkCopy_TS | DATETIME | No | Load timestamp (auto-populated) | 2025-10-01 16:10:52 |

**Key Characteristics:**
- **Primary Key:** None (staging table)
- **Retention:** Typically keeps 13 months of history
- **Row Count:** ~2.4 million rows (12 months × ~200K per month)
- **Load Method:** SQL Bulk Copy from flat file

**Data Quality Notes:**
- NDC codes should be 11 digits (some may have leading zeros)
- Procedure codes should be 5 alphanumeric characters
- Dates in source may be in MM/DD/YYYY format (converted to DATE)
- Numeric fields may contain non-numeric values (validated during processing)

---

### **3.2 Production Table: Procedure_NDC_Xwalk**

**Purpose:** Master crosswalk table mapping NDC codes to procedure codes with temporal validity

**Table Structure:**

| Column Name | Data Type | Nullable | Constraints | Description | Example |
|-------------|-----------|----------|-------------|-------------|---------|
| **Xwalk_ID** | INT IDENTITY(1,1) | No | PK | Surrogate primary key | 12345 |
| **NDC** | VARCHAR(11) | No | UK | National Drug Code | 00338101948 |
| **Procedure_Code** | VARCHAR(5) | No | UK | Medical procedure billing code | J2290 |
| DRUG_NAME | VARCHAR(255) | Yes | - | Drug product name | DEXAMETHASONE SODIUM |
| RC_DESC | VARCHAR(512) | Yes | - | Procedure description | Dexamethasone sodium phosphate |
| NOC_Indicator | CHAR(1) | Yes | CK | Not Otherwise Classified ('Y' or NULL) | Y |
| **Effective_Date** | DATE | No | UK | Start date of mapping validity | 2025-01-01 |
| Ineffective_Date | DATE | Yes | - | End date of mapping validity (NULL = active) | 2025-09-30 |
| BILL_UNITS | DECIMAL(12,6) | Yes | - | Billing units | 300.000000 |
| BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG | DECIMAL(12,6) | Yes | - | Billing units per package | 300.000000 |
| PKG_SIZE | DECIMAL(12,6) | Yes | - | Package size | 30.000000 |
| PKG_QTY | INT | Yes | - | Package quantity | 10 |
| UOM | VARCHAR(10) | Yes | - | Unit of measure | ML |
| PKG_UOM | VARCHAR(20) | Yes | - | Package unit of measure | Vial |
| User_ID | VARCHAR(100) | Yes | Default | User who last modified record | SYSTEM_USER |
| File_Date | DATE | Yes | - | Source file date that last updated record | 2025-12-01 |
| DLU | DATETIME | No | Default | Date/time last updated | 2025-12-01 17:27:55 |

**Constraints:**

```sql
-- Primary Key
CONSTRAINT PK_Procedure_NDC_Xwalk PRIMARY KEY CLUSTERED (Xwalk_ID)

-- Unique Business Key (prevents duplicate mappings for same effective date)
CONSTRAINT UQ_NDC_Proc_EffDate UNIQUE NONCLUSTERED (NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date)

-- Check Constraint (ensures NOC is only 'Y' or NULL)
CONSTRAINT CK_NOC CHECK (NOC_Indicator IS NULL OR NOC_Indicator = 'Y')

-- Defaults
DEFAULT (SUSER_SNAME()) FOR User_ID
DEFAULT (GETDATE()) FOR DLU
```

**Key Characteristics:**
- **Business Key:** (NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date) - enforces temporal uniqueness
- **Temporal Model:** Effective/Ineffective dates define validity period
- **Row Count:** ~180,000-200,000 active mappings
- **Growth Rate:** ~500-700 new mappings per month
- **Update Rate:** ~180,000 updates per month (idempotent refreshes)

**Data Integrity Rules:**
1. **No overlapping periods:** Same NDC-Procedure combination cannot have overlapping date ranges
2. **Closed records immutable:** Once Ineffective_Date is set, it cannot be changed
3. **NOC validation:** Only 'Y' or NULL allowed
4. **Temporal consistency:** Ineffective_Date must be > Effective_Date (validated before insert)

---

### **3.3 Log Table: NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log**

**Purpose:** Audit trail for processing errors, warnings, and optionally all transactions

**Table Structure:**

| Column Name | Data Type | Nullable | Description | Example Values |
|-------------|-----------|----------|-------------|----------------|
| **Log_ID** | INT IDENTITY(1,1) | No | Unique log entry identifier | 12345 |
| **File_Date** | DATE | No | Source file date being processed | 2025-10-01 |
| NDC | VARCHAR(11) | Yes | NDC code (if applicable) | 00338101948 |
| Procedure_Code | VARCHAR(5) | Yes | Procedure code (if applicable) | J2290 |
| Effective_Date | DATE | Yes | Effective date of record | 2025-01-01 |
| Ineffective_Date | DATE | Yes | Ineffective date of record | 2025-09-30 |
| **Log_Type** | VARCHAR(20) | No | Type of log entry | ERROR, WARNING, INFO |
| Error_Code | VARCHAR(50) | Yes | Standardized error/warning code | Reject 01, Reject 06, Warning 15 |
| Message | NVARCHAR(1000) | Yes | Detailed error/warning message | NDC INVALID FORMAT (length) |
| Action_Taken | VARCHAR(50) | Yes | Action performed | REJECTED, UPDATED, INSERTED |
| **Process_Date** | DATETIME | No | When log entry was created | 2025-10-01 17:10:57 |

**Constraints:**

```sql
-- Primary Key
CONSTRAINT PK_NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log PRIMARY KEY CLUSTERED (Log_ID)

-- Default
DEFAULT (GETDATE()) FOR Process_Date
```

**Logging Strategy:**

**Production Mode (Current - Errors/Warnings Only):**
```
✓ All field validation errors (Reject 01-06)
✓ Duplicate errors (Reject 11)
✓ Overlap errors (Reject 13/14)
✓ Group rejection errors
✓ Retro warnings (Warning 15)
✓ Closed record errors
✗ Successful inserts (commented out)
✗ Successful updates (commented out)

Monthly volume: ~200 log entries
Annual volume: ~2,400 log entries
```

**Debug Mode (Optional - All Transactions):**
```
Uncomment insert statements in stored procedure for full audit trail
Monthly volume: ~200,000+ log entries
Annual volume: ~2.4 million log entries
```

**Log Type Categories:**

| Log_Type | Description | Typical Count/Month |
|----------|-------------|---------------------|
| ERROR | Validation failures, rejections | ~200 |
| WARNING | Data quality concerns but not rejected | ~0-5 |
| INFO | Successful operations (if enabled) | ~200,000 (if uncommented) |

**Error Code Reference:**

| Error_Code | Category | Description | Count/Month |
|------------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Reject 01 | Field Validation | NDC format/missing issues | ~10-30 |
| Reject 02 | Field Validation | Procedure code format issues | ~0-5 |
| Reject 03 | Field Validation | NOC indicator invalid value | ~0-2 |
| Reject 04 | Field Validation | Effective date missing/invalid | ~0-5 |
| Reject 05 | Field Validation | Ineffective date invalid format | ~0-5 |
| Reject 06 | Business Logic | Ineffective < Effective date | ~50-100 |
| Reject 11 | Business Logic | Duplicate records in file | ~0-5 |
| Reject 13/14 | Business Logic | Overlapping date ranges | ~5-20 |
| GROUP_REJECTION | Business Logic | Record rejected due to group rule | ~50-100 |
| CLOSED_RECORD | Business Logic | Attempt to change closed record | ~0 (rare) |
| Warning 15 | Warning | Retro record (ineff < existing eff) | ~0-5 |

---

## **4. Business Rules & Validation Logic**

### **4.1 Field Validation Rules**

#### **Rule: Reject 01 - NDC Validation**

**Purpose:** Ensure NDC codes meet pharmaceutical industry standards

**Validation Checks:**
1. **Not Missing:** NDC must be present and not blank
2. **Length:** Must be exactly 11 characters
3. **Format:** Must contain only numeric digits (0-9)

**Examples:**

| NDC Value | Result | Reason |
|-----------|--------|--------|
| 00338101948 | ✅ PASS | Valid 11-digit NDC |
| NULL | ❌ FAIL | Reject 01: NDC MISSING |
| '' | ❌ FAIL | Reject 01: NDC MISSING |
| 0033810194 | ❌ FAIL | Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (length) - only 10 digits |
| 003381019481 | ❌ FAIL | Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (length) - 12 digits |
| 0033810194A | ❌ FAIL | Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (non-numeric) |
| 00338-1019-48 | ❌ FAIL | Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (non-numeric) - contains hyphens |

**Implementation:**
```sql
WHEN NDC IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) = '' 
    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC MISSING'
WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC))) <> 11 
    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (length)'
WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(NDC)) LIKE '%[^0-9]%' 
    THEN 'Reject 01: NDC INVALID FORMAT (non-numeric)'
```

---

#### **Rule: Reject 02 - Procedure Code Validation**

**Purpose:** Ensure procedure/reimbursement codes meet billing standards

**Validation Checks:**
1. **Not Missing:** Procedure code must be present and not blank
2. **Length:** Must be exactly 5 characters
3. **Format:** Must contain only alphanumeric characters (A-Z, 0-9)

**Examples:**

| Procedure Code | Result | Reason |
|----------------|--------|--------|
| J2290 | ✅ PASS | Valid 5-character code |
| X5724 | ✅ PASS | Valid alphanumeric code |
| NULL | ❌ FAIL | Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE MISSING |
| '' | ❌ FAIL | Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE MISSING |
| J229 | ❌ FAIL | Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (length) - only 4 chars |
| J22900 | ❌ FAIL | Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (length) - 6 chars |
| J229$ | ❌ FAIL | Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (alphanumeric) - special char |
| j2290 | ✅ PASS | Automatically converted to uppercase: J2290 |

**Implementation:**
```sql
WHEN REIMB_CODE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) = '' 
    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE MISSING'
WHEN LEN(LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE))) <> 5 
    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (length)'
WHEN LTRIM(RTRIM(REIMB_CODE)) LIKE '%[^A-Z0-9]%' 
    THEN 'Reject 02: PROCEDURE CODE INVALID FORMAT (alphanumeric)'
```

---

#### **Rule: Reject 03 - NOC Indicator Validation**

**Purpose:** Ensure "Not Otherwise Classified" flag is valid

**Validation Checks:**
1. **Allowed Values:** Only 'Y' (case-insensitive), NULL, or blank
2. **Case Insensitive:** 'y' or 'Y' both accepted (normalized to 'Y')

**Examples:**

| IS_NOC Value | Result | Normalized Value | Reason |
|--------------|--------|------------------|--------|
| NULL | ✅ PASS | NULL | Valid - drug has specific classification |
| '' | ✅ PASS | NULL | Treated as NULL |
| Y | ✅ PASS | 'Y' | Valid - Not Otherwise Classified |
| y | ✅ PASS | 'Y' | Valid - normalized to uppercase |
| N | ❌ FAIL | - | Reject 03: NOC INDICATOR UNKNOWN VALUE |
| X | ❌ FAIL | - | Reject 03: NOC INDICATOR UNKNOWN VALUE |
| YES | ❌ FAIL | - | Reject 03: NOC INDICATOR UNKNOWN VALUE |

**Implementation:**
```sql
-- Normalization (case-insensitive)
CASE 
    WHEN UPPER(LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC))) = 'Y' THEN 'Y' 
    ELSE NULL 
END AS NOC_Indicator

-- Validation
WHEN IS_NOC IS NOT NULL 
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC)) <> '' 
    AND UPPER(LTRIM(RTRIM(IS_NOC))) <> 'Y' 
    THEN 'Reject 03: NOC INDICATOR UNKNOWN VALUE'
```

---

#### **Rule: Reject 04 - Effective Date Validation**

**Purpose:** Ensure mapping has valid start date

**Validation Checks:**
1. **Not Missing:** Effective date must be present
2. **Valid Format:** Must be convertible to DATE datatype (MM/DD/YYYY expected)

**Examples:**

| ADD_DATE Value | Result | Converted Value | Reason |
|----------------|--------|-----------------|--------|
| 01/01/2025 | ✅ PASS | 2025-01-01 | Valid MM/DD/YYYY format |
| 2025-01-01 | ✅ PASS | 2025-01-01 | Valid ISO format |
| NULL | ❌ FAIL | - | Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE MISSING |
| '' | ❌ FAIL | - | Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE MISSING |
| 99/99/9999 | ❌ FAIL | - | Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT |
| 2025-13-01 | ❌ FAIL | - | Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT (invalid month) |
| ABC | ❌ FAIL | - | Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT |

**Implementation:**
```sql
-- Conversion
TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) AS Effective_Date

-- Validation
WHEN ADD_DATE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(ADD_DATE)) = '' 
    THEN 'Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE MISSING'
WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
    THEN 'Reject 04: EFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT'
```

---

#### **Rule: Reject 05 - Ineffective Date Validation**

**Purpose:** Ensure mapping end date (if provided) is valid

**Validation Checks:**
1. **Optional Field:** NULL or blank is acceptable (indicates open-ended mapping)
2. **Valid Format:** If provided, must be convertible to DATE datatype

**Examples:**

| RMV_DATE Value | Result | Converted Value | Reason |
|----------------|--------|-----------------|--------|
| NULL | ✅ PASS | NULL | Valid - open-ended mapping |
| '' | ✅ PASS | NULL | Treated as NULL |
| 09/30/2025 | ✅ PASS | 2025-09-30 | Valid MM/DD/YYYY format |
| 2025-09-30 | ✅ PASS | 2025-09-30 | Valid ISO format |
| 99/99/9999 | ❌ FAIL | - | Reject 05: INEFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT |
| 2025-13-30 | ❌ FAIL | - | Reject 05: INEFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT |
| XYZ | ❌ FAIL | - | Reject 05: INEFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT |

**Implementation:**
```sql
-- Conversion
CASE 
    WHEN RMV_DATE IS NULL OR LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) = '' THEN NULL
    ELSE TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101)
END AS Ineffective_Date

-- Validation
WHEN RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL 
    AND LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) <> '' 
    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NULL 
    THEN 'Reject 05: INEFFECTIVE DATE INVALID FORMAT'
```

---

#### **Rule: Reject 06 - Logical Date Validation**

**Purpose:** Ensure date range is logically consistent

**Validation Checks:**
1. **Temporal Logic:** Ineffective date must be >= Effective date
2. **Only if Both Present:** Only validated when both dates exist

**Examples:**

| Effective Date | Ineffective Date | Result | Reason |
|----------------|------------------|--------|--------|
| 2025-01-01 | 2025-09-30 | ✅ PASS | Ineff > Eff (9-month period) |
| 2025-01-01 | NULL | ✅ PASS | Open-ended (no ineffective date) |
| 2025-01-01 | 2025-01-01 | ✅ PASS | Same day (valid - closes immediately) |
| 2025-01-01 | 2024-12-31 | ❌ FAIL | Reject 06: INEFFECTIVE DATE BEFORE EFFECTIVE DATE |
| 2017-10-01 | 2011-10-15 | ❌ FAIL | Reject 06: INEFFECTIVE DATE BEFORE EFFECTIVE DATE |
| NULL | 2025-09-30 | ❌ FAIL | Would fail Reject 04 first (missing effective) |

**Real-World Example (from production data):**
```
NDC: 00781910985
Procedure Code: X5724
Effective Date: 2017-10-01
Ineffective Date: 2011-10-15
Result: REJECTED - Ineffective date is 6 years BEFORE effective date (data error)
```

**Implementation:**
```sql
WHEN TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NOT NULL 
    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101) IS NOT NULL
    AND TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) < TRY_CONVERT(DATE, ADD_DATE, 101)
    THEN 'Reject 06: INEFFECTIVE DATE BEFORE EFFECTIVE DATE'
```

**Why This Happens:**
- Source system data entry errors
- Incorrectly formatted dates (year in wrong position)
- Manual corrections not properly validated
- Legacy data migration issues

---

### **4.2 Business Logic Validation Rules**

#### **Rule: Reject 11 - Duplicate Detection**

**Purpose:** Prevent duplicate mappings within the same file

**Validation Logic:**
- **Business Key:** (NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date)
- **Scope:** Within current file being processed
- **Action:** Reject ALL occurrences if duplicates found (not just subsequent ones)

**Examples:**

**Scenario 1: Clean File (No Duplicates)**
```
File_Date: 2025-10-01
Record 1: NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-01-01  → ✅ PASS
Record 2: NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-06-01  → ✅ PASS (different eff date)
Record 3: NDC=00338101948, Proc=J3490, Eff=2025-01-01  → ✅ PASS (different proc code)
```

**Scenario 2: Duplicate in File**
```
File_Date: 2025-10-01
Record 1: NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-01-01, Ineff=2025-09-30  → ❌ FAIL
Record 2: NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-01-01, Ineff=2025-09-30  → ❌ FAIL
Both rejected with: "Reject 11: DUPLICATE RECORDS - Key appears 2 times"
```

**Scenario 3: Same NDC-Proc, Different Ineffective Date**
```
File_Date: 2025-10-01
Record 1: NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-01-01, Ineff=2025-09-30  → ❌ FAIL
Record 2: NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-01-01, Ineff=2025-10-31  → ❌ FAIL
Reason: Same business key (NDC, Proc, Eff) even though Ineff differs
```

**Important Notes:**
- Ineffective_Date is NOT part of the uniqueness check
- This prevents conflicting date ranges for the same mapping
- Common cause: File contains both old and updated version of same record

**Implementation:**
```sql
-- Detect duplicates
SELECT 
    NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date,
    COUNT(*) AS Occurrence_Count
FROM #WorkingData
WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
GROUP BY NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1

-- Log all occurrences
INSERT INTO NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (...)
SELECT 
    @FileDate, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
    'ERROR', 'Reject 11',
    'DUPLICATE RECORDS: Key appears ' + CAST(COUNT(*) AS VARCHAR) + ' times',
    'REJECTED'
FROM #WorkingData
WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
GROUP BY NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
```

---

#### **Rule: Reject 13/14 - Overlap Detection (Projected State)**

**Purpose:** Prevent overlapping date ranges for the same NDC-Procedure combination

**Key Innovation: Projected State Logic**
- Traditional overlap check compares new records against **current** production state
- **Problem:** Fails when file contains sequential records (close existing + start new)
- **Solution:** Create **projected** state by applying current file's updates first, then check overlaps

**Overlap Scenarios:**

**Scenario A: New Record Starts During Existing Period**
```
Existing:    [======2024-01-01 to 2025-12-31========]
New:              [======2025-06-01 to 2026-06-30===]
Result: ❌ OVERLAP (new starts before existing ends)
```

**Scenario B: New Record Ends During Existing Period**
```
Existing:         [======2025-06-01 to 2026-06-30===]
New:      [======2024-01-01 to 2025-12-31========]
Result: ❌ OVERLAP (new ends after existing starts)
```

**Scenario C: New Record Encompasses Existing**
```
Existing:     [===2025-03-01 to 2025-09-30===]
New:      [======2024-01-01 to 2026-12-31========]
Result: ❌ OVERLAP (new completely contains existing)
```

**Scenario D: Sequential (No Overlap) - Traditional Check FAILS, Projected State PASSES**
```
Production State:
   Record: NDC=50474075010, Proc=J0717, Eff=2024-09-17, Ineff=NULL (open)

December File Contains:
   Record 1: NDC=50474075010, Proc=J0717, Eff=2024-09-17, Ineff=2025-11-30 (close existing)
   Record 2: NDC=50474075010, Proc=J0717, Eff=2025-12-01, Ineff=NULL (start new)

Traditional Check:
   ❌ Record 2 rejected: "overlaps with existing open-ended record"

Projected State Check:
   Step 1: Apply Record 1 update → Projected state shows Ineff=2025-11-30
   Step 2: Check Record 2 against projected state → No overlap!
   ✅ Both records processed successfully
```

**Projected State Algorithm:**

```
1. Copy current production to #ProjectedProduction temp table
   
2. Apply updates from current file to projected state:
   - If file record updates ineffective date → update projected table
   
3. Check overlaps against PROJECTED state (not current state)
   - Compare new records against projected effective/ineffective dates
   
4. Reject only TRUE overlaps (not sequential changes)
```

**Real-World Example:**

```sql
-- Production Before Processing
NDC: 50474075010, Proc: J0717
   Eff: 2024-09-17, Ineff: NULL (open-ended)

-- December File
Record 1: NDC=50474075010, Proc=J0717, Eff=2024-09-17, Ineff=2025-11-30
Record 2: NDC=50474075010, Proc=J0717, Eff=2025-12-01, Ineff=NULL

-- Projected State After Record 1
NDC: 50474075010, Proc: J0717
   Eff: 2024-09-17, Ineff: 2025-11-30 (closed by Record 1)

-- Overlap Check for Record 2
Existing Range (projected): 2024-09-17 to 2025-11-30
New Range:                  2025-12-01 to NULL
Result: ✅ NO OVERLAP (new starts after existing ends)

-- Production After Processing
NDC: 50474075010, Proc: J0717, Eff=2024-09-17, Ineff=2025-11-30 (UPDATED)
NDC: 50474075010, Proc: J0717, Eff=2025-12-01, Ineff=NULL (INSERTED)
```

**Implementation:**
```sql
-- Create projected state
SELECT NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date
INTO #ProjectedProduction
FROM Procedure_NDC_Xwalk

-- Apply current file's updates to projected state
UPDATE proj
SET proj.Ineffective_Date = w.Ineffective_Date
FROM #ProjectedProduction proj
INNER JOIN #WorkingData w ON
    proj.NDC = w.NDC
    AND proj.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
    AND proj.Effective_Date = w.Effective_Date
WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
    AND w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL

-- Check overlaps against PROJECTED state
SELECT w.*
FROM #WorkingData w
INNER JOIN #ProjectedProduction p ON
    w.NDC = p.NDC
    AND w.Procedure_Code = p.Procedure_Code
    AND w.Effective_Date <> p.Effective_Date  -- Different effective dates
WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
    AND (
        -- New starts during existing
        (w.Effective_Date >= p.Effective_Date 
            AND (p.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Effective_Date <= p.Ineffective_Date))
        OR
        -- New ends during existing
        (w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL 
            AND w.Ineffective_Date >= p.Effective_Date
            AND (p.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date <= p.Ineffective_Date))
        OR
        -- New encompasses existing
        (w.Effective_Date <= p.Effective_Date
            AND (w.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date >= p.Effective_Date))
    )
```

**Why This Matters:**
- Allows year-end cleanup where mappings are closed and new ones started
- Supports data corrections that need to "close and reopen" mappings
- Prevents false positives that would block legitimate sequential changes

---

#### **Rule: Group Rejection (Rule 5)**

**Purpose:** Ensure data quality by rejecting entire NDC-Procedure groups if ANY record fails validation

**Rationale:**
- If one record in a group has data quality issues, entire group is suspect
- Prevents partial/incomplete crosswalk data
- Forces source system to fix all issues before any mapping is accepted

**Logic:**
1. Identify all NDC-Procedure combinations that have ANY validation error
2. Mark ALL records (even valid ones) in those groups as "GROUP_REJECTION"
3. Reject entire group together

**Example Scenario:**

**File Contains:**
```
NDC=00781910985, Proc=X5724, Eff=2008-09-01, Ineff=2011-03-31  → ✅ Passes field validation
NDC=00781910985, Proc=X5724, Eff=2011-06-01, Ineff=2011-08-31  → ✅ Passes field validation
NDC=00781910985, Proc=X5724, Eff=2017-10-01, Ineff=2011-10-15  → ❌ Reject 06 (Ineff < Eff)
```

**Processing:**
```
Step 1: Field Validation
   Record 1: PASS
   Record 2: PASS
   Record 3: FAIL (Reject 06: INEFFECTIVE DATE BEFORE EFFECTIVE DATE)

Step 2: Group Rejection Check
   Group (00781910985, X5724) has error → ALL records in group rejected
   
Final Result:
   Record 1: ❌ REJECTED (GROUP_REJECTION)
   Record 2: ❌ REJECTED (GROUP_REJECTION)
   Record 3: ❌ REJECTED (Reject 06)
```

**Log Entries:**
```
Log 1: NDC=00781910985, Proc=X5724, Eff=2017-10-01
       Error: Reject 06
       Message: INEFFECTIVE DATE BEFORE EFFECTIVE DATE
       
Log 2: NDC=00781910985, Proc=X5724, Eff=2008-09-01
       Error: GROUP_REJECTION
       Message: Rejected due to group rejection rule (Rule 5)
       
Log 3: NDC=00781910985, Proc=X5724, Eff=2011-06-01
       Error: GROUP_REJECTION
       Message: Rejected due to group rejection rule (Rule 5)
```

**Real-World Impact:**

**Before Pre-2014 Exclusion Was Added:**
```
Many old records had Reject 06 errors (bad historical data)
This caused GROUP_REJECTION of newer valid records
Problem: Valid 2025 mappings rejected because of bad 2011 data
```

**After Pre-2014 Exclusion:**
```
Old records with Ineff < 2014 excluded BEFORE validation
Only modern records participate in group rejection logic
Result: Dramatically reduced false rejections
```

**Implementation:**
```sql
-- Step 1: Find groups with any errors
SELECT DISTINCT NDC, Procedure_Code
INTO #ErrorGroups
FROM #WorkingData
WHERE Validation_Error IS NOT NULL

-- Step 2: Mark all records in error groups
UPDATE w
SET Validation_Error = 'GROUP_REJECTION'
FROM #WorkingData w
INNER JOIN #ErrorGroups eg ON 
    w.NDC = eg.NDC 
    AND w.Procedure_Code = eg.Procedure_Code
WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL  -- Only update previously valid records

-- Step 3: Log group rejections
INSERT INTO NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (...)
SELECT 
    @FileDate, NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
    'ERROR', 'GROUP_REJECTION',
    'Rejected due to group rejection rule (Rule 5)',
    'REJECTED'
FROM #WorkingData
WHERE Validation_Error = 'GROUP_REJECTION'
```

**Benefits:**
- ✅ Ensures complete, consistent crosswalk data
- ✅ Forces source system data quality improvements
- ✅ Prevents downstream claims processing errors
- ✅ Clear audit trail of why valid-looking records were rejected

**Trade-offs:**
- ⚠️ Strict rule - one bad record rejects entire group
- ⚠️ Requires source system fix and resubmission of entire group
- ✅ Mitigated by pre-2014 exclusion (reduces false positives)

---

#### **Rule: Warning 15 - Retro Record Detection**

**Purpose:** Flag potentially unusual temporal patterns for review (warning only, not a rejection)

**Definition:** A "retro record" has an ineffective date earlier than existing effective dates for the same NDC-Procedure combination

**When It Happens:**
- Historical data corrections
- Backfill of missing periods
- Gap-filling in crosswalk timeline

**Detection Logic:**
```
For incoming record with Ineffective_Date = X:
   Check if ANY existing record for same NDC-Proc has Effective_Date > X
   AND incoming record does NOT overlap with existing records
```

**Example Scenario:**

**Existing Production:**
```
NDC=12345678901, Proc=J1234
   Record 1: Eff=2020-01-01, Ineff=2020-12-31
   Record 2: Eff=2021-01-01, Ineff=NULL (current, open-ended)
```

**New Record in File:**
```
NDC=12345678901, Proc=J1234, Eff=2015-01-01, Ineff=2015-12-31
```

**Analysis:**
```
Ineffective Date: 2015-12-31
Existing Effective Dates: 2020-01-01, 2021-01-01
Result: Ineffective (2015-12-31) < Earliest Existing Effective (2020-01-01)
Action: ⚠️ WARNING 15 logged, but record is INSERTED (not rejected)
Overlap Check: PASS (2015 range doesn't overlap 2020-2021 ranges)
```

**Warning Message:**
```
"RETRO RECORD: Ineffective date (2015-12-31) earlier than existing effective date (2020-01-01)"
```

**Why It's a Warning (Not Error):**
- Legitimate use case: Filling historical gaps
- Not a data quality issue per se
- Allows business to review and confirm intentional backfill
- Doesn't block processing

**Real-World Use Cases:**

**Use Case 1: Historical Gap Fill**
```
Scenario: Crosswalk missing 2015-2019 data, vendor provides backfill file
Action: Process normally, Warning 15 flags for review
Outcome: Complete historical timeline
```

**Use Case 2: Data Correction**
```
Scenario: Discovered drug had different code in 2018, need to add historical mapping
Action: Insert retro record with Warning 15
Outcome: Corrected historical claims can be reprocessed
```

**Use Case 3: False Positive to Investigate**
```
Scenario: New record has typo in date (2015 instead of 2025)
Action: Warning 15 flags unusual pattern
Outcome: Business reviews and corrects source data
```

**Implementation:**
```sql
INSERT INTO NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (...)
SELECT 
    @FileDate,
    w.NDC, w.Procedure_Code, w.Effective_Date, w.Ineffective_Date,
    'WARNING', 'Warning 15',
    'RETRO RECORD: Ineffective date (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, w.Ineffective_Date, 23) + 
        ') earlier than existing effective date (' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, p.Effective_Date, 23) + ')',
    'WILL_INSERT'
FROM #WorkingData w
INNER JOIN Procedure_NDC_Xwalk p ON
    w.NDC = p.NDC
    AND w.Procedure_Code = p.Procedure_Code
WHERE w.Validation_Error IS NULL
    AND w.Ineffective_Date IS NOT NULL
    AND w.Ineffective_Date < p.Effective_Date
    AND NOT EXISTS (
        -- Ensure no overlap (already checked by Reject 13/14)
        SELECT 1
        FROM Procedure_NDC_Xwalk p2
        WHERE p2.NDC = w.NDC
            AND p2.Procedure_Code = w.Procedure_Code
            AND (
                (w.Effective_Date >= p2.Effective_Date 
                    AND (p2.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Effective_Date <= p2.Ineffective_Date))
                OR
                (w.Ineffective_Date >= p2.Effective_Date
                    AND (p2.Ineffective_Date IS NULL OR w.Ineffective_Date <= p2.Ineffective_Date))
            )
    )
```

**Monitoring:**
- Review Warning 15 logs monthly
- Confirm with business if backfills are expected
- Investigate unexpected retro records
- Typical count: 0-5 per month

---

#### **Rule: Closed Record Protection**

**Purpose:** Prevent modification of ineffective dates once a mapping is closed

**Business Rationale:**
- Once a mapping has an ineffective date, it represents historical truth
- Changing it would invalidate claims already processed
- Maintains data integrity and audit trail

**Logic:**
```
If existing record has Ineffective_Date = X (not NULL)
   AND new record has Ineffective_Date = Y (not NULL)
   AND X ≠ Y
   THEN reject with CLOSED_RECORD error
```

**Example Scenarios:**

**Scenario 1: Attempt to Extend Closed Mapping**
```
Production:
   NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-01-01, Ineff=2025-09-30 (CLOSED)

File Record:
   NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-01-01, Ineff=2025-12-31 (trying to extend)

Result: ❌ CLOSED_RECORD ERROR
Message: "Existing Ineff=2025-09-30, New Ineff=2025-12-31"
```

**Scenario 2: Attempt to Shorten Closed Mapping**
```
Production:
   NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-01-01, Ineff=2025-12-31 (CLOSED)

File Record:
   NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-01-01, Ineff=2025-09-30 (trying to shorten)

Result: ❌ CLOSED_RECORD ERROR
Message: "Existing Ineff=2025-12-31, New Ineff=2025-09-30"
```

**Scenario 3: Allowed - Setting Ineffective Date on Open Record**
```
Production:
   NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-01-01, Ineff=NULL (OPEN)

File Record:
   NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-01-01, Ineff=2025-09-30 (closing)

Result: ✅ ALLOWED - UPDATE executed
Action: Sets ineffective date, closing the mapping
```

**Scenario 4: Allowed - Idempotent Update (Same Ineffective Date)**
```
Production:
   NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-01-01, Ineff=2025-09-30 (CLOSED)

File Record:
   NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-01-01, Ineff=2025-09-30 (same)

Result: ✅ ALLOWED - UPDATE executed (idempotent)
Action: Refreshes DLU and File_Date, no data change
```

**Implementation:**
```sql
-- Check for closed record update attempt
DECLARE @ExistingIneff DATE = NULL

SELECT @ExistingIneff = Ineffective_Date
FROM Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
WHERE NDC = @NDC
    AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode
    AND Effective_Date = @EffDate

IF @ExistingIneff IS NOT NULL 
    AND @IneffDate IS NOT NULL 
    AND @IneffDate <> @ExistingIneff
BEGIN
    -- Log error
    INSERT INTO NDC_Xwalk_Process_Log (...)
    VALUES (
        @FileDate, @NDC, @ProcCode, @EffDate, @IneffDate,
        'ERROR', 'CLOSED_RECORD',
        'ATTEMPT TO UPDATE CLOSED RECORD: Existing Ineff=' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @ExistingIneff, 23) + 
        ', New Ineff=' + CONVERT(VARCHAR, @IneffDate, 23),
        'REJECTED'
    )
    
    -- Skip record
    CONTINUE
END
```

**Why This Is Critical:**
- **Claims Integrity:** Historical claims processed with closed dates must remain valid
- **Audit Compliance:** Cannot retroactively change historical crosswalk data
- **Data Trust:** Downstream systems rely on temporal stability

**Proper Way to Handle Closed Record Changes:**
1. **If mapping was closed in error:**
   - Do NOT attempt to reopen existing record
   - Contact data governance team
   - May require data fix exception process

2. **If mapping needs to continue with different dates:**
   - Keep existing closed record as-is
   - Create NEW record with new effective date
   - Ensures clean timeline with no gaps

**Example:**
```
WRONG Approach (Rejected):
   Update existing: Eff=2025-01-01, Ineff=2025-09-30 → Ineff=2025-12-31

CORRECT Approach:
   Keep existing:   Eff=2025-01-01, Ineff=2025-09-30 (unchanged)
   Add new record:  Eff=2025-10-01, Ineff=2025-12-31 (new period)
```

---

### **4.3 Pre-2014 Exclusion Rule**

**Purpose:** Exclude very old closed records that have historical data quality issues

**Rule:**
```
Exclude records where RMV_DATE IS NOT NULL AND RMV_DATE < '2014-01-01'
```

**Rationale:**

1. **Data Quality Issues:**
   - Pre-2014 records have higher error rates (Reject 06 common)
   - Historical date entry inconsistencies
   - Legacy system data migration errors

2. **Business Need:**
   - Not needed for current claims processing
   - Historical claims already processed with old data
   - Focus on maintaining current crosswalk

3. **Group Rejection Impact:**
   - Old bad records were causing GROUP_REJECTION of new valid records
   - Excluding them prevents false positives

**Examples:**

**Record 1: Pre-2014 Closed Record (EXCLUDED)**
```
NDC: 00781910985
Procedure_Code: X5724
Effective_Date: 2008-10-01
Ineffective_Date: 2011-04-30  ← Before 2014-01-01
Result: ❌ EXCLUDED before processing (not in #WorkingData)
```

**Record 2: Post-2014 Closed Record (INCLUDED)**
```
NDC: 00338101948
Procedure_Code: J2290
Effective_Date: 2025-01-01
Ineffective_Date: 2025-09-30  ← After 2014-01-01
Result: ✅ INCLUDED in processing
```

**Record 3: Open-Ended Record (INCLUDED)**
```
NDC: 54643565002
Procedure_Code: J3490
Effective_Date: 2020-01-01
Ineffective_Date: NULL  ← Not closed, always included
Result: ✅ INCLUDED in processing
```

**Impact Example (Real Production Data):**

**Without Pre-2014 Exclusion:**
```
NDC 00781910985, Proc X5724 Group:
   Record A: Eff=2008-10-01, Ineff=2011-04-30  → Would pass validation
   Record B: Eff=2011-06-01, Ineff=2011-08-31  → Would pass validation
   Record C: Eff=2017-10-01, Ineff=2011-10-15  → Reject 06 (Ineff < Eff)
   
Result: All 3 records GROUP_REJECTED (including valid A & B)
```

**With Pre-2014 Exclusion:**
```
NDC 00781910985, Proc X5724 Group:
   Record A: Eff=2008-10-01, Ineff=2011-04-30  → EXCLUDED (Ineff < 2014)
   Record B: Eff=2011-06-01, Ineff=2011-08-31  → EXCLUDED (Ineff < 2014)
   Record C: Eff=2017-10-01, Ineff=2011-10-15  → EXCLUDED (Ineff < 2014)
   
Result: All excluded before validation, no group rejection needed
```

**Implementation:**
```sql
-- In SELECT from RJHealth_Drug_Data
WHERE File_Date = @FileDate
    AND (
        -- Include if RMV_DATE is NULL (open-ended)
        RMV_DATE IS NULL 
        OR LTRIM(RTRIM(RMV_DATE)) = ''
        -- OR include if RMV_DATE >= 2014-01-01
        OR TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) IS NULL  -- Invalid dates caught later
        OR TRY_CONVERT(DATE, RMV_DATE, 101) >= '2014-01-01'
    )
```

**Monitoring:**
```
PRINT 'Total records in file: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords AS VARCHAR)
PRINT 'Excluded (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01): ' + CAST(@ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR)
PRINT 'Records to process: ' + CAST(@TotalRecords - @ExcludedRecords AS VARCHAR)

Typical Output:
   Total records in file: 232,212
   Excluded (RMV_DATE < 2014-01-01): 0
   Records to process: 232,212
```

**Important Notes:**
- Exclusion happens BEFORE validation (not counted in rejection stats)
- Does NOT delete existing pre-2014 records from production
- Only prevents NEW pre-2014 records from being loaded
- Typically 0 records excluded (modern files don't contain old data)

---

## **5. Load Logic Rules**

### **5.1 Processing Order (Rule 3)**

**Purpose:** Ensure consistent, predictable processing order

**Sort Order:**
```sql
ORDER BY Procedure_Code, NDC, Effective_Date
```

**Why This Order:**

1. **Procedure_Code First:**
   - Groups all mappings for same procedure together
   - Simplifies downstream procedure-based queries
   - Aligns with claims processing workflows

2. **NDC Second:**
   - Within procedure, groups same drug together
   - Maintains temporal sequence for drug history

3. **Effective_Date Last (Rule 4):**
   - Processes oldest mappings first
   - Critical for maintaining timeline integrity
   - Prevents newer records from blocking historical backfills

**Example Processing Sequence:**

```
Input Records (Unsorted):
   5. NDC=11111111111, Proc=J3490, Eff=2020-01-01
   2. NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-06-01
   4. NDC=11111111111, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-01-01
   1. NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-01-01
   3. NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-12-01

Processed In This Order:
   1. NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-01-01  ← Proc J2290, NDC 003..., Oldest
   2. NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-06-01  ← Same Proc/NDC, Next oldest
   3. NDC=00338101948, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-12-01  ← Same Proc/NDC, Newest
   4. NDC=11111111111, Proc=J2290, Eff=2025-01-01  ← Same Proc, Different NDC
   5. NDC=11111111111, Proc=J3490, Eff=2020-01-01  ← Different Proc
```

**Importance of Oldest-First (Rule 4):**

**Scenario: Historical Backfill**
```
Production (Before):
   NDC=12345678901, Proc=J1234, Eff=2020-01-01, Ineff=NULL

File Contains:
   Record A: NDC=12345678901, Proc=J1234, Eff=2015-01-01, Ineff=2015-12-31 (old)
   Record B: NDC=12345678901, Proc=J1234, Eff=2020-01-01, Ineff=2025-12-31 (update)

If Processed Newest-First (WRONG):
   1. Process Record B → Update Eff=2020-01-01 with Ineff=2025-12-31
   2. Process Record A → Insert Eff=2015-01-01
   Result: ✅ Works, but less intuitive

If Processed Oldest-First (CORRECT):
   1. Process Record A → Insert Eff=2015-01-01 (backfill)
   2. Process Record B → Update Eff=2020-01-01 with Ineff=2025-12-31
   Result: ✅ Works, logical timeline order
```

**Implementation:**
```sql
DECLARE process_cursor CURSOR LOCAL FAST_FORWARD FOR
    SELECT 
        NDC, DRUG_NAME, Procedure_Code, RC_DESC, NOC_Indicator, 
        Effective_Date, Ineffective_Date,
        BILL_UNITS, BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG, PKG_SIZE, PKG_QTY,
        UOM, PKG_UOM, File_Date
    FROM #WorkingData
    WHERE Validation_Error IS NULL
    ORDER BY Procedure_Code, NDC, Effective_Date  -- Rules 3 & 4
```

---

### **5.2 Insert vs. Update Logic (Rule 2)**

**Purpose:** Determine whether to create new record or update existing

**Decision Logic:**
```
Business Key: (NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date)

IF record with same business key exists in production THEN
    UPDATE existing record
ELSE
    INSERT new record
END IF
```

**Update Logic:**

**Triggered When:**
- Exact match on (NDC, Procedure_Code, Effective_Date)

**Fields Updated:**
```sql
UPDATE Procedure_NDC_Xwalk
SET 
    DRUG_NAME = @DrugName,              -- Always from source
    RC_DESC = @RCDesc,                  -- Always from source
    NOC_Indicator = @NOC,               -- Always from source
    Ineffective_Date = @IneffDate,      -- Always from source (can close mapping)
    BILL_UNITS = @BillUnits,            -- Always from source
    BILL_UNITS_PER_PKG = @BillUnitsPkg, -- Always from source
    PKG_SIZE = @PkgSize,                -- Always from source
    PKG_QTY = @PkgQty,                  -- Always from source
    UOM = @UOM,                         -- Always from source
    PKG_UOM = @PkgUOM,                  -- Always from source
    User_ID = SYSTEM_USER,              -- Audit: Who updated
    DLU = GETDATE(),                    -- Audit: When updated
    File_Date = @FileDate_Value         -- Audit: Which file updated
WHERE NDC = @NDC
    AND Procedure_Code = @ProcCode
    AND Effective_Date = @EffDate
```

**Important: Idempotent Updates**
- Update executes even if values are identical
